Summary: Non-Direct Feedthrough Flag is not honored for bus type input ports in S-Functions generated with S-Function Builder Block?

Summary: Longitudinal Vehicle block errors when the road slope port?is hidden
Description: Simulating a model that contains a Longitudinal Vehicle block generates the error message "Number of variables exceeds number of equations " when the?Show road slope inport ?parameter is cleared.
Workaround: To maintain a constant zero slope, select?Show road slope inport, and leave port PG?or port NG?disconnected.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Reusable subsystems with Interpolation using Prelookup blocks might generate inadequate code to protect interpolation
Description: Reusable subsystems with Interpolation using Prelookup blocks might generate inadequate code to protect interpolation when all of the following conditions are true:? A model has multiple identical atomic subsystems set to reuse code if possible. The subsystems have Interpolation using Prelookup blocks whose extrapolation methods are set to Clip .  In the first instance of the subsystem, the Interpolation using Prelookup block takes input from external Prelookup block(s) whose extrapolation methods are set to Clip.  In at least one other instance of the subsystem, the Interpolation using Prelookup block takes input from a block other than a?Prelookup block. These non-Prelookup blocks feed out-of-range index or fraction values to the Interpolation using Prelookup block.  Execution of the generated code might produce incorrect results for the latter because the code will not have protection against the out-of-range index or fraction values.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 9, R2023b Update 7, R2024a

Summary: Model block with initialize, reinitialize, reset, or terminate port produces incorrect simulation results in rapid accelerator mode
Description: Simulation results might be incorrect if all the following conditions occur:? The model is set to simulate in rapid accelerator mode. The model contains a Model block with?Show model initialize port  ,?Show model reinitialize ports,? Show model reset ports  , or Show model terminate port   option selected. The Model block has Schedule rates   option deselected. The top model uses variable step solver or has the configuration parameter Treat each discrete rate as a separate task  deselected. Alternatively,?get_param(model,'SolverMode') ?returns?'SingleTasking' . The referenced model contains only one discrete periodic sample time. The signal driving the initialize, reinitialize, reset, or terminate port of the Model block has different discrete periodic sample time than the discrete periodic sample time set for the referenced model.  You might observe that the Model block executes on the sample hits that are common in both the signal driving the initialize, reset, or terminate port, and the referenced model, instead of executing solely on the sample hits set for the referenced model.

Summary: Angular velocity computation in Spacecraft Dynamics block is incorrect when inertia tensor has nonzero off-diagonal terms
Description: Angular velocity computation in Spacecraft Dynamics block is incorrect when the inertia tensor has nonzero off-diagonal terms. For example, this inertia tensor is affected: [100 10 20;10 200 30;20 30 150]  This inertia tensor is not affected: [100 0 0;0 200 0;0 0 150]
Workaround: Do not use an inertia tensor with nonzero off-diagonal terms. Instead, use a similarity transformation to make the inertia tensor diagonal.A similarity transformation changes the new body axes to be the eigenvectors and the diagonal terms of the inertia tensor to be the eigenvalues. Calculate the eigenvectors and the diagonal matrix with the eigenvalues using the eig function:[eigenVector,diagonalInertiaTensor]=eig(inertiaTensor);
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 6, R2024a

Summary: XLS files containing invalid UTF-8 characters read with xlsread in basic mode can cause MATLAB to crash
Description: Certain XLS files containing invalid UTF-8 characters read with xlsread  in basic  mode can cause MATLAB to crash. For example, in the following code, crash.xls ?causes a MATLAB crash because it contains invalid UTF-8 characters and is read with xlsread  in basic  mode. >> [num, txt] = xlsread("crash.xls", "", "", "basic") The resulting crash dump includes the following phrase. mxCreateString called with non-UTF-8 input xlsread  uses basic  mode by default on platforms that do not have a Microsoft Excel COM server implementation.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 6, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Subsystem code might be incorrect when the subsystem contains a masked Model block
Description: The generated code for a model that meets all these conditions might be incorrect: The model contains at least two identical instances of a subsystem that contains a Model block. The Model block references a model that defines a model argument. The referenced model is either a function-call model or a model with a virtual bus at a root input or output port. Either the referenced model defines a system mask, or the Model block is directly masked. The model argument?values passed into the two different instances of the Model block are different.  The code generator might produce one function for all instances of the subsystem, using a hard-coded value for the model argument.

Description: Simulink PLC Coder generated code is missing call sites of Stateflow absolute time temporal logic functions after  and before? when the => syntax                for function block outputs is configured. Since R2023a  the configuration option PLC_UseCallOutputArgSyntax ?can be found under "PLC Code Generation\  Interface\Use output argument syntax in function?and function block calls ". In R2022a  and R2022b  this option is available as fUseCallOutputArgSyntax  plugin option in the custom target IDE workflow.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 6, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Unassigned fields of a nested structure conditionally defined via a function call
Description: The generated code might produce incorrect results if your MATLAB code satisfies all  these conditions: On one execution path, your MATLAB code creates structure s  containing structure x , such that structure  x  has more than one field  On different execution path, ?your MATLAB code sets s equal to the return value of function? foo  Function? foo ?returns a nested structure that has fewer fields than x   For example, consider the following MATLAB code: function y = test_struct(exp)if exp    s = struct('x', struct('f1',5,'f2',15));else    s = foo();endy = s.x.f2;endfunction out = fooout.x.f1 = 3;end In MATLAB execution, test_struct(false)  produces an error, while test_struct_mex(false) ?returns an undefined value. This issue affects MATLAB Coder and MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink.?
Workaround: If your code is affected by this bug, adding a temporary variable that saves the call result should prevent the bug from happening. In the example above, this amounts to changing    s = foo();to?    tempVar = foo(); s = tempVar;
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 6, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect array indexing when passing a multi-dimensional structured type input to a Model block that references a Simulink model

The input is configured to preserve dimensions in the generated code. The elements from the input are extracted by using blocks such as the Selector block and then passed as an input to the Model block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Fixed-point division in Simulink with non-power of 2 scaling might not saturate on overflow
Description: Fixed-point division with non-power of 2 scaling might not saturate on overflow when all of these conditions are met:? The division is set to saturate on overflow. The division has rounding mode set to "Simplest" or "Zero". The input types are signed and the output type is unsigned. At least one of the input types or the output type has a slope that is not a power of 2.  Negative values will wrap on overflow instead of saturating to 0. Simulation and code generation will both wrap the results.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Destination state is not executed properly when entered from an Entry Port connected to a supertransition
Description: If the destination of a supertransition is an Entry port on a state, Stateflow does not correctly execute the state or its children. State actions might not run, and outgoing transitions might not be taken.?
Workaround: Replace the supertransition with multiple transition segments connected by Entry Ports and Junctions at each level of the state hierarchy.

For example, if a parameter uses the?Define ?storage class to generate a?macro?such as?#define myParam 5 ?and that parameter is connected as an input for an atomic subsystem, then the code generator might return an error similar to the following:   Internal error while creating code interface description file: codeInfo.mat. Summary: Error while running MEX functions generated for MATLAB functions creating matrices of?less than intmax() elements
Description: You might get an error while running a MEX function generated from a MATLAB function which creates a matrix that meet these conditions:You create the matrix using a function whose arguments represent the size dimensions. For example, the function NaN . Any argument corresponding to the second size or later is a variable or a function of a variable. For example, NaN(x, y) , when using the?X = nan(sz1,...,szN ) form of syntax.? The argument is not guaranteed to be a positive integer.?  You might receive the following error even if the matrix being created by the MATLAB function does not have more than?intmax() ?elements : ? ? Cannot grow a matrix with greater than?intmax()?elements.
Workaround: To create the matrix with the MATLAB function, instead of using arguments that represent size dimensions, use a vector input whose elements represent the size dimension of the output.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2024a

Summary: From Spreadsheet block parameter Extrapolation before first data point has no effect when Range parameter includes range with only header row
Description: The Range  parameter of the From Spreadsheet block can include multiple, comma-separated ranges. If one of these ranges is only the first row (e.g. A1:C1,A5:C20 ), the block ignores the?Extrapolation before first data point? parameter. The data before the range is output according to the value of the parameter Data interpolation within time range .
Workaround: Modify the Range parameter to no longer include the header row. For example, if the Range was A1:C1, A5:C20, change the range to A5:C20.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 3, R2024a

Summary: Code generation fails with 'unhandled AUTOSAR memory category' assertion for AUTOSAR classic models
Description: During code generation for a classic AUTOSAR model, Simulink might throw an error similar to the following: Code generation assertion 'unhandled AUTOSAR memory category' failed in 'rtwcg_utils/rtwcg_interface_context.cpp'.  This code generation error might occur for a classic AUTOSAR model that meets the following conditions: Configuration parameter?Code interface packaging ?is set to?Reusable function . The model contains a Merge block with its output connected to a Unit Delay block. The inputs of the Merge block are from conditionally executed subsystems. For example, an Enabled Subsystem or If Action Subsystem.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Loop streaming optimization may generate incorrect code for loops that write to a persistent variable
Description: Incorrect code might be generated for MATLAB code with loops when loop streaming optimizations are enabled. For example, in the code shown below, generated code would contain an extra cycle updating the state p1.function y = fcn(u1, u2) persistent p1if isempty(p1)    p1 = int32(0);endfor count = 2:100    temp = p1;    p1 = int32(3) + int32(temp)*int32(-2);endy = p1;end
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model simulation and generated code might produce different results for reinitialized triggered subsystem
Description: Triggered subsystems might produce different results for model simulation and code generation when all the following conditions are true: Show subsystem reinitialize ports  parameter is enabled for the Triggered Subsystem block. For the Trigger block inside the subsystem, the?Trigger type ?parameter is set to?rising  and the Initial trigger signal state  parameter is set to?zero . The trigger signal does not trigger the execution of the subsystem and the Reinitialize Function block inside the subsystem is activated by a reinitialize function-call event.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b

Summary: The problem-based workflow might error when using a Global Optimization Toolbox solver with UseParallel=true
Description: When using the problem-based workflow and selecting one of the Global Optimization solvers ga , surrogateopt , or patternsearch , with UseParallel=true , the solve  function might error. For example: x = optimvar('x',2, 'LowerBound', -5, 'UpperBound', 10);prob = optimproblem; prob.Objective = 4; prob.Constraints.con1 = x(2) <= 1;prob.Constraints.con2 =  1 <= x(1) + x(2);gaOptions = optimoptions('ga', 'UseParallel', true);x0.x = [-2 3];[xga, fga, efga, outga] = solve(prob, x0, 'solver', 'ga', 'options', gaOptions); The returned error can appear as follows:? Error using fcnvectorizerFunction to evaluate must be represented as a string scalar, character vector, or function_handle object.Error in makeState (line 69)        Score = fcnvectorizer(state.Population(initScoreProvided+2:end,:),FitnessFcn,1,...Error in galincon (line 24)state = makeState(GenomeLength,FitnessFcn,Iterate,output.problemtype,options);Error in ga (line 420)            [x,fval,exitFlag,output,population,scores] = galincon(FitnessFcn,nvars, ...Error in optim.problemdef.OptimizationProblem/callSolverError in optim.internal.problemdef.ProblemImpl/solveImplError in optim.problemdef.OptimizationProblem/solveCaused by:    Failure in user-supplied fitness function evaluation. GA cannot continue.
Workaround: As a workaround, you can use the prob2struct function to generate the function files for the objective and constraint in the current folder, and then call the solver directly on the output structure.For example:probStruct = prob2struct(prob, x0, 'solver', 'ga');probStruct.options = gaOptions;[xga, fga, efga, outga] = ga(probStruct);
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code reuse subsystem inside Initialize Function block generates incorrect initialization code
Description: A CodeReuseSubsystem block generates incorrect initialization code when all these conditions apply: The CodeReuseSubsystem block is placed inside an Initialize Function block.? The Initialize Function block is placed inside an Atomic Subsystem block. The Atomic Subsystem block is placed inside a Function-Call Subsystem block. The Function-Call Subsystem block is in an export-function model. The Function-Call Subsystem block executes at union rates, where one sample time unites two or more event sample times.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b, R2024a

Summary: Comments in custom .cgt files might not correctly appear in shared files

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: When pulse generator sample time values are mismatched, these values are ignored in certain subsystems with periodic rates
Description: In fixed-step  models, pulse generator blocks compute their sample time from a combination of the "Period" and "Pulse Width" parameters. This computed sample time must match the sample time of its parent subsystem. However, Simulink is unable to detect this mismatch. When this mismatch occurs and is not detected, incorrect behavior will result. The incorrect behavior may look like:? Blocks which ignore the sample time specified in parent atomic subsystems. Generating incorrect code for blocks in periodic function call subsystems.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b

The correct statement is: xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.asam.net/schema/CDF/r2.1 cdf_v2.1.0.sl.xsd"

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code might be incorrect when a custom TLC storage class interacts with inlined S-function
Description: The generated code might be incorrect if asignal or state of a block downstream of inlined S-Function is? assignedto?a?storage classusing a custom TLC file , whereby?the contents  data access is requested where the set  access isexpected.? For example, get_data() ?might be generated on theleft-hand side of an assignment resulting in code such as get_data() =tmp;? instead of set_data(tmp); . The generated code might not compile depending on how the data access is implemented. A summary of the conditions are:? The model configuration parameter Enable local block outputs ?is selected Use of a storage class using a custom TLC file Use of an inlined S-Function with output set as local and reusable The storage class is not directly assigned to the S-Function output and is assigned downstream consisting of only data copy operations (that might be optimized)
Workaround: Option 1:Directly specify the storage class on the output of the inlined S-Function.Option 2:?Configure the output of the inlined S-Fuction as a test point.?Option 3:Force the output of the inlined S-Function to be global and non-reusable, usingssSetOutputPortOptimOpts(S, PortIndex,?SS_NOT_REUSABLE_AND_LOCAL)Option 4:?Clear the model configuration parameter Enable local block outputs.?
Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 5, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Calls to enumeration?function with string input
Description: For MATLAB code containing calls to the enumeration  function with a string input, the generated code returns an empty matrix instead of the contents of the enumeration. This bug does not affect calls to the enumeration  function? using character vector input.  For example, consider this MATLAB code: classdef LightColors < uint8    enumeration        red(1)        yellow(2)        green(3)    endendfunction out = getColors()out = enumeration("LightColors");end? In MATLAB execution, calls to getColors  return the contents of the enumeration. If you generate a MEX for getColors , calls to getColors_mex  incorrectly return an empty matrix.? ?This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Workaround: In your MATLAB code, replace string input to the?enumeration?function with character vector input.For example, in the code in the bug description, replace double-quoted input to the?enumeration?function ("LightColors") with single-quoted input ('LightColors').?
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 4, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Array indexing might be incorrect for inplace MATLAB functions that accept variable-size arrays as inputs
Description: Incorrect code might generate from a MATLAB function when all of the following conditions are true: The MATLAB function is an inplace function (that is, uses the same input and output variable) whose input is a variable-size array. Inside the MATLAB function, an inplace operation changes the size of the input/output variable. The code in the MATLAB function performs some inplace operations on the input/output variable. You generate code that uses the row-major array layout. You generate code that uses N-dimensional indexing by setting the?PreserveArrayDimensions ?property to  true .  In R2021b or later,  EnableImplicitExpansion  is turned off.  Consider this MATLAB function that uses the same input and output variable x  and accepts a variable-size array as an input. The size of x ?is changed inside of the function through an inplace operation. function x = foo(x)? ? ? ? % In-place function with variable-sized input/output x = x(1,4:1:5);? ? ? ? ? ? % The size of x is changed  x = x + (x + ones(1,2));? ?% Inplace operation  end   When you configure the code generator to use row-major array layout and N-dimensional indexing,?you may see these code lines in the generated code: x_data[0] += x_data[0] + 1.0;  x_data[1] = x_data[0] + (x_data[0] + 1.0);   The generated code calculates the value assigned to? x_data[1] ?using?x_data[0] ?incorrectly.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b

Constant: '<S2>/Const_dummy_Byte_8'   */   ?myParam = simplify1_ConstP.Raw_Bytes_Y0.Raw_Byte_8;?    }? Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: When using a storage class with?Data Access set to Function and Allowed Access set to Write-only, the generated write method ignores the identifier naming rule
Description: The code generator generates code that does not honor the identifier naming rule when all ?of the following are true: You use a storage class that has Data Access ?set to Function  That storage class also has Allowed Access ?set to Write-only   For example, consider a model that as an import testa , a Gain block that has a value of 2, and an outport  testb . Summary: MATLAB fails to authenticate SSH connections using RSA keys to remote machines running OpenSSH server 8.8 onwards
Description: When you authenticate an SSH connection to a remote machine with an identity file in the RSA format, MATLAB Parallel Server fails to establish the connection and produces an error like the following: Identity file not accepted by remote host?'machine32.mycompany.com'. Check the identity file is valid and has correct permissions.  Error from library: Username/PublicKey combination invalid  This error occurs when authenticating an SSH connection to a machine running OpenSSH server version 8.8 or later. The RSA private key might work when you authenticate using OpenSSH client on the command line. A private key is in RSA format if it begins with the following line: -----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY -----
Workaround: Do one of the following:Use a private key in a format other than RSA, such as Ed25519.Use a password to authenticate.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: reshape function with dlarray input
Description: Code generated for the?reshape ?function that is passed a?dlarray ?input might return incorrect answer if?all ?these conditions are true: The size argument of reshape  is a row vector. The number of elements of this row vector is equal to the number of dimensions of the input.? Summary: Truth table transitions are flagged by checks "mathworks.jmaab.jc_0752" and "mathworks.jmaab.jc_0774" and cannot be fixed
Description: Check usage of parentheses in Stateflow transitions  (Check ID:? mathworks.jmaab.jc_0752)? flags Stateflow truth table transitions even though you can not add a new line before the condition and action statement. Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Automatic parallelization of for loops containing saturating reduction operations on integer types
Description: This issue affects the generated code if all  these conditions are true:?? Your MATLAB code contains a for  loop that contains reduction operations on integer data? The configuration parameters Enable automatic parallelization? (EnableAutoParallelization ) and Optimize reductions  ( OptimizeReductions ) are enabled  The configuration parameter? Saturate on integer overflow ?(SaturateOnIntegerOverflow )?is enabled  In such situations, the code generator produces parallel?for  loops that perform arithmetic reduction operations on variables of saturating integer type. However, saturating integer reduction operations are not associative because the final result of several such operations depend on the order in which the threads execute. This might cause the concurrent code to produce numerically incorrect results when compared with the results produced by the sequential code.
Workaround: Do any one of the following:?Disable the configuration parameter?Optimize reductions?(OptimizeReductions). However, this action disables parallelization of for loops that contain any type of reduction operations - arithmetic, logical, min, max.If you can identify the loop that might cause this issue, you can disable parallelization of that specific loop by adding the coder.loop.parallelize('never') pragma immediately before that loop in your MATLAB code. For example,coder.loop.parallelize('never');for ii = 1:numel(arr1)    var = var + arr1(ii);end

Summary: Entropy weight optimizer of rlSACAgent is reset when the agent is saved during training
Description: The entropy weight used in rlSACAgent  controls the balance between exploitation and exploration. It is automatically adjusted if LearnRate  of EntropyWeightOptions  is positive. The agent uses an optimizer to update the entropy weight. However, this optimizer is incorrectly reset every time the agent is saved during training (the agent is saved when SaveAgentCriteria  of rlTrainingOptionsis  is different than none ). Specifically, when the agent is saved, a new entropy weight optimizer is created using the?Algorithm ?and OptimizerParameters ?properties in?EntropyWeightOptions , hence any internal information in the optimizer from the previous episode is discarded. As a consequence, the training results will differ between a training session in which the agent is not saved and one in which the agent is saved.
Workaround: You can use one of the following workarounds to avoid the issue:Do not save the agent during training. Set SaveAgentCriteria and SaveAgentValue in rlTrainingOptions to none.Use a fixed entropy weight. Set LearnRate of EntropyWeightOptions of rlSACAgentOptions to 0, and adjust EntropyWeight (default value is 1) in EntropyWeightOptions of rlSACAgentOptions. Try 0.5, 0.2, or 0.1 for?EntropyWeight,?and increase this value if the agent needs more exploration, or reduce this value if more agent exploitation is desired.Use rlTD3Agent instead of rlSACAgent. TD3 agents are generally competitive with SAC agents.

Summary: Entropy weight of rlSACAgent is reset when the agent is saved during training
Description: The entropy weight controls the balance between exploitation and exploration. It is automatically adjusted if LearnRate  of EntropyWeightOptions  is positive.  However, the entropy weight of rlSACAgent  is incorrectly reset every time the agent is saved during training. The agent is saved when SaveAgentCriteria  of rlTrainingOptionsis  is different than none . When the agent is saved, the entropy weight is reset to the EntropyWeight  value in EntropyWeightOptions.  This incorrect behavior can lead to poor training results depending on the value of EntropyWeight  in EntropyWeightOptions . The training results will differ between a training session in which the agent is not saved and one in which the agent is saved.
Workaround: To avoid the issue, use one of the following workarounds:Do not save the agent during training. Set?SaveAgentCriteria?and?SaveAgentValue?in?rlTrainingOptions?to?none.Set EntropyWeight (default value is 1) in EntropyWeightOptions of rlSACAgentOptions to a smaller value such as 0.5, 0.2, 0.1 or 0.01, so that the entropy weight is reset to these values, and it does not encourage too much exploration.Use a fixed entropy weight. Set LearnRate of EntropyWeightOptions of rlSACAgentOptions to 0, and adjust EntropyWeight (default value is 1) in EntropyWeightOptions of rlSACAgentOptions. Try 0.5, 0.2, or 0.1 for?EntropyWeight,?and increase this value if the agent needs to explore more, or reduce the value if the agent needs to explore less.Use rlTD3Agent instead of rlSACAgent. TD3 agents are generally competitive with SAC agents.

Summary: Generating PDF report containing nonprinting control characters in document heading or caption crashes MATLAB
Description: If you generate a PDF report that contains nonprinting control characters in a document heading, the following error occurs and MATLAB crashes. Unsupported control characters include characters with ASCII codes between 1 and 31, except horizontal tab (ASCII code 9), line feed (ASCII code 10), and carriage return (ASCII code 13). Error using mlreportgen.internal.fop.FOPObject/executeCannot convert "C:/workspace/root.fo" to "C:/workspace/test.pdf". Reason: Invalid Unicode character: U+001B? ? If the nonprinting character appears in a table of contents or list of captioned report elements, such as figure captions or table titles, MATLAB crashes.? For example, the following code adds the escape character to a document. MATLAB crashes when the document is closed. d = mlreportgen.dom.Document("output");append(d,mlreportgen.dom.TOC);append(d,mlreportgen.dom.Heading1(char(27))); close(d);
Workaround: Remove unsupported characters from the input string before adding the string to a DOM component or document. For example,inputChar = ['line feed: ',char(10),'escape character: ',char(27)];supportedChars = [9, 10, 13]; % Horizontal tab, line feed, and carriage return are supportedtoRemove = ~ismember(inputChar,supportedChars) & (inputChar < 32);inputChar(toRemove) = []

Summary: C++ compilation error due to assignment operations to this pointer
Description: MATLAB Coder might generate assignment operations to this  pointer when generating C++ code from MATLAB code that satisfies all  of these conditions: The MATLAB code contains a handle class. The handle class has a non-static class method. The class method contains an arguments block  . The first argument declaration in the?arguments?block  ?includes a specification of the size of the argument.  For example, consider this MATLAB code: classdef MyClass < handle  ??? ...   ??? methods   ??? ...   ??????? function Foo(obj)  ? ????????? arguments   ? ? ? ????????? obj (1, 1) {mustBeA(obj, "MyClass")}  ? ? ? ????? end   ? ????????? ...   ??????? end   ??? end   end   MATLAB Coder generates this C++ code for the above MATLAB code:   void MyClass::Foo()  {  ??? this = handle_init();  ??? ...  }   In the generated code,? the first argument of the non-static class method in the MATLAB code is represented as?this  pointer, ?and an assignment operation happens to the pointer.?  Assignment to this  pointer ?is not allowed in C++. MSVC gives the following error message: error C2106: '=': left operand must be l-value  GCC gives the following error message: error: lvalue required as left operand of assignment   Clang gives the following error message:  error: expression is not assignable
Workaround: Remove the size specification from argument declarations in the arguments block.? In the MATLAB code example given above, replacing the code line obj (1, 1) {mustBeA(obj, "MyClass")}?with?obj {mustBeA(obj, "MyClass")} resolves the error.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 5, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect results might occur for MATLAB code containing loops with the same number of iterations that might conditionally write to the same scalar value
Description: Incorrect code might generate from the MATLAB code that meets all  of these conditions: The code contains for -loops or while  loops or a combination of loops that have the same number of iterations.? The loops write to the same scalar variable. One of the writes is conditional, where the output of the condition varies across loop iterations.?  This happens because of an optimization that incorrectly fuses loops of the above MATLAB coding pattern. Consider this example MATLAB: ? ? ?function y = example(a,b,y)  ? ? ? ? for i = 1:7  ? ? ? ? ? ? y(1).f2 = b(6:1:6,4:1:4);? ? ? ? ? ? ??  ? ? ? ? end  ? ? ? ? for j = 1:7  ? ? ? ? ? ? if (a((j) + (6),1)) >= (double(0))  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? y(1).f2(1:1:1,1:1:1) = abs(int16(0));  ? ? ? ? ? ? end  ? ? ? ? end  ? ? end  The for- loops in the MATLAB code have the same number of iterations and both write to y(1).f2 . T he second loop conditionally writes to it based on the if -condition. The generated C code looks like this: ? ?void example(const short a[13], const short b[81], struct0_T y[2])  ? ?{  ? ? short b_i;  ? ? for (int i{0}; i < 7; i++) {  ? ? ? b_i = b[32];  ? ? ? if (a[i + 6] >= 0) {  ? ? ? ? b_i = 0;  ? ? ?} } ? ? y[0].f2 = b_i;  ? ? y[0].f2 = b_i; } The generate code incorrectly fused the MATLAB for -loops which produces incorrect results.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect backfolding of variable assignment into function calls
Description: In a MATLAB function, when a global variable is declared and used in only one MATLAB function and the functions that it calls, it might be converted to a local variable and passed into the called function as an input. Code generation might incorrectly fold back the assignment that copies the value of the variable into the call site of the called function. This incorrect backfolding results in a change of evaluation order and incorrect results. For example, this MATLAB function calls the function callee : function out = caller(in1, in2)?  ? ? global P2. ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?// (1) Declaration of global variable P2  ? ? [P1] = callee(in1,in2);? ? ? ?// (2) Callsite of function callee , which directly access global P2  ? ? P1(1,1) = P2;? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?// (3) Copy of P2  end?  ?In the generated code, the variable P2  is converted to a local variable and passed into callee  as an input in statement (2). The copy of P2  in statement (3) is folded back to the callsite (2) and statement (3) is eliminated. The logic in the the generated code is: ? ? [P1] = callee(in1, in2, P(1, 1))  ?This optimization incorrectly changes the evaluation order of P(1, 1)  .
Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 1, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Size of variable-size struct field incorrectly modified prior to generated code that reads and writes the same data
Description: MATLAB Coder might generate incorrect code when all ?of these are true: An assignment statement completely writes a variable-size struct field The right-hand side of the assignment statement contains an access of a single element of the same variable-size struct field using a subscript  MATLAB Coder might incorrectly generate code for the statement. The generated code modifies the size of the variable-size struct field prior to the code that reads the variable-size data. This causes incorrect behavior when the read of the variable-size struct field also uses the modified size to compute a subscript. The generated code might read uninitialized memory or incorrect numeric results. The attached example ReproductionModel.zip  demonstrates this issue. This code snippet from the example shows the coding pattern described above: s.f1 = s.f1(2,2) - temp;   See this corresponding generated code: i = s->f1->size[0] * s->f1->size[1];  s->f1->size[0] = temp->size[0];  s->f1->size[1] = 1;  emxEnsureCapacity_real_T(sp, s->f1, i, &d_emlrtRTEI);  b_s = s->f1->data[s->f1->size[0] + 1];  for (i = 0; i <= loop_ub; i++) {  ? s->f1->data[i] = b_s - temp_data[i];  }   The expression? s->f1->data[s->f1->size[0] + 1] ?in the generated code?corresponds to the MATLAB expression? s.f1(2,2) . Note that the size of the struct field  s->f1  has been incorrectly modified prior to this expression. The generated code should update the size of  s->f1 ?after that expression.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 2, R2023b

Summary: Variants are applied in incorrect order in Group Simulation or Fit Data programs
Description: The SimBiology Model Analyzer  app sometimes applies variants in the incorrect order when you run the Group Simulation  or Fit Data  programs. The incorrect order might be used when you run the program after making a change to the variants, such as unchecking the box to use a baseline variant. The order of variants might also differ when running the program versus viewing the program code. If you have fewer than ten variant columns in the variant and dose setup table, then variants are in the correct order when you view the program code even if they are not applied in the correct order when running the program. If you have ten or more variant columns, then the variants will never be in the correct order when you view the program code. Note that the order of variants matters only if multiple variants modify the same quantity. In such cases, the last value is taken from the last variant to modify the component.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, update the variants to remove any overlapping content. Then, the results do not depend on the order in which variants are applied.If it is not practical to remove overlapping content, you can ensure correct results when running?a program by saving, reloading, and then rerunning a program after making any changes to the configuration of variants in that program. Reloading the project avoids the issue by updating the variant information stored in the SimBiology Model Analyzer app. However, if you have more than ten variant columns in the variant and dose setup table, variants are still applied in the incorrect order when viewing the program code.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: datetime time zones do not reflect recent changes to the IANA time zone database, including time zones in Mexico
Description: MATLAB uses the IANA time zones database for up-to-date time zone information. MATLAB is missing recent updates to this database. As of the R2022b general release, MATLAB includes database version 2022a. However, in R2022b MATLAB does not include the 2022f version of the IANA database, which also captures time zone updates from database versions 2022b, 2022c, 2022d, and 2022e. Most notably, these updates include changes to a number of time zones which no longer observe daylight saving time (DST). For example, in October 2022 the government of Mexico announced that the country no longer observes DST except near the US border.?The time zone data in MATLAB does not reflect this recent change. IANA Time Zone Database:?https://www.iana.org/time-zones  In versions of MATLAB that do not have these changes, datetime  arrays in what used to be the DST period will have incorrect UTC offsets. Specifically, they will be one hour ahead of the actual times in those time zones.
Workaround: If possible, update to a release with an IANA time zone database version of 2022f or later.To check the IANA time zone database version in MATLAB, use the second output of the timezones function. When the database is up to date, the second output is '2022f'.>> [~,vers] = timezones vers =    '2022f'https://www.mathworks.com/help/matlab/ref/timezones.htmlIf you are unable to update to a newer release, you can obtain the correct time zone information for datetime arrays on or after October 30th 2022 (where the desired time zone is in Mexico) by using?the corresponding fixed time zone offset. For example, replace the time zone 'America/Chihuahua'?with the time zone 'UTC-6' on or after?October 30th, 2022.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Workaround: To avoid or resolve this issue, use the full perturbation solver Jacobian method in the top model.In the Simulink Toolstrip, on the Modeling?tab, click Model Settings.Select the Solver?pane.?Expand Solver details.Set the Solver Jacobian method?to Full perturbation.For some models, the full perturbation solver Jacobian method might not be an appropriate choice or might slow simulation. If using the full perturbation solver Jacobian method does not work for your model, you can disable algebraic loop minimization for the referenced models instead.To disable the Minimize algebraic loop occurrences?parameter for a referenced model:Open the referenced model as a top model.In the Simulink Toolstrip, on the Modeling?tab, click Model Settings.?Select the Model Referencing?tab.Under Options for referencing this model?clear Minimize algebraic loop occurrences.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: Microsoft Excel date values stored in the 1904 date system as doubles might be incorrectly imported by readcell

Summary: Passing string literals to external C functions in a C Function block might crash MATLAB

Summary: Modbus read and write functions error or return incorrect values

Summary: Unrecognized method, property, or field?error during SIL/PIL simulation
Description: In a model that logs a signal, if the logged signal is a non-virtual bus and its implementation resolves to a storage class, then running a software-in-the-loop (SIL) or processor-in-the-loop (PIL) simulation?can produce this error:? Workaround: Identify the logged signal that is causing the issue.Connect the signal to a Signal Copy block.Set the Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization?block parameter to on.Log the output of the Signal Copy block, which forces the creation of a variable in the generated code.

Summary: App help text is removed when loading an app that has code containing the less than sign surrounded with whitespace
Description: When loading an app that contains any code or comments with the less than sign surrounded with whitespace (e.g. " < "), other than with the class definition on line 1, any app help text previously specified for the app will be removed from the generated code in App Designer. If edits to the app are then saved, the former app help text will be permanently lost.
Workaround: The issue can be prevented from occurring by removing any whitespace surrounding any use of the less than sign: "<".If the app already contains code or comments with the less than sign and surrounding whitespace, first get a copy of the current help text by executing >> help MyApp.mlapp where MyApp.mlapp is the name of the app. Second, load the app, remove any whitespace surrounding all uses of the less than sign, and re-add the app help text copied from the first step.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 3, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Changing initial value of a chart output and recompiling can result in wrong answers

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code for AUTOSAR Adaptive model might lead to data race condition

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code that uses deep learning libraries might produce incorrect answers if 1x1?convolution2dLayer?post-dominates?maxPooling2dLayer
Description: Generated code that uses MKL-DNN, ARM Compute or cuDNN library might produce incorrect answers if your deep learning network satisfies all  these conditions: The network contains a  maxPooling2dLayer ?followed by a  convolution2dLayer .  All paths in the layer graph that start at the  maxPooling2dLayer ?go through the  convolution2dLayer ?(that is, the? convolution2dLayer ?post-dominates ?the?maxPooling2dLayer ). The  convolution2dLayer ?has  FilterSize  equal to  [1 1], ?and ?has equal strides that are greater than  1 , and ?has no spatial padding .  The  maxPooling2dLayer ?has  FilterSize  greater than  1 , or ?has strides greater than  1 , or  has spatial padding.  The only layers between the? maxPooling2dLayer ?and  convolution2dLayer ?are  reluLayer, additionLayer, clippedReluLayer, leakyReluLayer.   This is an example network layer graph that might produce incorrect answers in code generation:? ?lg = layerGraph([imageInputLayer([5 5 3], Normalization = 'none', Name = 'input')?  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? maxPooling2dLayer([2 3], Name = 'maxpool')?  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? reluLayer(Name = 'relu')?  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? convolution2dLayer(1,3,Stride = [2 2], Name = 'conv')]);?  This issue affects code generated by using any  of these products: MATLAB Coder  Simulink Coder  GPU Coder when using  TargetLibrary='cudnn'?
Workaround: For code generation targeting Intel, ARM, or AMD based CPUs, use?TargetLibrary='none'.For code generation targeting NVIDIA GPUs, use TargetLibrary='tensorrt'.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: Unexpected license checkout when using "Other uses of functionName" links in help text
Description: After calling help on a function that is overloaded in several classes, clicking on the "Other functions named functionName  " link at the bottom of the help text can cause licenses to be checked out.
Workaround: Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps. On Mac systems, the folder name will have a .app extension, e.g. /Applications/MATLAB_R20XXa.app  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Windows Systems: You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Mac Systems: If you are using Safari on a Mac, disable automatic unzipping of the downloaded file by unchecking Open Safe Files After Downloading in Preferences > General.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. To see the contents of the .zip file:          Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.      Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):        unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.         Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder.          Windows Systems:                  Open the .zip file.          Extract the contents of the file into your <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.                    Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):         cd <MATLAB>        unzip <.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.        Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Constant-folding expressions that accept nonvirtual buses as inputs might generate incorrect results
Description: The code generator might generate incorrect results when all  of the following are true: ?You generate code for a model that contains a MATLAB Function block. The code in the MATLAB Function block has an expression that accepts a nonvirtual bus containing a matrix as an input. The code uses the matrix in the nonvirtual bus input to initialize a matrix variable. A value is assigned to an element of the initialized?matrix variable. The code uses the coder.const  directive to constant-fold the expression.   Constant-folding the expression in the generated code might produce incorrect results. For example, consider the function  loca l. The function is called by passing the input  u, ? which is a nonvirtual bus that contains a matrix of complex numbers in the field f . function y = fcn(u)  ? ? u.f = repmat(0+2i,2,1);  ? ? t=coder.const(local(u));  ? ? y=t.f;  end  function ovar = local(ivar)  ? ? ovar.f = ivar.f;  ? ? temp = ivar.f;  ? ? temp(1) = 5+5i;  end  The function local ? initializes the variable temp  using the input and assigns the value 5+5i  to a matrix element. The generated code constant-folds the expression local(u) ,?which ?produces the incorrect result?[0+2i,5+5i], ?where t he correct result is [0+2i,0+2i] .

Summary: Some UIs appear blank when accessing via Remote Desktop
Description: When using tools like Remote Desktop or TeamViewer to remotely access a Windows machine, some UIs can appear blank. Affected UIs include, but are not limited to: App Designer AddOns Explorer AddOns Manager Simulink Test Manager Simulink Data Inspector? Any UI produced with UIFigure or App Designer  The UIs run, and the window appears with the correct tile, but the contents of the window remain blank.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, run the following commands as the first commands in a MATLAB session:wm = matlab.internal.webwindowmanager.instance;wm.setStartupOptions('ExternalProcess', '--ignore-gpu-blacklist --disable-gpu-compositing');?You can add these commands to a?user-defined startup script?to ensure that they run early in the MATLAB startup process.

Summary: Unrecognized method, property, or field error during Model block SIL/PIL simulation
Description: Consider a Model block with a referenced model that logs a signal. If the logged signal implementation resolves to a custom storage class, then running a simulation with block parameter Simulation mode  set to ?Software-in-the-loop (SIL)  or Processor-in-the-loop (PIL) ?can produce this error:? Unrecognized method, property, or field 'BaseRegion' for class 'coder.descriptor.BasicAccessFunctionExpression'?  ?
Workaround: Identify the logged signal that is causing the issue.Configure the logged signal as a test point. For more information, see?Configure Signals as Test Points.

Summary: Unable to view custom UI component in App Designer Component Library if the description for the component contains newlines
Description: When configuring a custom UI component to be usable from App Designer, if the description field contains newlines, the configuration will successfully complete but the component will not appear in the App Designer Component Library.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Roadrunner Scenario Reader block might output values from the current time step instead of the previous time step
Description: The Roadrunner Scenario Reader block should output actor or vehicle poses from the previous time step. However, if a Simulink model has both Roadrunner Scenario Reader and RoadRunner Scenario Writer blocks, the Roadrunner Scenario Reader block might incorrectly output pose values written in the current time step by the?RoadRunner Scenario Writer block.? This situation might occur if the Roadrunner Scenario Writer block, through its message lines or signal input lines, cannot be traced back to the RoadRunner Scenario Reader block.

Summary: spectrumAnalyzer release method if called after hide does not unlock the object
Description: If the spectrumAnalyzer object is provided with data, then hide method was called followed by release, the scope will remain locked preventing any changes to non-tunable properties.
Workaround: Before calling release method on spectrumAnalyzer object, if hide was called, call show method on the scope then call release.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect behavior when periodic function-call subsystem uses invalid periodic sample time
Description: Updating a model produces an incorrect error when all of these conditions are true: A periodic function-call subsystem has a Trigger block with Sample time type  set to periodic . In this periodic function-call subsystem, a block explicitly specifies a different periodic sample time. This periodic function-call subsystem is one of multiple subsystems in the block diagram. Summary: Clock-rate pipelining can result in unnecessarily high register usage and latency
Description: Starting in R2017b, clock-rate pipelining was improved to work better through subsystem hierarchy in a model. However, there are certain instances where high register usage and latency reported from delay balancing can occur.
Workaround: Enable?FlattenHierarchy?on your design to get a more optimal result from clock-rate pipelining.

Summary: Simulation results might be incorrect for export-function model with State Writer block that writes to Outport block
Description: Simulating an export-function model produces incorrect results when all these conditions apply: The?Data Import/Export > Format ?configuration parameter is set to?Dataset . The model contains two root Inport blocks that output function-call signals. Each function-call signal connects to a function-call port of a function-call subsystem. The first function-call subsystem contains a State Writer block. The State Writer block writes to an Outport block in the second function-call subsystem. The corresponding output port directly drives a root Outport block.  The output of the root Outport block is logged each time either function-call subsystem executes. When the first function-call subsystem executes, the output of the root Outport block is logged incorrectly. When the second function-call subsystem executes, the output of the root Outport block is logged correctly.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 2, R2023a

Summary: SimBiology projects lose data and become corrupted when apps are left open overnight
Description: If you leave a SimBiology project open overnight in the SimBiology Model Builder  app or the SimBiology Model Analyzer  app, the project might lose data and become corrupted. This data loss can lead to errors when saving or loading the project or when trying to load data or results from the project. This corruption happens because the operating system sometimes deletes temporary files needed by the SimBiology project.
Workaround: You can prevent the operating system from deleting SimBiology temporary files by changing the MATLAB temporary folder. Set the appropriate environment variable to a different location, such as your documents folder. On Windows, set the environment variable TMP. On macOS or Linux, set the environment variable TMPDIR.

Summary: Exploration noise in DDPG, TD3, and epsilon in DQN do not decay in parallel training
Description: The exploration noise in DDPG and TD3, and epsilon in DQN, do not decay during parallel training. This happens because the EnableNoiseDecay  property (for DDPG and TD3), and the EnableEpsilonDecay  property (for DQN) in the exploration policy is not handled correctly. Due to this problem, the DQN agent does not typically learn a good policy in parallel training when the default value of epsilon is used (because this default value is too large). Similarly, TD3 and DDPG agents fail to learn a good policy if the initial standard deviation is large.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, use one of the followoing workarounds:  To prevent parallel training for DQN/DDPG/TD3 agents, set UseParallel in rlTrainingOptions to false. Use small values for standard deviation or epsilon:  DQN: Set Epsilon in EpsilonGreedyExploration of rlDQNAgentOptions to a relatively small value.  DDPG: Set StandardDeviation in NoiseOptions of rlDDPGAgentOptions to a relatively small values if  the value of StandardDeviationDecayRate is greater than zero.  TD3: Set StandardDeviation in ExplorationModel of rlTD3AgentOptions to a relatively small values if the value of StandardDeviationDecayRate is greater than zero.
