Description: Include Simulink Design Verifier Checks option on the Metrics dashboard is always dimmed.

Summary: Toolstrip in the Simscape Variable Viewer is empty
Description: In a Simscape?model window, on the?Debug? tab, when you click?Simscape? >?Variable Viewer , the Variable Viewer window opens but?the buttons in its toolstrip are not visible, which makes the tool unusable.
Workaround: To work around the issue:Start a new MATLAB session. Before opening any Simscape models, issue the command simscape.state.internal.mdom_viewer(false).?For any Simscape models that you open in this session, the Variable Viewer works as expected.

Summary: Model Advisor check?Check for usage of text inside states? (Check ID:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0739)?gives incorrect warnings
Description: Check for usage of text inside states? (Check?ID : mathworks.jmaab.jc_0739 ) gives warnings when the State label  does not exceed its boundary. These warnings are incorrect as the check?verifies the objects inside state such as transitions, junctions, substates and all the text labels that do not exceed state boundary.

Summary: MATLAB slows down when using echotcpip or UDP functions
Description: Due to a memory leak, MATLAB might slow down and become unresponsive when using echotcpip  or UDP functions. The issue is more prominent when you send and receive large quantities of data (e.g., data sizes of 1 GB).  This issue can occur when using the following functions and possibly other functions: echotcpip  echoudp  udpport("byte")
Workaround: Restart MATLAB to free up the leaked memory. However, the issue might reappear if you use the affected functionality again.
Products: Instrument Control Toolbox, Industrial Communication Toolbox
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 5, R2022b

Summary: The insertText, insertObjectAnnotation, and listTrueTypeFonts?functions cause a?MATLAB??crash for non-UFT-8 fonts
Description: The  insertText ,?insertObjectAnnotation, ?and?listTrueTypeFonts ?functions cause a?MATLAB?crash when any of the fonts that are installed on the computer uses a character encoding format other than UTF-8 encoding. The stack trace generated by MATLAB includes these lines: [  0] 0x00007ffba92d9cb3 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022a\bin\win64\libmwfl.dll+00761011 foundation::core::diag::symbolic+00000275 [  1] 0x00007ffba92d8758 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022a\bin\win64\libmwfl.dll+00755544 foundation::core::diag::stacktrace_base::capture+00000024 [  2] 0x00007ffba922828c C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022a\bin\win64\libmwfl.dll+00033420 foundation::core::diag::symbols::getSymbolAddress+00015580 [  3] 0x00007ffba92268a1 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022a\bin\win64\libmwfl.dll+00026785 foundation::core::diag::symbols::getSymbolAddress+00008945 [  4] 0x00007ffba922bfdb C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022a\bin\win64\libmwfl.dll+00049115 foundation::core::diag::terminate+00000059 [  5] 0x00007ffba298f193                            bin\win64\pgo\libmx.dll+00258451 matrix::WarningObserver::getNumWarnings+00047283 [  6] 0x00007ffba2957330                            bin\win64\pgo\libmx.dll+00029488 matrix::detail::noninlined::mx_array_api::mxCreateString+00000044 [  7] 0x00007ffb51942c94 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022a\toolbox\vision\visionutilities\visioninit\private\fontpicker.mexw64+00011412 mexFunction+00000580

Summary: MATLAB might crash when simulating large model with fast restart enabled
Description: Simulating a large model with fast restart enabled might crash MATLAB during the initialization phase of the simulation.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect simulation and code generation result for Truth Table block that directly connects to a conditionally executed subsystem Outport block
Description: The initial value of the Truth Table block output is overwritten by the Outport block when all these conditions apply: The model contains a conditionally executed subsystem, such as an enabled or triggered subsystem. The subsystem contains a Truth Table block. An output of the Truth Table block directly connects to an Outport block of the subsystem. The parameter Initial value ?of the Truth Table block output port has a different value than the parameter Initial output ?of the Outport block.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block between the Truth Table block and the Outport block.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 2, R2023a

Summary: Model Advisor Exclusions error out when saving exclusions on models with Referenced Configset.
Description: For models using referenced configsets, saving exclusions in Model Advisor Exclusion Editor errors out.

Summary: siderealTime?function returns incorrect results for non-scalar Julian date inputs
Description: The?siderealTime  function returns incorrect?results when more than one Julian date is provided as an input.
Workaround: Use siderealTime with only scalar inputs.? To calculate sidereal times for more than one julian date, call siderealTime in a for loop.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when generating code from models containing nested buses and signals resolved to Simulink.Signal objects that are initialized with parameter structures
Description: MATLAB might crash when generating code from a model that meets these conditions:The model contains a nested bus whose cumulative compile size is greater than 2560 bytes. The model also contains a root-level signal that resolves to a Simulink.Signal  object initialized with a parameter structure.  When this issue occurs the crash contains the following stack trace message: [snip]  Fault Count: 1  Abnormal termination:  std::terminate()  [snip]?  [ ?9] 0x00007ff9cd90aece CG::Const::cloneValue+00000030 (C :\..\win64\dist\bin\win64\cg_ir.dll) [ 10] 0x00007ff9d0088e2c CG::LoweringBaseImpl::lowerConst+00000140? (C :\..\win64\dist\bin\win64\cg_ir.dll) [ 11] 0x00007ff9d0089ac1 CG::LoweringBaseImpl::lowerSymbol+00000385 ?(C :\..\win64\dist\bin\win64\cg_ir.dll) [snip]?
Workaround: Use an Initial Condition block to initialize the Simulink.Signal object.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: Full-duplex audio can cause MATLAB to crash
Description: Full-duplex audio can crash MATLAB if audioDeviceReader  is set up before audioDeviceWriter , or when an overrun or underrun occurs, in particular with devices that have more than four channels. This bug affects audioPlayerRecorder , or the simultaneous use of audioDeviceWriter  and audioDeviceReader  with the same device (with Driver  set to 'ASIO'  if using Windows).
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: Variant Subsystem with default variant choice containing Simulink function-caller blocks might cause parse error during build
Description: Variant Subsystem  with default variant choice containing Simulink function-caller blocks might cause parse error during build with ERT based targets when all the below conditions are met:Variant Subsystem  block is configured to generate pre-processor conditionals through 'code compile ' activation? Variant Subsystem  block uses default variant choice The default variant choice has a Simulink function-caller block but no corresponding Simulink function block  You may get a similar build error:? Operation not supported. Parsing of expression failed.  'A~= 1 && A~= 3'

Summary: gimbalAngles method output does not match steering angles input to pointAt method
Description: The?gimbalAngles ?method outputs an equivalent but not identical set of angle outputs compared to the steering angles provided as input to the?pointAt ?method.

Summary: Missing?GOMP_4.5 on Linux when running Polyspace Bug Finder or Polyspace Code Prover
Description: On some operating systems such as Ubuntu 16.04, an analysis with Code Prover or Bug Finder can stop with a message like this: /.../R2022a/bin/glnxa64/ps_middle_end: /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libgomp.so.1: version `GOMP_4.5' not found (required by /.../R2022a/bin/glnxa64/libmwvvir_dl_impact.so) The reason for the message is that some Polyspace libraries have a dependency on the runtime library libgomp , but libgomp  is not installed in the system.
Workaround: If the external library libgomp.so.1 is not present in the system, a possible workaround is to install the library manually. For instance, in Ubuntu 16.04, you can install the package gcc-6, which comes with the library.

Summary: MATLAB might crash or produce an error when generating code for a model containing Simulink function or Graphical function in Stateflow chart
Description: MATLAB might crash or produce an error when generating code for a model containing a Stateflow chart under the following circumstances: The Stateflow chart has a Simulink function exported from Stateflow. The Stateflow chart contains a graphical function.  When generating code, you may get this code generation error: ValidCore violated: Failed assertion: fcnType->numInputs() == nInputs || (fcnType->isVariadic() && fcnType->numInputs()".   MATLAB might crash. The stack trace generated by MATLAB includes these lines:[ 0] 0x00007ff8bd15fd64 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022b\bin\win64\rtwcg.dll+06356324 RTWCG::RTWContext::getModule+00000020  [ 1] 0x00007ff8bcc5924a C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022b\bin\win64\rtwcg.dll+01086026 CG::GenericTagCategory<CG::RTWCGMatlabFcnInplaceTag,0>::GenericTagCategory<CG::RTWCGMatlabFcnInplaceTag,0>+00000714
Workaround: For Simulink function, set the Function Packaging parameter to Inline and for Graphical function, set the Function Inline option to?Inline.?
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 3, R2023b

Summary: Custom attributes are omitted when importing requirements with custom URIs from IBM DOORS Next?
Description: When you import requirements from IBM DOORS Next, requirements' artifact attributes are imported as custom attributes. However, artifact attributes that satisfy one of these criteria are omitted during import:The attribute has a custom URI. The attribute is based on an attribute data type that has a custom URI.? The attribute is based on an enumeration based attribute data type that has an enumeration value that has a custom URI.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 5, R2023a, R2023b

Summary: Simscape diagnostic mistakenly identifies PS-Simulink Converter block when model has unequal number of variables and equations
Description: When a Simscape model has an unequal number of variables and equations, a compilation error is issued, saying: "Number of variables exceeds number of equations" or "Number of equations exceeds number of variables." The list of blocks identified by the error message sometimes mistakenly includes a PS-Simulink Converter block, for example: ?Error compiling Simscape network for model model.?Caused by:  Number of variables exceeds number of equations. Click on any Simscape blocks identified below for more detailed diagnostics.?  model/PS-Simulink Converter   model/Block     Clicking the link for the PS-Simulink Converter block returns either: " Error: Character vector is not terminated properly. " or? " Update the Simulink block diagram and try again. Simscape file with package path 'simscape.builtin.components.output' is not on MATLAB path and not in import context declared by import statements. "?   PS-Simulink Converter blocks should never appear in the list of Simscape blocks with more detailed diagnostics. The top-level error is still correct and the model does have unequal number of equations and variables, so inspect other blocks in the diagnostic list instead.
Workaround: Ignore any PS-Simulink Converter blocks identified in the diagnostic list when a Simscape model has an unequal number of variables and equations.

Summary: Simulink.Parameter with expression value has a different behavior than Simulink.Parameter with numeric value in mask initialization
Description: When you use aSimulink.Parameter  object as the value of a mask parameter, if the value of the parameter object is an expression, the behavior of the initialization code is different than if the value of the parameter object is a numeric value. When the value of the parameter object is an expression the parameter is not restored as a Simulink.Parameter  object after exiting the initialization code.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: MATLAB Job Scheduler workers might get stuck in the 'busy' state after rerunning a communicating job
Description: Workers of a MATLAB Job Scheduler cluster might get stuck in the 'busy ' state when jobs created with createCommunicatingJob() or batch(...,'Pool',N)    fail and are automatically rerun. Consequently, the workers are unable to run jobs and tasks again.  The likelihood of a worker getting stuck increases with the number of workers that the job uses.
Workaround: Restart workers stuck in the 'busy' state with the stopworker and startworker scripts.To prevent workers from becoming stuck, disable the automatic rerun for communicating jobs. Use one of the following:For jobs created with createCommunicatingJob(), set the MaximumRetries property on the first task of the communicating job to 0 before submitting the job using the following syntax.? ? >> task = createTask(...)            ? ? >> task.MaximumRetries = 0Set the MaximumRetries property of your cluster profile to 0 in the Cluster Profile Manager.  This option works for all communicating jobs created with the cluster profile, including jobs created with batch(...,'Pool',N), but also disables the automatic rerun for independent jobs.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 5, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated HDL code for Discrete FIR Filter with partly serial structure fails to compile in HDL simulator
Description: This issue affects the Discrete FIR Filter block when you set the Filter structure  parameter to Partly serial systolic .  When you compile the generated HDL code in MentorGraphics??ModelSim?, you may see this error message: # -- Compiling module SimpleDualPortRAM_generic???# ** Error: (vlog-13069) SimpleDualPortRAM_generic.  v(54): near "if": syntax???error,   unexpected if,   expecting '('.  ???# ** Error: (vlog-13069) SimpleDualPortRAM_generic.  v(57): near "<=": syntax error, unexpected <=.?

Summary: Incomplete export of bus objects from Bus Editor to MAT file (.mat) or MATLAB function (.m)
Description: In the Bus Editor toolstrip, clicking the?Export ?button arrow and selecting an export option does not export all the bus objects from the base workspace to a MAT file (.mat ) or MATLAB function (.m ) when all of these conditions apply: The list of bus objects in the Contents  pane exceeds the default pane height in the Bus Editor. The Contents  pane has not been scrolled to the end. No filters have been specified.
Workaround: Scroll to the end of the Contents pane before exporting from the Bus Editor.

Summary: Referencing a software architecture model from another software architecture model generates an error
Description: When a software architecture model references another software architecture model that has no function associated with it, Simulink displays the following error: Placing nonvirtual block 'SoftwareArch/myNonvirtualBlock' of type 'ModelReference' at the root level of export-function model 'SoftwareArch' is not supported. This message is related to a hidden ModelReference block.

Summary: Sample time behavior for Model block that references sample-time-independent model and has reset ports might be unpredictable
Description: Simulation behavior might be unpredictable for a Model block when all these conditions apply: The referenced model contains a Reset Function block that directly connects to a root Inport or Outport block. The referenced model also has a root Inport or Outport block that connects to a block other than the Reset Function block, and that block has an inherited sample time (-1 ). The referenced model is sample-time independent. The Model block has the parameter Show model reset ports ?enabled. The Model block port that corresponds to the root Inport or Outport block that directly connects to the Reset Function block connects to a signal with a different sample time than the remaining Model block input and output ports.  The part of the referenced model that is sample-time independent might inherit either of the sample times for normal and accelerator simulation mode, depending on which sample time is propagated to the Model block first. For the rapid accelerator mode, the sample-time independent part of the model will execute whenever there is a sample hit for either of the sample times.
Workaround: Connect all input and output ports of the Model block to signals with the same sample time.

Summary: MATLAB may crash during code generation with branched bus signals and option?VariantConditionMismatch set to error/warning
Description: MATLAB might crash when the conditions below are TRUE:  The model contains branched bus signals. The?VariantConditionMismatch  (Diagnostics  -> Modelling issues related to variants  -> variant condition mismatch at signal source and destination ) option is set to either?error/warning    Stack snippet:? Stack Trace (from fault):[??0] 0x00007fff478e1099 GetPortCGVCEId at B:\ matlab\ src\ sl_compile\ variants\ VCEUtils. cpp:240 (in E:\ jobarchive\ BR2021bd\ 2022_06_16_h10m49s14_job1983861_pass\ matlab\ bin\ win64\ sl_compile. dll)
Workaround: Change the VariantConditionMismatch option?to the default setting:Set (Diagnostics?->?Modelling issues related to variants?->?variant condition mismatch at signal source and destination) option to none.Command line: set_param(gcs,'VariantConditionMismatch', 'none')

Summary: MATLAB crashes when using VISA-related functionality on Intel-based macOS Monterey 12.x
Description: MATLAB might crash on Intel-based macOS Monterey 12.x if the Instrument Control Toolbox Support Package for National Instruments VISA and ICP Interfaces is installed. Impacted MATLAB releases install NI-VISA 21.0 with this support package. The crash can occur when using any of the following VISA-related functions, apps, or Simulink blocks: visa  visadev  visadevlist  instrhwinfo  Test and Measurement Tool Query Instrument?block To Instrument?block  For example, the following line of code crashes MATLAB: instrhwinfo("visa","ni")
Workaround: To work around this issue, install the NI-VISA 2022 Q4 driver on your Intel-based macOS Monterey 12.x machine.Download and install NI-VISA 2022 Q4 by following the instructions on the NI-VISA Download website.

Summary: Invalid block diagram name when tracing coverage results to model in the Test Manager
Description: If you select a subsystem for coverage analysis in the Configuration Parameters for a model, this error might occur when you trace coverage results to the model in the Test Manager: Invalid block diagram name supplied. Valid block diagram names are the same as valid MATLAB variable names. Type "help isvarname" for more information.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Nonvirtual bus element at Outport block accessed by State Writer block might cause incorrect SIL mode simulation results
Description: SIL mode simulation produces incorrect results when all these conditions apply: A State Writer block?sets the state of an Outport block in a conditionally executed subsystem. The State Writer block is in an Initialize Function block. The Outport block receives a signal from a Mux block. The Mux block receives a signal selected from a nonvirtual bus.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b

Summary: Lookup Table Editor might hang when plotting unsupported data
Description: The Lookup Table editor might appear to hang if you input invalid data and then try to view it on the Plot  tab. For example, a contour plot cannot show data with a constant Z  value.?

Summary: Manual changes to a tunable block parameter value might be ignored when the value is written by a Parameter Writer block
Description: During simulation, manually changing a tunable block parameter value does not change the parameter value when a Parameter Writer block writes to the parameter value. For example, a new value specified by the Block Parameters dialog box or?set_param ?function?is ignored during simulation.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Reusable custom storage class with variant sink block may generate incorrect code
Description: Using reusable custom storage classes with variant sink blocks may generate incorrect code when all below conditions are met. Variant sink block is configured to generate code via code-compile  or startup  activation Reusable custom storage classes are used in the model and we have those signals propagating via variant sink block Variant sink block connected to an atomic subsystem port which has the same custom storage class being used and atomic subsystem may be unconditional due to variants.  We may generate code something like below: ? ? ? ? #if V== 1 || V ==2?? ? ? ? ? ? out_sig = 10;?  ? ? ? ? #endif?? ? ? ? #if V != 2? ??? ? ? ? ? ? out_sig = 0;?? ? ? ? #endif? ? ? ?  ? ? ? ?Here out_sig  is reusable custom storage class, whose value is overwritten V =1 .? ?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Variant?Bus Element Out without signal names may generate incorrect code when output is driven from a subsystem
Description: A Simulink model containing conditional?Bus Element Out ? may generate incorrect code when the below conditions are true: The variant condition on the Bus Element Out  is propagated through a Variant Source  or Variant Sink . The Bus Element out  does not have a signal name assigned. The model contains Bus Creator  and Bus Selector  blocks for routing the conditional bus signals. The output is driven from an atomic subsystem or a virtual subsystem with Treat as grouped when propagating variant conditions  parameter is turned on  In the generated code, you can notice the output (e.g.:?rtb_VM_Conditional_Signal_Out1_BusCreator_0 ) is incorrectly reset to zero.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 3, R2023a

Summary: 4-Way 3-Position Valve (IL) block pre-parameterization option might cause unexpected behavior
Description: The 4-Way 3-Position Valve (IL) block pre-parameterization option Bosch 5-.WE J19  lacks the minimum information to pre-parameterize the block. The option can cause unexpected behavior of the block.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 3, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: The model parameter Allow clock-rate pipelining of DUT output ports can result in inconsistent I/O between the original model and HDL code
Description: When using the model parameter?ClockRatePipelineOutputPorts , the ordering of the outputs in the generated model and HDL code may not be the same as what was modeled originally. If these outputs have differing amounts of data rate latency, the validation model can show a mismatch.  Additionally, if input signals inside the DUT are left unconnected or drive unconnected logic that is considered dead and removed by optimizations, the signals are missing from the generated model and HDL code.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b

Summary: Parameter configuration behaves incorrectly when?DVParameterConfiguration conflicts with the older options DVParameters  & DVParametersUseConfig
Description: The new option DVParameterConfiguration  (Parameter configuration ) configures parameters incorrectly in the following scenarios: When you set DVParameterConfiguration? to None , DVParameters  to on , and DVParametersUseConfig  to off , the Design Verifier uses parameter configuration file instead of treating all the parameters as constants. When you set DVParameterConfiguration  to None , DVParameters  to on , and DVParametersUseConfig  to on, Design Verifier uses parameter table instead of treating all the parameters as constants. When you set DVParameterConfiguration  to UseParameterConfigFile , DVParameters  to on , and DVParametersUseConfig  to on , Design Verifier uses?parameter table instead of using parameter configuration file. When you set DVParameters  to off and DVRebuildModelRepresentation ?to IfChangeIsDetected  the translation cache can be reused incorrectly.
Workaround: Use set_param to switch off the deprecated options.?set_param(<modelName>, 'DVParameters' , 'off') ;?set_param(<modelName>, 'DVParametersUseConfig' , 'off') ;?With these options, the analysis will only consider values specified in DVParameterConfiguration? for parameter configuration.To avoid the incorrect reuse of model representation, set DVRebuildModelRepresentation?to Always.set_param(<modelName>, 'DVRebuildModelRepresentation', 'Always');?

Summary: Lookup Table Editor might stop updating cell selection red dots in plots after reloading or?refocusing
Description: The Lookup Table Editor might stop showing cell selection red dots in plots after reloading or refocusing. This behavior might be triggered if you perform one of these actions: Click the Reload  button in the toolstrip. Move the cursor out of Lookup Table Editor, edit the data currently being edited in Lookup Table Editor, then return to Lookup Table Editor.
Workaround: Close and reopen the Lookup Table Editor Plots tab.

Summary: cd errorswhen navigating to an empty folder on an SFTP server
Description: cd  errors when navigating from the current folder to an empty folder on an SFTP server. >> mkdir(S,"EmptyFolder")>> cd(S,"EmptyFolder")Error using matlab.io.sftp.SFTP/cd"EmptyFolder" is nonexistent or not a directory. Note: S ?is an?matlab.io.sftp.SFTP  object.
Workaround: You can work around this issue by creating a dummy folder in the empty folder.>> mkdir(S, "EmptyFolder/DummyFolder")>> cd(S, "EmptyFolder")Note:?S?is an?matlab.io.sftp.SFTP?object.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Missing SIL Initialization of model parameters when they are variant
Description: The generated SIL code fails to initialize model parameters when all the following conditions are met:?The parameter is defined in the model workspace. The model workspace parameter is under the influence of a code-compile variant condition due to a variant block such as a Variant Subsystem  or Variant Source . The model workspace parameter definition is exported by Simulink

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Models containing Variant Source blocks whose input signals have an?ExportedGlobal?storage class?and output signals have a reusable storage class might result in incorrect code
Description: Embedded Coder might generate incorrect code for a model meets the following conditions: The model contains a Variant Source block whose input signal uses an?ExportedGlobal ?storage class. The output signal of the block uses a?Reusable ?storage class. A root inport uses the same?Reusable ?storage class.  In the generated code, the global input buffer of the Variant Source block replaces its output buffer, which is also assigned for root input values. Replacing the output buffer causes the loss of runtime input data.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion?block for the input signal of the Variant Source block,?and select the?Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization?parameter of the Signal Conversion block. With the addition of the Signal Conversion block, an intermediate buffer is introduced, which prevents the global input buffer from replacing its output buffer.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 5, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: Compiler error for FMU files generated by using Standalone FMU Export and compiled with function slrealtime.fmu.compileFMUSources
Description: When compiling FMU files generated by using Standalone FMU Export and compiling with function slrealtime.fmu.compileFMUSources , the compiler issues the error: Error using slrealtime.fmu.compileFMUSources Cannot compile these FMU sources for Simulink Real-Time.

Summary: False enumerated data type mismatch error between C and Simulink
Description: When you simulate or generate code for a model containing a C Caller block or a C Function block, Simulink throws a false enumerated data type mismatch error between C and Simulink enum  types when both of the following conditions are true:  C enum  type has type name as prefix for all enum  names. Simulink enum  type sets addClassNameToEnumnames  to true.  You see an error message similar to the one below:  Value of enumeration elements specified for argument 'u1' for C Caller block?'C Caller1' does not match the argument's type in the custom code.?Check the enumeration definition for 'myEnum' in Configuration Parameters > Simulation Target pane.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b

Summary: buildinfo.mat?error when MdlrefConfigMismatch check?is run with?parallel building of referenced models enabled
Description: When you run the Simulink Coder Model Advisor check,? mathworks.codegen.MdlrefConfigMismatch,  with parallel building of referenced models enabled ( EnableParallelModelReferenceBuilds  set to? 'on' ), the check produces an error indicating that it is unable to find? buildInfo.mat .
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 2, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for?hess might return incorrect results for ill-scaled matrices
Description: Code generated for generalized Hessenberg decomposition [aa,bb,q,z] = hess(a,b)  might return incorrect results if all  these conditions are satisfied: ?You generate a static library, a dynamic library or an executable that accepts real matrix inputs ?You do not instruct code generator to include calls to an external LAPACK library ?During run time, the input matrices a  or b  have elements with large magnitude ( > 1e137 for double precision and > 1e11 for single precision inputs)?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible when initial conditions of a Stateflow chart are reset by the surrounding subsystem
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model containing a subsystem that meets the following conditions: The subsystem contains a Stateflow chart that has initial values for states and data.? The subsystem also contains one of these control port blocks:? Action Port block with the parameter States when execution is resumed ?is set to reset .? Enable Port block with the parameter States when enabling  is set to reset .? Trigger block with the parameter States when enabling ?is set to reset .? Reset block.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 6, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: Simulation results might not match when a Parameter Writer block writes to a block in a function-call subsystem
Description: Simulation results might not match between normal mode and SIL mode when all the following conditions are true: A parameter owner block is in an atomic subsystem that is in a function-call subsystem. A Parameter Writer block outside of the function-call subsystem writes to the parameter owner block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generator does not report an error when a non-inlined S-function accesses a global parameter with storage class
Description: The code generator does not report an error when a model that contains a non-inlined S-function accesses a parameter object in the base workspace or a data dictionary. This results in generated code that might behave incorrectly. This incorrect code generation can occur when any of the following conditions are true:The parameter object has a storage class other than Auto  or Model Default  The parameter object has storage class Auto  or Model default  and the default mapping for External parameters   is a value other than Default  The default mapping for Model parameters  is a value other than Default
Workaround: If a non-inlined S-function references a parameter object in the base workspace or a data dictionary, check that all of the following conditions are satisfied:The parameter object has storage class Auto or Model DefaultThe default mapping for External parameters is DefaultThe default mapping for Model parameters is Default
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 4, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: MATLAB might crash with the assertion "In assertingCast: Failed to cast CG::Type (class CG::Type const * __ptr64)" while generating code
Description: MATLAB might crash when generating MISRA compliant code from a model that assigns data from custom #define  parameters in a Stateflow Chart. You might get the following?assertion message: CGIR assertion 'In assertingCast: Failed to cast CG::Type (class CG::Type const * __ptr64) from |unknown| to |class CG::QuasiNumericType|' failed in 'B:\ matlab\ src\ cg_ir\ export\ include\ cg_ir\ type\ Type. hpp:556'  [??0] 0x00007ffadcad2b0e??bin\ win64\ cg_ir. dll+00338702 CG::QuasiNumericType::cast+00000142[??1] 0x00007ffac83a748d?? bin\ win64\ cgir_cpp_emitter. dll+00947341 CG::domCpp::ResolveMisraRule17_7_A0_1_2::operator=+00338765
Workaround: Cast the #define parameter to it's underlying type at the point of use.For example given:#define TRUE (unsigned int8(1))The usage can be updated from someStateflowVar = TRUE; to someStateflowVar = uint8(TRUE);.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated when export-function model has access functions and root level Model block with muxed input signals
Description: Incorrect code would be generated when all of following conditions are met: Inside an export-function model, data root Inport blocks are configured to have access functions. At least one of those data root Inport blocks has its output signal muxed with other signals to drive a Model block in the root level of the export-function model.?
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for?a model containing a?Unit Delay block inside a?For Each Subsystem block
Description: The generated code contains an incorrect initialization of a Unit Delay block state for a model that meets all of these conditions: The Unit Delay block is inside a For Each subsystem. The Initial Condition  of the Unit Delay block initialization is specified by a parameter instead of a fixed value. In the For Each Subsystem block parameters dialog box, the?Parameter Partition ?is selected .   There is a bug with the same symptoms introduced in release R2018b. See 2047809 for more information.?
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: Lookup Table Editor plots fail to update after fixed-point data is edited
Description: When you view the plot for a Lookup Table with fixed-point table data or breakpoints, the plot fails to update after you edit the fixed-point data.
Workaround: Reopen the plot.?

Summary: Using?visdiff with models containing internally saved test harnesses might error if Simulink Test license not available
Description: Using?visdiff  with models containing internally saved test harnesses might error if a Simulink Test license is not available. For example, comparing the f14 and f14_2 models using visdiff('f14','f14_2')   might produce this error:? Unable to check out a Simulink Test license needed to use this feature.?
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 6, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code might produce incorrect results if model contains For Each subsystem that accepts a nonscalar signal as input
Description: The generated code might be incorrect if your model satisfies?all ?these conditions: The model contains a For Each subsystem. The For Each subsystem accepts an input that is a nonscalar signal In the subsystem, at least one nonscalar signal connects to multiple output ports.  This is an example of generated incorrect code: void mRepro_ss_7(const real_T rtu_input[10], real_T rty_Out3[10]){? real_T tmp;? int32_T ForEach_itr;? int32_T i;? int32_T i_0;? int32_T tmp_0;? memcpy(&forEachInput[0], &rtu_input[0], 10U * sizeof(real_T));? memcpy(&shared_output2[0], &rty_Out3[0], 10U * sizeof(real_T));? for (i = 0; i < 2; i++) {??? for (i_0 = 0; i_0 < 5; i_0++) {????? forEachInput[i + (i_0 << 1)] = rtu_input[(i_0 << 1) + i];??? }??? for (ForEach_itr = 0; ForEach_itr < 2; ForEach_itr++) {????? tmp =? mRepro_no_2_19a_ConstP.ForEachSS_rtw_collapsed_sub_expr_0[ForEach_itr];????? for (i_0 = 0; i_0 < 5; i_0++) {??????? tmp_0 = (i_0 << 1) + ForEach_itr;??????? rty_Out3[tmp_0] = forEachInput[tmp_0] * tmp;????? }??? }? } }  In this code, the?memcpy ?operation for copying data from?shared_output ?to output port is executed before the?ForEach_itr ?loop. This code pattern produces incorrect results. The correct generated code for the above example looks like the following. Here the?memcpy ?operation for copying data from?shared_output ?to output port is executed after the?ForEach_itr ?loop. void mRepro_ss_7(const real_T rtu_input[10], real_T rty_Out3[10]) {  real_T tmp;  int32_T ForEach_itr;  int32_T i;  int32_T s1_iter;  int32_T tmp_0;  memcpy( & forEachInput[0], & rtu_input[0], 10 U * sizeof(real_T));  for (s1_iter = 0; s1_iter < 2; s1_iter++) {    for (i = 0; i < 5; i++) {      forEachInput[s1_iter + (i << 1)] = rtu_input[(i << 1) + s1_iter];    }    for (ForEach_itr = 0; ForEach_itr < 2; ForEach_itr++) {      tmp = mRepro_no_2_23a_ConstP.ForEachSS_rtw_collapsed_sub_expr_4847605804183166791_1[ForEach_itr];      for (i = 0; i < 5; i++) {        tmp_0 = (i << 1) + ForEach_itr;        rty_Out3[tmp_0] = forEachInput[tmp_0] * tmp;      }    }    memcpy( & shared_output2[0], & rty_Out3[0], 10 U * sizeof(real_T));  }}
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Symbolic dimension is incorrectly inlined during model reference code generation
Description: The code generated for a model reference hierarchy might incorrectly inline the symbolic dimension variable for a signal under these conditions:The signal crosses model reference boundaries. One of the parent models in the model reference hierarchy is not connected to the data source (base workspace, Simulink data dictionary, or external source) used by the referenced model.?
Workaround: Explicitly link the parent model(s) to the data source, even if the objects in the source are not directly used by the model.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Models containing Stateflow Charts that have conditionally executed?while?or?do-while?constructs might result in incorrect code
Description: Incorrect code might occur for a model that meets the following conditions:The model contains a Stateflow Chart and a While Iterator block. The?Function packaging ?parameter of the Chart is set to Auto  or Inline.  The Chart also contains a conditionally executed while  or do-while  construct.  In the generated code, a part of the?while ?or do-while  loop is incorrectly hoisted.? For example, the correct code contains this while  loop that contains a do-while  loop while() {?  ? ? a =? inMatrix[1];  ? ? do {?  ? ? ? ? a++;?  ? ? ? ? // do something else  ? ? } while();  ? ? for(i=...) {?  ? ? ? ? outMatrix[i] += a? ? ?  ? ? }? }? In the incor rect code, the do-while  loop is incorrectly hoisted, and the outer while  loop becomes: do {  ? ? // do something else?  ?} while();?  while() {?  ? ? temp = inMatrix[1];?  ? ? for(i=...) {?  ? ? ? ? outMatrix[i] += temp ?} }?
Workaround: Set the?Function packaging of the Stateflow chart to Reusable function or Nonreusable function.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: Generating IP core for?Generic Xilinx Platform?in HDL Workflow Advisor may fail when the Allow clock-rate pipelining for DUT output ports settings is enabled for a model
Description: Using the IP Core generation workflow for Generic Xilinx Platform , HDL Workflow Advisor task 3.2 Generate RTL code and IP core ?fails if the configuration parameters Allow clock-rate pipelining for DUT output ports  is enabled for the model. The following error is generated:? Error using hdlcoder.pir/getDutExtraLatency  Unexpected Error during HDL Code Generation.   Contact support@mathworks.com with reproduction steps.   Assertion Failed With Debug Message:   B:\matlab\src\cgir_hdl\dom_pir_core\delaydata.cpp:405:outPort->isData()
Workaround: Disable Allow Clock-rate Pipelining for DUT output ports in the configuration parameters.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for?eig might incorrectly return NaN values
Description: The eig  function uses an iterative algorithm to solve for eigenvalues and eigenvectors, and this algorithm might fail to converge in certain situations. In such situations, the function returns NaN ?values , and targets that support warnings also produce a warning. This issue is a possibility in both  MATLAB and the generated code. The implementation of the eig  function in the generated code is based on an older version of LAPACK and?might return NaN ?values when the output in MATLAB is finite. This issue can occur if both  these conditions are true:? ?You generate a static library, a dynamic library, or an executable by using MATLAB Coder or Simulink Coder ?You do not instruct the code generator to include calls to an external LAPACK library  This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 4, R2022b

Summary: Code generation error when code interface packaging is C++ Class and system target file is custom derived from ert.tlc
Description: C++ code generation might produce an error for a model with the configuration parameter CodeInterfacePackaging ?set to C++ Class? when the model uses a custom target file derived from ert.tlc. The error assertion might be similar to either of the following: Code generation assertion 'fpcArgSpecType != FPC_Value' failed in..  STD exception 'std::logic_error': 'basic_string::_M_construct null not valid' was caught.
Workaround: Review? the instructions to "Support C Function Prototype Control" for custom embedded targets and apply the steps found in section "Declaring C Function Prototype Control Compliance".
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 2, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect rapid accelerator mode simulation results for models using root-level bus element ports and function-call input ports
Description: Simulating a model results in incorrect answers when all ?of these conditions apply: The model simulates in rapid accelerator mode. The model has at least one In Bus Element or Out Bus Element block at the root level. The model has at least one Inport block at the root level that outputs a function-call signal and has a sample time of -1 . The ExternalInput  configuration parameter specifies time steps when the function-call input will run.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 1, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code might be incorrect for model containing variant control expressions and a subsystem which is set to be generated in a separate file
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that contains variant control expressions, a reusable subsystem and the File Packaging  model configuration parameter set to Modular . The generated code is incorrect because the separate file generated for the subsystem does not contain a?#include  statement for the required header file that defines the variants.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Export-function model with multiple function-call root Inport blocks driving a function-call subsystem with discontiguous output might result in incorrect code
Description: The signal memory for the root Outport block in the generated code might not be written to when all these conditions apply:? The model that contains the root Outport block is an export-function model. The model contains two root Inport blocks that output function-call signals. The root Inport blocks connect to the same Mux block. The output of the Mux block drives a function-call subsystem. The function-call subsystem drives a root Outport block. The signal that drives the corresponding Outport block in the function-call subsystem is discontiguous. A discontiguous signal can result from Mux, Selector, or Bus Selector blocks.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Reinitialized triggered subsystem might generate incorrect code
Description: A triggered subsystem might generate incorrect code when all these conditions are met: The triggered subsystem is reinitialized. The Trigger block in the triggered subsystem has Trigger type ?set to rising , falling , or either . The Trigger block in the triggered subsystem has Initial trigger signal state ?set to zero , positive , or negative .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated when a parameter or signal is an array of buses and specifies preservation of dimensions
Description: The code generated for a model might be incorrect when the model has a signal or parameter that meets these conditions. The parameter is an array of buses. The parameter specifies preservation of dimensions. A bus in the array has an element that is an array.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 4, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: MATLAB might crash when you select and move signal lines in a Simulink model multiple times using the arrow keys
Description: MATLAB might crash when you select and move signal lines in a Simulink model multiple times using the arrow keys. Moving signal lines does not include changing connections. The generated stack trace contains lines similar to the following: [ 0] 0x00007ffd4ab671cb Simulink::Routing::getPartialSegment[ 1] 0x00007ffd4ab7f598 `anonymous namespace'::getPartialSegmentKeyPairs?
Workaround: When moving signal lines using arrow keys, reselect the lines after each move or drag them using the pointer.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 2, R2023a

Summary: Having a large number of Data Dictionary objects causes a degradation in Model Explorer performance
Description: Having a large number of Data Dictionary objects can negatively affect the performance of the Model Explorer to the point that the Model Explorer becomes unresponsive.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 4, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: Accelerator mode simulation of an export function model with root-level Simulink function generates an incorrect result
Description: Accelerator mode simulation generates incorrect result for an export function model if all the following conditions are met: The model contains a Simulink function at the root level. The Simulink function is called from another export function within the model. The Simulink function contains a nested function-call subsystem.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 1, R2023a

Summary: polyspace-configure?cannot extract predefined macro definitions of qcc compiler
Description: polyspace-configure  can extract the predefined macro definitions of the gcc  compiler by passing option -dM  to that compiler (gcc -dM  lists all the gcc  predefined macros). Because qcc  is a gcc  based compiler, Polyspace attempts to use the same option to obtain the qcc  macro definition. However, because qcc  uses a slightly different option to list its predefined macros, polyspace-configure  cannot extract the qcc  macro definitions and you see a warning message similar to this in the log: WARNING: The predefined macros could not be computed and it may result in an incomplete project  If Polyspace does not know the definitions of the compiler macros, the analysis might fail.
Workaround: Use the option -import-macro-definitions from-source-tokens when you run polyspace-configure.The option changes the strategy that?polyspace-configure?uses to compute the predefined macros for only the current run.

Summary: System object blocks are not considered when verifying unsupported blocks check using HDL Model Advisor
Description: The HDL Model Advisor does not consider system object blocks when verifying unsupported blocks during the unsupported block check.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Reusable subsystem outport with wide signal having a Source block with constant sample time might result in incorrect code
Description: Incorrect code might be generated for a model that meets all these conditions: The DefaultParameterBehavior ?configuration parameter is set to Inlined . The?BufferReusableBoundary  c onfiguration parameter is selected. The model contains a reusable subsystem whose outport has a muxed signal. The muxed signal at the reusable subsystem?outport has a source with constant sample time and  a source with discrete or asynchronous sample time
Workaround: Manually insert a Signal Conversion block inside the reusable subsystem at the outport.

Summary: Assertion thrown during HDL code generation when using clock-rate pipelining with reusable subsystems
Description: When using clock-rate pipelining in R2022a, an assertion can be hit when SubsystemReuse  is turned on or For Each Subsystem blocks are used.? Assertion Failed With Debug Message: matlab\src\cgir_hdl\pir_transforms\PirNetworkSort.cpp:330:! state->done
Workaround: To avoid creating a reused subsystem, enable the HDL block propery?FlattenHierarchy?on the subsystem to request inlining during the code generation process.

Summary: UAVScenario Designer App loads session incorrectly after sensor renaming.
Description: Inthe UAV Scenario Designer app, changing the default name of a sensor beforesaving a session causes the session file to load incorrectly, where the scenebrowser does not show all the platforms, and some entities are not renderedin the scenario view.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Some trigonometric functions in CORDIC mode for Trigonometric Function, Magnitude-Angle to Complex, and MATLAB Function blocks might compute incorrect output
Description: Incorrect output might occur for Trigonometric Function, Magnitude-Angle to Complex, and MATLAB Function blocks when all of the following conditions are true: The model contains one or more of the following: Trigonometric Function block with Function  parameter set to sin ,  cos ,  sincos , or  cos + jsin ?and Approximation method  set to  CORDIC .  Magnitude-Angle to Complex block with Approximation method  parameter set to  CORDIC .  MATLAB Function block that calls any CORDIC function.  The blocks have high-precision fixed-point data types as input,?where the data type can represent values with a magnitude of at least 1 but not greater than 2. The Input port values are outside the range  [-pi/2, pi/2] .   This issue might also affect CORDIC-based trigonometric MATLAB functions when called outside the MATLAB Function block.

Description:
MATLAB might occasionally crash during startup. When this happens MATLAB does not open, but displays a crash report dialog. Summary: Incorrect diagnostic error message with sltest.testmanager.createTestForComponent and SLDVTestGeneration
Description: sltest. testmanager. createTestForComponent  with?SLDVTestGeneration ? property  On , throws an incorrect diagnostic error:  ?A field named 'InputScenario' doesn't exist ?.  This happens when the invoked?SLDVTestGeneration ? ?generates 9 or more tests during the analysis.

Summary: Code generation for MATLAB code containing str2double function might fail with an error
Description: Code generation from MATLAB might fail if both  these conditions are true:  Your MATLAB code passes a character array to the?str2double  function?  The code configuration property Dynamic Memory Allocation for Variable-Size Arrays ('DynamicMemoryAllocation') is disabled   For example, consider this MATLAB function:? function out = foo(inData)idx = zeros(2,2);j = 1;idx(2,2) = 2;for i = 1:2    idx(j, 1) = i - 1;    j = j + 1;endc = char(inData(idx(2,1):idx(2,2)));out = real(str2double(c));end Suppose that you attempt to generate code for foo  by running these commands:? cfg = coder.config('lib');cfg.DynamicMemoryAllocation = 'Off';codegen -config cfg foo -args {[49 48]} -report In such situations, MATLAB displays the following error message: Computed maximum size is not bounded.  Static memory allocation requires all sizes to be bounded.  The computed size is [1 x :?].  Please consider enabling dynamic memory allocation to allow unbounded sizes.  Error in ==> str2double  When using Simulink Coder, this issue can affect code generation for a Simulink model that satisfies all? these conditions: The model contains a MATLAB Function block The code in the MATLAB Function block passes a character array to the?str2double ?function  Dynamic memory allocation in MATLAB functions disabled
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b

Summary: MATLAB might crash when a component is added to an architecture with default component stereotype when profile is unhealthy
Description: Adding components to architectures with a default component stereotype will apply the default component stereotype to the added component automatically. This default component stereotype is defined in another stereotype in a profile imported into the model. When the profile is unhealthy (i.e. a necessary enumeration file is missing), adding a component in an architecture with a default component stereotype will result in a crash.?
Workaround: Repair the profile so that it goes back into a healthy state. For example, if an enumeration file is missing, put the enumeration file back in scope.

Summary: MATLAB scripts generated by the Design Filter Live Editor task may not update correctly when filter parameter values are changed in quick succession
Description: Setting two or more filter parameters back-to-back in quick succession may cause some of the parameters to not be updated in the MATLAB code generated by the Design Filter  Live Editor task.

Summary: Configuring UDP Send to broadcast address 255.255.255.255 causes error when loading real-time application
Description: If the To IP address  parameter in the UDP Send block is set to broadcast address 255.255.255.255 , Simulink Real-Time reports an error when loading the real-time application.
Workaround: In R2021b, you can set the To IP address in the UDP send block to the subnet broadcasting address.For example, if the local IP address is 10.10.10.15 and the subnet mask is 255.255.255.0, the subnet broadcasting address is 10.10.10.255.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a

Summary: Mask initialization error in the Filter block
Description: A mask initialization error occurs in the Filter block when you configure the Filter block in the following order:Change?Design method  parameter from Ideal   to either Butterworth   or Chebyshev  Set?Implementation  parameter to either LC Tee  or LC Pi  Select Apply   Error: Block Parameters: Filter  Error in 'untitled/Filter': Failed to evaluate mask initialization commands.  Caused by:  Unrecognized field name "DesignData".
Workaround: Use one of the following:Delete and then re-add the Filter block.Set Design method to Inverse Chebyshev and select Apply, and then set Design method and Implementation parameters as desired.Set Design method to Butterworth or Chebyshev. Next, set Implementation to Transfer function and?select Apply. Then change Implementation parameter as desired.

Summary: In certain cases, pointAt method of satellite scenario Satellite class interprets Euler angle inputs in radians rather than degrees
Description: When the?satelliteScenario ?object property?AutoSimulate ?is false, Euler angle attitude data provided to the Satellite  pointAt  method as part of timetable  or timeseries  data?is incorrectly treated as radians rather than degrees.?

Summary: Error can occur during training for an agent with rlTable due to undefined struct2array function
Description: If struct2array ?is not available or undefined, you might encounter an error during training for an agent that uses rlTable (for example, rlQAgent and rlSARSAAgent).?This does not occur if you have Simulink or Signal Processing Toolbox installed.

Summary: Verify Configuration fails during Deep Learning HDL Toolbox Hardware Support Package setup
Description: After installing the Xilinx or Intel hardware support package for Deep Learning HDL Toolbox, there is a guided hardware setup process, as shown here: https://www.mathworks.com/help/supportpkg/xilinxdeeplearning/ug/guided-sd-card-setup.html   When running through the guided setup, the last step to Verify Configuration consistently fails with an error such as: "Could not connect to Zynq hardware board using IP address 192. 168. 1. 101.  Check that your device is connected to a LAN or direct network connection accessible from the MATLAB development computer. " ?
Workaround: Ignore the error message and close guided setup wizard. You may proceed with the Deep Learning HDL Toolbox workflow. The hardware connectivity will also be verified in the regular workflow.

Summary: String type custom attributes are imported from IBM DOORS Next as enumerated type
Description: When importing requirements from IBM DOORS Next to Requirements Toolbox, requirements with custom attributes of string data type are imported as enumerated data type instead. This is viewed as a combobox based custom attribute instead of a text edit based custom attribute.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 5, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Bit to Integer Converter block with Input bit order set to?LSB First wraps to incorrect value when overflow occurs
Description: The Bit to Integer Converter block fails to wrap input data correctly when converting a number that exceeds the number of bits specified for the output data type when all of the following conditions are true: ? Bit to Integer Converter block?After bit packing, treat resulting integer values as ?parameter is set to an unsigned type. Bit to Integer Converter block?Input bit order ?parameter is set to LSB First . Bit to Integer Converter block?Output data type ?parameter is set to a signed type that is smaller than the number of bits in the input bitstream.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when simulating n-D Lookup Table block with fixed-point data types?in a model reference hierarchy
Description: MATLAB might crash when you simulate a model if all of the following conditions are true: Parent model contains a Model block with at least one breakpoint or table passed in as a model argument. Model block contains an n-D Lookup Table block with?Source? set to?Input port ?and ?at least one breakpoint or table input.? The model argument passed from the model block is set as a parameter value to the breakpoint or table parameter.  The n-D Lookup Table block inputs, breakpoints, and table data types are fixed-point.
Workaround: Replace the n-D Lookup table block with its equivalent Prelookup and Interpolation Using Prelookup block combination.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 4, R2022b

Summary: Exporting Simulink Design Verifier generated test cases to Simulink Test causes Export operation aborted error
Description: Exporting test cases from Simulink Design Verifier to Simulink Test displays an Export operation aborted  error dialog box when all the following conditions are true: The analysis is performed on a subsystem block The subsystem contains Function Caller  blocks without corresponding Simulink functions or blocks in the AUTOSAR Blockset Basic Software block library that model component calls to AUTOSAR Basic Software (BSW) services Harness source is set to Signal Editor  or Signal Builder

Summary: UAV Scenario Designer app scales custom geometry to unit scale.
Description: In the UAV ScenarioDesigner app, importing a custom geometry using SceneObject > Custom Object option causes the imported geometry to besized down to unit scale between [-0.5,0.5].?This issue alsooccurs if you import a custom platform using Platform> Custom Platform.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for svd might produce incorrect results if the input is an empty matrix
Description: In the generated code,?[U,S,V] = svd(A) ?might return incorrect results for the outputs U  and V  if: You specify the input matrix A ?to be variable-size during code generation, and  The input matrix?A  reduces to an empty matrix at runtime.?  This issue can arise if any  of these conditions are true:?  ?You generate a MEX file ?You use svd  in a MATLAB function block ?You generate a static library, a dynamic library, or an executable, and instruct the code generator to include calls to an external LAPACK library
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 4, R2022b

Summary: Long responses from?readline?and writeread?on?visadev?objects can contain corrupt data
Description: When using?readline ?or?writeread ?for?visadev ?objects, responses greater than 4096 characters might contain corrupt data. The following example shows data that is not corrupted and data that is corrupted from the same GPIB resource. resource = "GPIB0::5::INSTR";vd = visadev(resource);%% Set up the DMM to return 256 readings (after ~1 sec) - no data corruptionwriteline(vd, "*RST")writeline(vd, "SAMP:COUN 256") writeline(vd, "SAMP:SOUR TIM") writeline(vd, "SAMP:TIM 0. 01") writeline(vd, "TRIG:SOUR BUS") writeline(vd, "INIT") writeline(vd, "*TRG")%% Each ASCII reading requires 16 bytes,  which requires 16*256, or 4096, bytes of storage. correctData =  writeread(vd, "FETC?")%% Set up the DMM to return 257 readings (after ~1 sec) - data corruptionwriteline(vd, "*RST")writeline(vd, "SAMP:COUN 257") writeline(vd, "SAMP:SOUR TIM") writeline(vd, "SAMP:TIM 0. 01") writeline(vd, "TRIG:SOUR BUS") writeline(vd, "INIT") writeline(vd, "*TRG")incorrectData = writeread(vd, "FETC?")

Summary: Long processing time for the Model Advisor to check product license availability
Description: When executing checks, the Model Advisor verifies that the required product license is available. Sporadically, a long processing time occurs when the license checking mechanism is invoked by custom checks or when running edit-time checks.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect Simulation results for modelscontaining inline variants with?startup?activation and?updatediagram analyze all choices
Description: Incorrect Simulation results for modelscontaining inline variants with?startup ?activation combined when with?updatediagram analyze all choices? variant activations. The incorrect behavior is seen when all below conditionsare met: Model should contain variant source or sink withstartup ?activation and connected to another variant source or sink withupdate diagram analyze all choices? activation. Thevariant source or sink with update diagram?analyze?all choices  activationmust be inside the atomic subsystem.  Model is in Accelerator  modeSimulation or GRT code generation
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 5, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: HDL code generation for Action Subsystems that are configured to be reusable can result in a validation model mismatch
Description: If Action Subsystems contain identical logic and are either marked atomic or SubsystemReuse  is set to Atomic and Virtual , the validation model could show a mismatch. Output selection logic on the Merge block may be wired incorrectly in the generated model and the resulting generated HDL code.
Workaround: Configure the Action Subsystems to not be reusable or set SubsystemReuse to off to avoid the validation model mismatch.>> hdlset_param(<model_name>, 'SubsystemReuse', 'off')

Summary: Test execution can error out in SIL mode
Description: If a test case is configured to run in Software in the Loop (SIL) simulation mode and has signals logged in the Simulation Outputs section, then the execution can error out.

Summary: The installer for the development version of MATLAB Web App Server in MATLAB Compiler cannot connect to the internet to download MATLAB Runtime
Description: On Windows systems, when running the installer for the development version of MATLAB Web App Server in MATLAB Compiler, the installation fails and an error dialog displays the following message: "Call to download server failed (HTTP error code: 12007). Try to rerun the installer."?
Workaround: Follow instructions in the Limited Internet Connectivity Installation section of the?documentation.?

Summary: Using admin-docker-agent fails when trying to pull gateway-image
Description: Executing admin-docker-agent  fails to pull gateway-image with one of the following errors: Pulling image mw-docker.repositories.mathworks.com/com.mathworks.cloudservices.gateway.gateway-image:1.22.0  Error response from daemon: Get "https://mw-docker.repositories.mathworks.com/v2/": Bad Gateway  Error response from daemon: Get https://mw-docker.repositories.mathworks.com/v2/: dial tcp: lookup mw-docker.repositories.mathworks.com: no such host   The script for creating the distribution of R2022a Update 1 Polyspace Access uses gateway-image version 1.23.0, but the admin service uses gateway-image version1.22.0.? The admin service incorrectly tries to pull gateway-image version 1.22.0, which is not included in the distribution zip file.
Workaround: If you already attempted to install Update 1, run the following commands to re-tag gateway docker image and re-install Update 1:docker tag mw-docker.repositories.mathworks.com/com.mathworks.cloudservices.gateway.gateway-image:1.23.0 mw-docker.repositories.mathworks.com/com.mathworks.cloudservices.gateway.gateway-image:1.22.0./admin-docker-agent

Summary: Assigning a custom name to ERTHeaderFileRootName?and generating code for a?model that uses a storage class generates an extra header
Description: The code generator generates an extra header file model .h ?when both of the following are true:  The model uses a storage class You assign a custom name to the configuration parameter ERTHeaderFileRootName ?  No other generated files reference the model .h  file.?The file might incorrectly include the header file configured in the storage class HeaderFile  property.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 4, R2022b, R2023a

Description: When generating an aggregated coverage report for coverage collected from different test harnesses the following error might occur:? Not Unique

Summary: Security Issue: tab completion may execute code
Description: Using the tab completion feature for a command involving a MAT-file may execute code contained within the specified MAT-file.? If the MAT-file comes from an untrusted source, tab completion could have unexpected behavior.

Summary: Incorrect validation metrics when using the?trainNetwork?function and moving batch normalization statistics
Description: When you train a network with batch normalization layers using the?trainNetwork ?function with the BatchNormalizationStatistics ?training option set to?"moving" , ?the accuracy, RMSE, and loss validation metrics are incorrect.
Workaround: When you train the network, use an output function that reads from checkpoint networks and calculates the validation accuracy using the predict function.For an example, see the attached files.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 2, R2022b

Summary: MATLAB Java engine crashes when called from user defined threads
Description: If a call to MATLAB engine is made from a user defined Java thread, then the Java application crashes.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 3, R2022b

Summary: Security Issue: who -file might execute code
Description: Calling who -file  on a MAT-file can execute code contained within the specified MAT-file. If the MAT-file comes from an untrusted source, this function could have unexpected behavior.

Summary: "Error using attach_name"?when opening MATLAB apps on Windows with no internet connection
Description: When opening a MATLAB app on a Windows system without an internet connection, MATLAB throws this error:? Error using attach_name?  Unexpected internal error in shareEngine: System error: Failed to get current user groups?   Error in matlab.engine.isEngineShared (line 17)?   name = attach_name;?

Summary: The Comparison Tool errors because of missing SimBiology license
Description: If you have SimBiology installed, but do not have a license for SimBiology, opening the Comparison Tool throws the error: registerSimBiologyDiffGUIProviders requires a SimBiology license.
Workaround: Remove SimBiology from the path by executing the following commands in the MATLAB Command Window:rmpath(fullfile(matlabroot,"toolbox","simbio","simbio"))savepath

Summary: JMAAB check for?jc_0741?reports incorrect warning for variables used in condition action in Stateflow charts
Description: Model Advisor check Check updates to variables used in state transition conditions ?(id:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0741) ?reports incorrect warning for variables used in condition action. This happens when the variables are not updated inside the functions called in during action of state in Stateflow charts.

Summary: Model containing Constantblocks with overflowing fixed-point values might cause simulation mismatch?
Description: Simulation mismatch may occur in the generatedmodel when your model contains Constant block with a fixed-point data type and thefixed-point value exceeds the largest value that can be precisely representedusing double datatype.?

Summary: ecdf?might return incorrect answer for empirical CDF, survivor function, or cumulative hazard function when input data is double-censored
Description: The ecdf  function might return an incorrect answer for the empirical CDF, survivor function, or cumulative hazard function if double-censored input data is specified by using the?"Censoring"  name-value argument. An incorrect answer is returned by ecdf ?if the double-censored input data contains values at which there are: many right-censored observations no fully-observed observations no left-censored observations
Workaround: Interval-censoring is a generalization of double-censoring. Use the interval-censoring format by specifying the input argument y?as a two-column matrix with rows specifying the range of possible values for each observation. For an observation that occurs at the value t, the corresponding interval-censored format is:[t,t]?ˇŞ Fully observed at?t[¨CInf,t]?ˇŞ Left-censored at?t[t,Inf]?ˇŞ Right-censored at?t
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 5, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: Logged signals not shown for tests using block diagram harness and parameter overrides if model dirtied during compilation
Description: A test that uses a block diagram harness and parameter overrides does not show the logged signals in the Test Manager if the model is dirtied by a callback during compilation, such as by an InitFcn  on a mask.
Workaround: Set the Dirty flag of the model to off in the postload callback of the test case:?set_param('<model_name>','Dirty','off');
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 3, R2023a

Summary: MATLAB crashes when Simulink Design Verifier generated test cases are exported to Simulink Test.
Description: MATLAB crashes if the test cases generated by Simulink Design Verifier are exported to Simulink Test when all the following conditions are true: The analysis is performed on a model block The referenced model contains Function Caller blocks without corresponding Simulink functions or blocks in the AUTOSAR Blockset Basic Software block library that model component calls to AUTOSAR Basic Software (BSW) services Harness source is set to Inport   The generated stack trace contains a line similar to: [? 0] 0x00007fb950ae67dc sl::simharness::HarnessMiscAPI::clearContTsSetting(double, slsvString, double)

Summary: Numeric differences between SIL/PIL and normal mode simulations when logged output has constant sample time
Description: When a model or subsystem under test has an output with constant sample time, you might observe numeric differences between SIL/PIL and normal mode simulations for these scenarios. Scenario 1:  A Model block has these parameter settings: Simulation mode  - Software-in-the-loop (SIL)  or Processor-in-the-loop (PIL)  Code interface  - Model reference  Show model reset ports ?- on   In the model under test, the output signal? with constant sample time is mapped to the Auto  storage class. The parent or harness model triggers the Reset  port of the Model block at a time greater than zero.  After the Reset? port trigger, the logged signal for the output with the constant sample time is set to zero, which is incorrect. Scenario 2:  A Model block has these parameter settings: Simulation mode  -?Software-in-the-loop (SIL) ?or?Processor-in-the-loop (PIL)  Code interface  -?Model reference  Show model initialize port  - on  The parent or harness model triggers the Initialize  port at a time greater than zero.  At t = 0, the logged signal for the output with the constant sample time is set to its final value, which is incorrect until the Initialize  port trigger, the logged signal value must be zero. ?Scenario 3:  A Model block has these parameter settings: Simulation mode  - Software-in-the-loop (SIL)  or Processor-in-the-loop (PIL)  Code interface  - Top model  Show model initialize port  - on   The parent or harness model triggers the Initialize  port at a time greater than zero.  After the Initialize  port trigger, the logged signal for the output with the constant sample time remains zero.Scenario 4:  A test harness is created for a subsystem in a model. An output of the subsystem has constant sample time. The SIL/PIL Manager or Simulink Test runs a SIL or PIL simulation of the subsystem under test.  The value logged for the output with constant sample time might be zero instead of the true value from the generated code.?
Fixed In: R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 5, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: Creating a Raspberry Pi connection fails in Desktop MATLAB
Description: In Desktop MATLAB with the MATLAB Support Package For Raspberry Pi Hardware installed, creating a connection to a Raspberry Pi board using the raspi  object fails. MATLAB displays the following error: picam.c:19:10: fatal error: RaspiCamControl.h: No such file or directory Updated standards in GitHub now require secure authentication using HTTPS. As a result, the raspberrypi/userland  repository in GitHub is not accessible from the existing installation scripts of the support package.
Workaround: To work around this issue, use either of the following:Install the latest support package updates for R2021a, R2021b, or R2022a.?For other releases, before creating the raspi object in MATLAB, run the following commands in a Raspberry Pi terminal:cd /optsudo git clone https://github.com/raspberrypi/userland.gitcd /opt/userlandsudo git pull originTip: You can use any software to SSH into Raspberry Pi to get the terminal window, or you can use the below link to launch a Raspberry Pi terminal window.https://magpi.raspberrypi.com/articles/terminal-help
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 4, R2022b
