The Roll , Pitch , Yaw  and Position  fields of input structure type BusActorPose  do not convert to the expected actor pose values.
Workaround: Manually convert the Roll, Pitch, Yaw and Position fields of input structure type BusActorPose to the Pose field of input structure type BusActorRuntime. Then, write the BusActorRuntime structure type with newly calculated Pose to the Actor Pose topic using the RoadRunner Scenario Writer block.

Summary: Simulation results might be wrong for subsystems that have a Reinitialize Function block and Stateflow charts with Simulink states
Description: Simulation might produce incorrect results for a subsystem when all the following conditions apply:? A Subsystem block has Show subsystem reinitialize ports  selected. The subsystem contains a Reinitialize Function block and Stateflow charts. The Stateflow charts have Simulink states. The Simulink states do not define another reinitialize function with the same event name as the Reinitialize Function block.

Summary: JMAAB check for?db_0126?reports incorrect warning for events in Stateflow charts
Description: Model Advisor check Check definition of Stateflow events ?(id:?mathworks.jmaab.db_0126) ?reports below incorrect warning for events used in parallel states. ? Warning  The following Stateflow events are not defined at theirsmallest scope:?  Event? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? Smallest Scope  model/Chart/E??? model/Chart/A/A2  model/Chart/F? ? model/Chart/A/A1?  This warning message is incorrect as the events are defined at the smallest possible scope.??
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 8, R2022b

Summary: Error on Linux when working with?HDF5 files on NFS4 network locations
Description: An error is thrown when using Linux platforms and working with HDF5 files in network locations that are mounted as NFS4. For example, the error can happen when using h5writeatt  or h5write  after h5create . >> outFile = 'myfile.h5';>> h5create(outFile, '/mysignal', [10 1])>> h5write(outFile, '/mysignal', ones(10, 1))Error using hdf5lib2Unable to open the file because of HDF5 Library error. Reason:UnknownError in H5F.open (line 130)file_id = H5ML.hdf5lib2('H5Fopen', filename, flags, fapl, is_remote);Error in h5write (line 108)    file_id    = H5F.open(Filename,flags,fapl); It can also happen when re-opening the file for writing using HDF5 Library Packages, as shown in this call to H5F.open . >> fid = H5F.open('myfile.h5','H5F_ACC_RDWR','H5P_DEFAULT');Error using hdf5lib2Unable to open the file because of HDF5 Library error. Reason:UnknownError in H5F.open (line 130)file_id = H5ML.hdf5lib2('H5Fopen', filename, flags, fapl, is_remote);
Workaround: This issue is specific to Linux platforms. To work around it, set the environment variable HDF5_USE_FILE_LOCKING to FALSE before launching MATLAB. For example, to set the environment variable in a?C-shell terminal, and then launch MATLAB from the same terminal session, use these commands.% setenv HDF5_USE_FILE_LOCKING FALSE% matlabTo set the environment variable in a bash terminal, use the "export" command, e.g.:% export HDF5_USE_FILE_LOCKING='FALSE'% matlabSetting this environment variable to FALSE?disables the file locking behavior that was introduced in HDF5 1.10.x versions (more details can be found here:?https://docs.hdfgroup.org/hdf5/rfc/RFC_THG_2014-01-10.pdf). In terms of file locking and protection from file corruption, setting HDF5_USE_FILE_LOCKING to FALSE is equivalent to using a MATLAB release earlier than R2021b, when we upgraded to HDF5 1.10.7. But disabling file locking like this is not recommended by The HDF Group (the developers and maintainers of HDF5).

Summary: Compatibility check may fail with top model and referenced model linked to different data dictionaries
Description: If the Rebuild model representation  option is set to If change detected  and if the top model and reference models point to different data dictionaries, subsequent runs of Design verifier analysis could fail with the following error: Validating cached model representation from 30-March-2022 13:07:52. . . Errors occurred during compilationThe data dictionary '<ModelName>_replacement_DD. sldd' was not found. '<ModelName>_replacement' is incompatible for test generation with Simulink Design Verifier.
Workaround: Set Rebuild model representation option is set to Always

Summary: Code generation does not work for RGB to BGR, BGR to RGB, and BGR to intensity options in the Color Space Conversion block
Description: Simulation works as expected but code generation does not work for all these Conversion  options in the Color Space Conversion block:RGB to BGR  BGR to RGB  BGR to intensity

Summary: Renamed signal in Signal Editor not saved with correct name in MAT-file if signal dimension greater than 1
Description: In Signal Editor, if you rename a signal that has a dimension size greater than 1, the updated name is reflected in the Signal Editor interface, but is not saved with the new name in the MAT-file.

Summary: Label UI components in a fitted?grid layout manager?might not appear
Description: Components in a grid layout manager might clip or disappear when all of these conditions hold:A grid layout manager is created using the uigridlayout  function. A label created with the uilabel  function is placed in the grid layout manager.? The ColumnWidth? property of the grid layout manager contains 'fit'  in the entry corresponding to the column containing the label.
Workaround: You can alternatively use a numeric value for the grid ColumnWidth property.fig = uifigure;g = uigridlayout(fig);g.ColumnWidth = {120};

Summary: Results download from Polyspace Access using the?polyspace-access?command fails.
Description: If you use the?polyspace-access ?command from a Polyspace product version R2022a or earlier to download results from Polyspace Access version R2022a_update1 or later, the download fails and you see an error message similar to this:?   Error: no chunk size found while parsing data from server answer.
Workaround: To work around this issue, upgrade the Polyspace client product that you use to download results from Polyspace Access to version R2022a_update1 or later.

Summary: Unable to load saved model workspace parameter value during model load/open operation
Description: Unable to load saved parameter values from model workspace during model load/open operation for?Narrowband Transmit Receive Array  or Monopulse Feed  Blocks (from Phased Array System Toolbox ). The issue is specific to values coming from the model workspace and in this case the block is loaded with the default settings coming from the library?
Workaround: Right click on the model and choose Model PropertiesClick the Callbacks tab and modify PostLoadFcn and CloseFcn as given below:In PostLoadFcn, add the model work space variables into the base work space:? ? ? ? hws = get_param(gcs,'modelworkspace')? ? ? ? a = getVariable(hws,'a')? ? ? ? e = getVariable(hws,'e')? ? ? ? s = getVariable(hws,'s')In CloseFcn, clean up the base work space.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 5, R2022b

Summary: MATLAB crash during "Check for Run-Time Issues" step in the MATLAB Coder app
Description: In the MATLAB Coder app, the Check for Run-Time Issues ?step might cause MATLAB to crash. The error message mentions "CycleEquivalence. cpp line 216:?In getClass: edge has no entry in CycleEquivalence::getClass ". A similar error might occur when simulating a model that contains MATLAB System blocks that have the Simulate using  ?parameter set to Code generation.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, clear?Collect MATLAB line execution counts?in the MATLAB Coder app during the Check for Run-Time Issues?step.See the attached image.To avoid this issue in Simulink, change?Simulate using?of the affected MATLAB System block to Interpreted execution. Alternatively, download and extract the attached .zip file. Run ">> SimulinkWorkaroundG2681332" in MATLAB command window.

Summary: Experiment Manager app shows incorrect export options after you refresh a custom training experiment running as a batch job
Description: After you refresh the results of a ?custom training experiment running as a batch job, the Experiment Manager app shows the options Export  >?Trained Network?and?Export?>?Training Information, instead of the correct option?Export?>?Training Output.?Additionally,  the option?Export?>?Results Table?is incorrectly disabled. This problem persists until you close and reopen the experiment results page.

Summary: Connecting a Message Triggered Subsystem block to a root Inport block might cause a MATLAB crash
Description: Using a Message Triggered Subsystem block might cause a MATLAB crash during model compilation if the following conditions are true: Trigger port of the Message Triggered Subsystem is connected to a root-level Inport block. The output port of the Trigger block inside the subsystem is unconnected or connected to virtual blocks for example, Outport block, From or Goto blocks, or Signal Specification block, that are not connected to nonvirtual blocks. By default, the Trigger block?is connected to the subsystem's Outport block and the crash occurs when the subsystem's output port is unconnected.

Summary: Exporting models with mixed datatypes in bus outputs as tool-coupling FMUs might produce incorrect simulation results
Description: When a model is exported as a tool-coupling FMU, the simulation results from the FMU might be incorrect if the following conditions are true: The model has a bus or nested bus output. The bus or nested bus has elements or sub-bus with mixed datatypes (boolean, double, int...).
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: An atomic subsystem with no inports or outports and containing a referenced model may crash during code generation
Description: When an atomic subsystem with no inports or outports contains a referenced model with a bus input and no outports, the model may crash during code generation.

Summary: Generating?multi-instance?code for a model with Message Merge block might cause a CGIR assertion error or MATLAB crash
Description: Building a model with Message Merge block might cause a CGIR assertion error or MATLAB crash for one of the following scenarios: You build the top model when the?model configuration parameter Code interface packaging ?is set to Reusable function.  You build a referenced model when the?model configuration parameter Total number of instances allowed per top model ?is set to Multiple.

Summary: Tests that specify "Stop simulation at last time point" and use external inputs for a test harness different than Test Case inputs might error during execution?
Description: When you run a test that has?Stop simulation at last time point ?selected in the Test Manager?Inputs ?section, and you use an external input signal? from a test harness with both of these conditions: The harness fails to compile. The harness is not the one you specified in the Test Manager?System Under Test ?section.  This error occurs: Invalid Simulink object name: <harness_name>.  The block diagram '<harness_name>' is not loaded.
Workaround: Deselect Stop Simulation at Last Time Point in the Test Manager Inputs section.Specify a Stop Time in the Simulation Settings and Release Overrides subsection?of the System Under Test section.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 2, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: An error during HDL code generation can occur for a model that has streaming on and contains an Assignment block
Description: When generating HDL code from a Simulink model with a subsystem that uses streaming and has an Assignment block with vector data input, this error during code generation can occur:  Error using hdlcoder.pirctx/createCGIR  Code generation failed with the error message : Undefined function 'Dimensions' for input arguments of type 'hdlcoder. tp_ufixpt'.

Summary: The generated MATLAB function is missing training code?for a model loaded from a saved session in Classification Learner or Regression Learner
Description: For a model loaded from a previously saved session in Classification Leaner or Regression Learner, the generated MATLAB function for training a machine learning model with new data is missing some code. The?problem occurs when you click Generate Function ?in the Export  section of the?Classification Learner ?or Regression Learner ?tab after selecting a model that was trained in a previous app session. The generated code does not include MATLAB function calls necessary for training the machine learning model (for example, fitctree ) and performing cross-validation. As a result, after running the generated code, the trainedModel  output structure does not contain a field with a trained model, and using the predictFcn  function of the output structure returns an invalid output.
Workaround: Duplicate the selected model by clicking?Duplicate?in the Models section of the?Classification Learner or Regression Learner?tab. For optimizable models, specify the hyperparameter values used by the original model in the Model Hyperparameters section of the model Summary tab. That is, in the duplicated model Summary tab, clear the Optimize check boxes and specify the hyperparameter values under Values.Train the duplicated model and then generate code.

Summary: When comparing variables, the Comparison Tool might fail or use the wrong comparison type
Description: The Comparison Tool might fail or use the wrong comparison type if both  conditions are true: You are comparing two variables. The names of the variables match names of files or folders on the MATLAB search path.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Root-level In Bus Element block with top-level virtual bus and nested nonvirtual bus might produce incorrect code
Description: A Simulink model might generate incorrect code when all the following conditions are true: A root-level In Bus Element block selects a nonvirtual bus from a virtual bus. A Bus Selector block selects a multidimensional leaf signal from the nonvirtual bus. A Selector block selects an element of the multidimensional leaf signal. The selected element of the multidimensional leaf signal passes to an atomic subsystem.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block after the Bus Selector block that selects the multidimensional leaf signal. Then, double-click the Signal Conversion block and select the?Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization parameter.

Summary: SQO report generation fails when report generator version is older than Polyspace Access version
Description: If you generate an SQO report from Polyspace Access results, and if the version of the Polyspace product that you use to generate the report is older than the Polyspace Access version, the report generation fails. You see an error message similar to this:? Polyspace Report GeneratorLoading resultsError: internal - unable to find check definition for: Defect_AUTOSAR_LIB_NON_COMPLIANCEError: internal - unable to find check definition for: Defect_INCORRECT_VALUE_FORWARDINGError: internal - unable to find check definition for: Defect_NOEXCEPT_FUNCTION_THROWS...?  The issue occurs when the Polyspace Access SQO definition that you use to generate the report contains defect or coding rule checkers that are not available? in the older Polyspace product version.
Workaround: To work around this issue:Log into PolyspaceAccess.Create a custom SQO definition where you select only defect andcoding rule checkers that are available in the older Polyspace product version.Apply the custom SQO definition to the project for which you want to create an SQO report.?Even if you are able generate an SQO report with the original SQO definition, use the custom SQO definition instead to avoid seeing defect and coding rule checkers that are not available in the version of the product used to generate the report.?
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 5, R2022b

Summary: Simscape compilation might fail with the error message "Invalid parameter type set" the first time a model is compiled or simulated
Description: When a Simscape block has a mask initialization function that sets the value of a parameter on the block, simulating the model can result in a confusing error with the message:  Invalid parameter type set at <parameter name>. Expected type ({logical,real,integer,index},~). Got type string.  This happens when a parameter that controls the visibility of other mask parameters is not properly evaluated before compilation.
Workaround: There are two ways that you can work around this issue:1) Call set_param twice in the mask initialization function. For example:>> set_param('circular', 'false');>> set_param('circular', 'false');?2) Call SIM twice and ignore the first error, for example:>> try;? sim(mdl); end;>> sim(mdl)?

Summary: License checkout can fail for Audio Toolbox when using a borrowed license
Description: When using?audioDeviceReader ?with a borrowed license, you might get this error: Unable to check out license for Audio Toolbox.
Workaround: Explicitly check out the borrowed toolbox license before using?audioDeviceReader. Use capital initial letters for the product name:license('checkout','Audio_System_Toolbox') % With the capitalization of ASTAn alternative workaround is to call an Audio Toolbox function before using?audioDeviceReader. For example, callmel2hz(2595)before?audioDeviceReader?to check out the borrowed license.

Summary: Continuous block inside model reference that uses local solver might generate wrong answer during normal mode simulation
Description: These blocks might produce an incorrect answer when they exist inside a model reference that uses a local solver and the top solver takes time steps that are smaller than the fixed step size for the local solver:? Rate Limiter block Derivative block Variable Transport Delay block Transport Delay block First Order Hold block with input signal that uses continuous sample time
Workaround: Do not use a local solver for model references that contain any of the affected blocks. You can disable the local solver for a model reference programmatically using the set_param function:??set_param(mdl,"UseModelRefSolver","off")For mdl, specify the model reference.?

Summary: Preserve array dimensions property not present in the Custom Storage Class Designer dialog box
Description: In the Custom Storage Class Designer dialog box, the Preserve array dimensions  property is not visible when For signals  property is selected and?For parameters ?property is not selected.
Workaround: Select For parameters even if the storage class is only used for signals.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when running MEX generated by GPU Coder with deep learning network if no GPU installed
Description: Running MEX generated by GPU Coder without a GPU causes CUDA errors. MATLAB crashes when running code generated for deep learning network during resource release. It prints out a message similar to the one below in the stack trace: 0x00007f89ee200ff0 [unknown function] at matlabroot/bin/glnxa64/libcublas.so.11+311280 (no debugging symbols found)

Summary: The evaluateSemanticSegmentation function returns a NaN value for boundary F1-measure (BF) scores.
Description: The evaluateSemanticSegmentation   function returns a NaN value for BF scores when the network predictions do not contain values for every class.

Summary: Reduced Order Flexible Solid block with ten or more interface frames gives erroneous?results
Description: When a Reduced Order Flexible Solid block has ten or more interface frames, the internal mapping between interface frames and connection frame ports on the block is incorrect, giving erroneous simulation results.

Summary: MATLAB might crash for models with multiple callsites of the same function or system object analyzed by Simulink Design Verifier
Description: Simulink Design Verifier might cause MATLAB to crash in rare instances when running Simulink Design Verifier with  Test Generation or Design Error Detection being run with MCDC objective enabled.  Multiple calls to the same function or multiple instances of the same system object in the model.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 2, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Logging messages may produce incorrect time data when generating multitasking code for a model with messages and different sample times
Description: ?If you use one of the following logging methods to log messages in a message line: Connecting a Scope block to the message line and selecting the?Log data to workspace  checkbox? Using To Workspace block? Using Simulink Data Inspector?  and the message line is connected to blocks with different sample times, logging may produceincorrect results in the?time?field of the logged workspace data, in one of the following scenarios: You generate code when the model parameter EnableMultiTasking  (Treat each discrete rate as a separate task ) is enabled, You use rapid accelerator mode simulation and the logging block is directly/indirectly connected to periodic/aperiodic partitions.
Workaround: For code generation, use one of these workarounds:In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, in the?Solver?pane, clear the checkbox?Treat each discrete rate as a separate task.At the MATLAB command prompt, enter set_param(<model_name>, EnableMultiTasking, 'off').

Summary: Incorrect simulation results for compartments when unit conversion is enabled
Description: SimBiology might return incorrect simulation results for compartments when all of the following conditions are met: Unit conversion is enabled. The model contains multiple compartments with different units. The model contains nested compartments.  In such cases, SimBiology might use the wrong unit multiplier to convert a compartment capacity from internal units to user-specified units. Internally, the simulation proceeds with the correct units, and the reported simulation results are correct for species and parameters.
Workaround: Work around the problem by doing any of the following:Disable unit conversion.Ensure all compartments have the same units.Convert all nested compartments to top-level compartments.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Simulation 3D Probabilistic Radar mounts incorrectly when setting a non-zero?Relative rotation?parameter
Description: If you select the Simulation 3D Probablistic Radar block Specify offset ?parameter and set?Relative? rotation ?to a non-zero value, the block incorrecly mounts the sensor.
Workaround: Apply a degree-to-radian conversion to the?Relative rotation [Roll, Pitch, Yaw]?values. For example,? to provide a 60 deg roll, 45 deg pitch, and 10 deg yaw offset, set?Relative rotation [Roll, Pitch, Yaw]? to?deg2rad([60, 45, 10]).The conversion mounts sensor correctly in the 3D environment and corrects the sensor output detections. However, the Birds Eye Scope may not correctly provide the field-of-view (FOV).

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Single-rate sharing/streaming can cause a silent wrong answer when the clock-rate region also has extra clock-rate latency
Description: A Modelsim simulation failure or a numerical mismatch between the original model and the generated model might occur if these conditions are met: ? Sharing Factor or Streaming Factor is set to be greater than 1 Clock-Rate Pipelining is being used Amount of clock-rate latency in the clock-rate region containing resource serialization is greater than the oversampling factor

Summary: Signal label mismatch during test harness compliation
Description: If you have a test harness for a subsystem whose output signal is explicitly named, and signal label propagation is enabled, this error might occur during compilation:Signal label mismatch: <harness_name>/Subsystem output port 1 label <signal_name>, should match <harness_name>/Output Conversion Subsystem output port 1 label <signal_name><propagated_signal_name>.
Workaround: 1) Manually set Show propagated signals?to ON?for the?signals that are outputs of the signal conversion subsystem.2)? Set Signal label mismatch?to none in the?Diagnostics?> Connectivity?category of Model Configuration Parameters.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 3, R2022b

Summary: Loss of test objectives when performing test case generation in C/C++ S-Functions and Simulink Custom Code blocks
Description: Loss of all test objectives for functions in Custom code/C Caller blocks that have name longer that 100 characters. You may notice different results for two functions with the same body but with different names. This scenario may happen in a workflow where function names are automatically generated/mangled and where the name are long.?

Summary: Reading map data from HERE HD Live Map web service can cause error
Description: MATLAB can issue an error when you read map data from the HERE HD Live Map web service using these options: The Driving Scenario Designer app The roadNetwork  object function of the drivingScenario  object The read  object function of the hereHDLMReader  object  An example of the error when constructing hereHDLMReader object is:? The '' protocol specified in URL, 'catalogs?verbose=1&organizationType=here&layerType=versioned&q=hdlm-protobuf', is not supported. Specify the URL with the protocol 'http://' or 'https://'.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Sharing can result in numeric mismatch when there are hierarchies inside the subsystems to be shared
Description: HDL code generation might result in numeric mismatch between the original model and the generated model if the following conditions are met: Resource sharing is turned on. There are hierarchies inside the subsystems to be shared.  Note that this bug only affects the generated model and not the generated HDL code.
Workaround: A workaround for this issue is to flatten the hierarchy for all subsystems inside the subsystems to be shared.For example, if you are sharing two subsystems shared_subsystem1?and shared_subsystem2, and both of them contain a subsystem culprit_subsystem. Flatten the hierarchy for culprit_subsystem using these commands:>> hdlset_param('model/DUT/shared_subsystem1/culprit_subsystem', 'FlattenHierarchy', 'on');>> hdlset_param('model/DUT/shared_subsystem2/culprit_subsystem', 'FlattenHierarchy', 'on');Perform HDL code generation after running these lines and there is no longer a numeric mismatch in your validation model.

Summary: JMAAB check for?jc_0627?displays abnormal exit warning for Discrete-Time Integrator blocks
Description: The?Sub-ID b? of??Check usage of Discrete-Time Integrator block? (id:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0627 )? displays abnormal exit warning for Discrete-Time Integrator  blocks when both Upper and Lower saturation limits  are defined by parameter objects whose data types are not auto .
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Concatenating empty variable-sized signals might produce incorrect results
Description: Incorrect code generation and rapid accelerator simulation might occur when Vector Concatenate blocks accept empty variable-sized signals as inputs.See the attached model for an example. The model contains a Vector Concatenate block which accepts a variable-sized signal from an Enabled Subsystem as an input. The block concatenates the variable-sized signal with another signal. When the Enabled subsystem is disabled, it passes an empty output, and as a result the Concatenate block just passes through the other signal. The incorrect generated C code from the example model: ? repro_DW.concatenateBlk_DIMS1 = repro_DW.Enabled_SS._DIMS1[0] + 1;  ? loop_ub = repro_DW.Enabled_SS._DIMS1[0] * repro_DW.Enabled_SS._DIMS1[1] - 1;  ? for (i = 0; i <= loop_ub; i++) { // -------------------> When the output of Enabled Subsystem is empty, this loop is never executed  ? ? tmp_data = repro_B.Enabled_SS.u; // -----------------> This is trying to copy output of Enabled Subsystem to tmp_data  ? }  ? repro_B.concatenateBlk[0] = tmp_data; // --------------> tmp_data is still copied to output of Concatenate block  ? repro_B.concatenateBlk[1] = repro_P.Constant3_Value;  The generated code treats the empty signal as non-empty and incorrectly copies the signal to the output of the Concatenate block.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b

Summary: In Wide Bus Sheets in Parallel Link Designer, SPICEsimulations fail when Standard Load Sims checkbox is selected in SimulationOptions.
Description: In Wide Bus Sheets in Parallel Link Designer, and Standard Load Sims checkbox is selected in Simulation Options. SPICE simulations error with??the following error: Error,   Too many actual parameters in subcircuit "wb1_tsc" (xwb1_tsc_l)
Workaround: Uncheck Standard Load Sims checkbox is in Simulation Options for WideBus sheets

Summary: Changes to workspace variables are not applied to constellation diagram block or plot
Description: Changes to workspace variables that are used to compute Constellation Diagram configuration are not applied to the Constellation Diagram plot output when you rerun the simulation.
Workaround: Update the Constellation Diagram configuration settings directly in the dialogs to ensure the constellation diagram plot output will reflect changes when you rerun the simulation.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for?a model containing a Merge block and a Unit Delay block or signals specified to have a Reusable custom storage class
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets either of the following scenarios: Scenario 1: The model contains a Merge block and a Unit Delay block. The outputs of a set of conditionally executed subsystems are feeding into a Merge block. The output of the Merge block is connected to a Unit Delay block and then fed back to the input(s) of any of the conditionally executed subsystems. The input(s) of the conditionally executed subsystems is/are used in a computation generating an output different from the one connected to the Merge block. The execution of that output happens after the execution of the output connected to the Merge block.  Scenario 2: The model contains a Merge block. The outputs of a set of conditionally executed subsystem are feeding into a Merge block. The output of the Merge block is specified to have a Reusable custom storage class. The input of any of these subsystems is specified to have a Reusable custom storage class with the same variable name as the output of the Merge block. The input(s) of the conditionally executed subsystems is/are used in a computation generating an output different from the one connected to the Merge block. The execution of that output happens after the execution of the output connected to the Merge block.?
Workaround: Connect a Signal Conversion block to the output of the Merge block. In the Signal Conversion block parameters dialog box, select the Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization parameter.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 2, R2023a

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier may incorrectly show?Incompatible for models with arrays of complex numbers as output or input to function calls
Description: Simulink Design Verifier may incorrectly show?Incompatible  for models that meet the following conditions:The model contains arrays of complex numbers? The complex array is either the input or the output of a function call The complex array is not a simple variable?  For example, the following pattern may cause the model to be incorrectly shown as incompatible:? obj.complexArray = functionCall(obj.complexArray);  However, the following pattern will not be shown as incompatible:? complexArray = functionCall(complexArray);
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a

Summary: MATLAB may crash if an invalid CAD File is input to a File Solid block and the Convex Hull?output is selected.
Description: If the?File?Name ?input refers to an invalid or unsupported CAD file, and the Convex Hull  output is used by a Spatial Contact Force block, MATLAB may crash when running the simulation. The File Solid block cannot read CAD drawing or assembly files, and supports the following versions of CAD formats:? ACIS - Up to 2019 Autodesk Inventor - Up to 2020 CATIA V4 - Up to 4.2.5 CATIA V5 - Up to V5-6 R2019 (R29) CATIA V6 - Up to V5-6 R2019 (R29) Creo-Pro/E - Pro/Engineer 19.0 to Creo 6.0 I-deas - Up to 13.x (Nx5), Nx I-deas 6 IGES - 5.1, 5.2, 5.3 NX - V11.0 to NX 12.0, and 1899 Parasolid - Up to v32.0 Revit - 2015 to 2020 Rhino3D - 4, 5, 6 Solid Edge - V19 - 20, ST - ST10, 2020 SolidWorks - From 97 to 2020 STEP - AP 203 E1/E2, AP 214, AP 242?
Workaround: Validate the CAD file in the File Solid block visualization pane prior to running the contact simulation.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Unexpected error may occur during HDL Code Generation for a large model that uses the NCO HDL Optimized block
Description: HDL code generation may error out for a model that uses the NCO HDL Optimized block with frame-based output. This error happens for bigger models like the Wireless HDL Toolbox reference application models. Before R2022a, this block was named NCO HDL Optimized, and was included in the DSP System Toolbox HDL Support library. Starting in R2022a, the block is named NCO and is in the DSP HDL Toolbox library. The error message is:? Error using hdlcoder.pirctx/addZeroProtection  Unexpected Error during HDL Code Generation.  Contact support@mathworks.com  with reproduction? steps. Assertion Failed With Debug Message:  pir_transforms/PropagateInitVal.cpp:205:getInitVal(s) == InitVal::NOT_VISITED?
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Highlighting analysis results may take long time or throw an error for models containing Simulink Function blocks connected in a loop
Description: If a model contains Simulink Function  blocks connected in a loop and if design error detection analysis is performed on one such block, then highlighting analysis results may have one of the following behavior: If highlighting is performed?from the Results Summary  window, then it may take a long time and the text InspectorUtils.extendRangeInfo  may be displayed in the MATLAB Command Window If highlighting is performed using sldvhighlight  API, then the following error may be thrown:Out of memory. The likely cause is an infinite recursion within the program.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MinMax block might produce incorrect results in accelerator and rapid accelerator modes with NaN input and zero-crossing detection
Description: The MinMax block produces incorrect results in accelerator and rapid accelerator modes when the following conditions are true: Model solver is variable-step. MinMax block Enable zero crossing detection  is selected. MinMax block input has continuous sample time. Input to MinMax block has double data type. One of these is true: MinMax block has only one input and the first element of the input is NaN. For example, input [NaN 1] can output NaN, which is an incorrect result. MinMax block has multiple inputs and the first input has a NaN. For example, for a MinMax block with two inputs, the first input has an input of NaN and the second input has a 1, the resulting incorrect result is a NaN.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Excessive array expression hoisting when generating PLC code
Description: Users may encounter statements like the following when generating PLC code for certain models: temp1 := testCycleNum;cycle_U := tb_U[temp1];temp2 := testCycleNum;cycle_Y := tb_Y[temp2];  Here, the temporary variables are unnecessary and yet they are created just so that every array index expression is hoisted.?

Summary: MATLAB might crash when it is launched in non interactive mode using -batch option
Description: When launching MATLAB in non interactive mode using -batch option, MATLAB might sporadically crash.

Summary: IEC Certification Kit may exclude some Simulink Design Verifier validation tests cases due to timeout
Description: The Simulink Design Verifier analysis time might exceed the IEC Certification Kit test execution threshold. In this situation, the IEC Certification Kit?excludes the affected test cases. In the validation test report, it sets the validation status for these tests to: Executed Tests Passed, Unexecuted Tests Need Review .

Summary: ?Import operation in?Mixed-Signal Analyzer?app is highly time consuming.
Description: Importing Cadence??simulation results in the?Mixed-Signal Analyzer ?app takes equal amount of time as generating those data in Cadence??ADE (Analog Design Environment). Currently, whether you import the data in the app by generating a mat file and then load it in the app or directly import the data through Cadence??database, both methods take significant amount of time.

Summary: AUTOSAR Component Quick Start always changes the model system target file to either autosar.tlc or autosar_adaptive.tlc.
Description: When configuring a model using the AUTOSAR Component Quick Start, the system target file automatically changes to the default system target file based on the selected platform once the wizard has finished. The system target file the model was using is replaced with either autosar.tlc or autosar_adaptive.tlc, even if the model target was already AUTOSAR compliant.
Workaround: Manually change the system target file after the AUTOSAR Component Quick Start has finished.

Summary: When using a perpetual license, the License Borrowing UI does not allow you to borrow licenses for more than two days.
Description: When using a perpetual license, the License Borrowing UI does not allow you to borrow licenses for more than two days.
Workaround: Borrow licenses using the command linein MATLAB instead of using the License Borrowing UI. The borrow feature command allowsyou to borrow a feature for a length of time by specifying the returndate.To check out one feature at a time, run the borrow feature command in MATLAB :system_dependent('lmBorrowFeature',?'MATLAB',?'20-feb-2022 23:59')Replace the values 'MATLAB' and '20-feb-2022' with the feature name and the return date for the feature you want to borrow. Feature names are quite specific. To get a list of feature names, run the command? >>system_dependent('lmfeaturelist').The date must be in ˇ®dd-mmm-yyyyˇŻ format, and you must provide a timestamp.This command will throw an error for invalid arguments, for example, incorrect or invalid product names or dates, missing timestamp, or if the user is on an exclude list for borrowing.?

Summary: JMAAB check for jc_0753 does not report Transition Actions not enclosed in curly braces
Description: The?Sub-ID a1 ?of Model Advisor check jc_0753_a1: Check transition actions in Stateflow charts?(id: mathworks.jmaab.jc_0753)? doesn't report warning for Transition Actions  not enclosed within curly braces in C Action Language chart .
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: After loading a System Composer model, this error appears: Uniqueness Violation
Description: When opening or loading a System Composer model, the error: Uniqueness Violation  is shown in the MATLAB command window or in the Diagnostic Viewer. This error can occur under the following conditions: The System Composer model contains at least one component which is a reference to another System Composer model. The referenced System Composer model has been updated by renaming a port at the root of the model or deleting and re-adding a root port with the same name.

Summary: Build Failure in LMS Filter System object and Block when 'DSP Intel AVX2-FMA' Code Replacement Library (CRL) is Usedˇ±
Description: When you use the  ˇ®DSP Intel AVX2-FMA (Windows/Linux/Mac)ˇŻ?code replacement library (CRL) to generate SIMD code from LMS Filter System object? and block for C++ targets, the build process fails with this error:   undefined reference to `MWSPCGlmsnb_S_avx2(float const*, float const*, float, unsigned int*, float*, float*, int, float, int, float*, float*, float*, unsigned char)'  collect2: error: ld returned 1 exit status
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Inlining interface and subcomponents of a reference component in a software architecture may cause MATLAB to crash
Description: In System Composer, inlining a reference component may cause MATLAB to crash?if the following conditions are true: You have a software architecture model that contains a reference component The reference component references another software architecture model? When inlining the reference component, you select the?Interface and subcomponents  as the inlining option.
Workaround: Instead of selecting  Interface and subcomponents as the inlining option, select Interface only and manually copy the contents of the referenced software architecture into the newly created software component.

Summary: Data type of Simulink.Parameter object might change when you tune its value using a dashboard block
Description: When you use a dashboard block to tune the value of a Simulink.Parameter  object in a data dictionary, the DataType  property of the Simulink.Parameter  object changes to 'auto' .?

Summary: Using function syntax hints in a user-defined function can cause an error
Description: If the help text in a user-defined function has paragraphs that begin with tabs instead of being indented with spaces, typing the function name at the Command Window and waiting for the code suggestion causes an error in the Command Window.
Workaround: Replace tabs at the beginning of help text paragraphs with spaces.

Summary: Incorrect Unsatisfiable objectives for MCDC objectives where the overall condition is always false
Description: Simulink Design Verifier might report incorrect Unsatisfiable (or Dead Logic)  objectives for MCDC objectives when the following conditions are met: The MCDC objective is part of a MATLAB System or MATLAB Function block. The overall expression for a || or && operation is always false.?  When the overall expression is?A && B , when this issue is experienced, all MCDC objectives are reported as Unsatisfiable : A && B with A true  A && B with A false  A && B with B true  A && B with B false?  However,  if A can be false, "A && B with A false " should be reported as Satisfiable?  if B can be false (when A can be true), "A && B with B false " should be reported as Satisfiable    On the other hand, when the overall expression is?A || B , when this issue is experienced, all MCDC objectives are reported as?Unsatisfiable : A || B with A true  A?||?B with A false  A?||?B with B true  A?||?B with B false?  However, "A || B with A false " should be reported as?Satisfiable?  "A || B with B false " should be reported as?Satisfiable
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a

Summary: JMAAB check for db_0032 reports incorrect warning for signal lines
Description: The Sub-ID b  of Model Advisor check db_0032_b: Check signal overlaps (id: mathworks.jmaab.db_0032)? displays the following warning for signal lines that do not overlap with other signal lines in the model diagram.Warning :  The following signals overlap with other signals in the diagram.

Summary: Cosmetic misalignment in comparison results for modified model configuration sets or internal test harnesses in published HTML reports
Description: When comparing Simulink models with modified model configuration sets or internal test harnesses, resulting changes in the published HTML report are untidy, with inconsistent alignment. This is easy to notice when it happens and does not affect the interpretation of the results of the comparison.

Summary: Error in computing higher order derivatives with dlfeval/dlgradient when using a convolution layer with 3D image inputs
Description: Computing higher order derivatives of a convolution layer with 3D inputs using dlfeval/dlgradient throws an error. This affects some workflows that require higher order derivatives with 3D images - for example, training a WGAN-GP model on 3D images.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect simulation results in accelerator and rapid accelerator modes for?a model containing a Bus Creator block
Description: Incorrect simulation results are possible in accelerator and rapid accelerator modes for a model that meets these conditions: The model contains a Bus Creator block inside a Conditional or an Enabled subsystem. An input to the Bus Creator block has Inf  sample time.

Summary: Experiment Manager displays incorrect training loss values
Description: When you set the training option OutputNetwork to best-validation-loss , then Experiment Manager returns the trained network with the lowest validation loss, but displays the training loss for the last training iteration. The displayed training loss might not equal the training loss for the network. If you run a Bayesian Optimization experiment to optimize the training loss, then the Experiment Manager passes the incorrect training loss value to the optimization algorithm producing incorrect results.

Summary: FVTool may show incorrect behavior when using the toolstrip or zooming
Description: Users may see incorrect behavior when using the FVTool toolstrip or when trying to zoom in on a display:The toolstrip may show multiple analysis tabs when adding new analysis documents through the Command Window or through the New  button on the toolstrip. Toolstrip buttons may be out of sync when adding legends, changing the grid, or modifying the filter analysis through the Command Window. Zooming in with the mouse may result in the figure displaying incorrect data. Zooming in may drag the axes. Adding multiple analysis documents and then removing the first one may cause FVTool to error out. Figure numbers are not added at all, or are added in an inconsistent way.

Summary: C++ and AUTOSAR code generation may not reflect Shared utilities identifier format setting
Description: The model configuration parameter Shared utilities identifier format , also known asCustomSymbolStrUtil  when configured programmatically, may not be used properly by Embedded Coder to generate code for a model?if the following conditions are true: The model uses autosar.tlc  or system target files derived from it or uses ert.tlc  with the Language  parameter set to C++ . Code was previously?generated for the model and artifacts from that codegeneration still exist. The?CustomSymbolStrUtil ?setting was changed (by using set_param ) since the code was previously generated.
Workaround: Togenerate code correctly for an AUTOSAR model, delete the following artifacts that may have been produced when code was previously generated: The     code generation directory (for example,     <modelName>_autosar_rtw?) The     relevant subdirectory of the slprj directory (for example,     slprj/autosar/<modelName>) The     Simulink cache file for the model (for example, <modelName>.slxc)After you haveremoved the previous code generation artifacts, generate the code again.?To generate code correctly for an ERT C++ model, delete the following artifacts that may have been produced when code was previously generated:The code generation directory (for example,?<modelName>_ert_rtw?)The relevant subdirectory of the slprj directory (for example,?slprj/ert/<modelName>)The Simulink cache file for the model (for example,?<modelName>.slxc)?After you have removed the previous code generation artifacts, generate the code again.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 2, R2022b

Summary: Simulation mismatch between numerical SIL and normal mode for models that use AUTOSAR 4.0 code replacement library for the function Mfx_ConvertP2
Description: Numerical SIL and normal mode simulation results may be different when simulating an AUTOSAR model using an AUTOSAR 4.0 code replacement library that triggers code replacement for the function Mfx_ConvertP2  (for example, a fixed-point conversion from one radix to another).?

Summary: In Signal Editor, Draw Signal context menu stays visible even after clicking Close Draw Signal
Description: Upon closing the Draw?Signal  tab in Signal Editor, the corresponding context menu remains visible in the toolstrip with broken menu actions.

Summary: Root Inport Mapper freezes when saving MAT-file with same name as any variable in From Workspace link dialog box
Description: When importing data through the From Workspace link dialog, the Root Inport Mapper stops responding if the specified MAT-file name matches any of the workspace variable names being imported.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur when a Matrix Concatenate?block is connected to a Model block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect when a model contains a Matrix Concatenate?block connected to a Model block.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block between the?Matrix Concatenate?and the Model block, and select the?Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization?property of the Signal Conversion block.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: The initial condition of an output port block may be ignored if the output port block is inside an inactive variant subsystem block.
Description: An output port block inside an inactive variant subsystem may be removed according to the configuration of the variant subsystem block. This may lead to the loss of initial condition and cause answer mismatch while simulating in normal or rapid accelerator modes as the behavior when the output port block is removed in these two modes could be different according to the setting.?
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 2, R2022b

Summary: An error during HDL code generation might occur for models with code reuse and clock-rate pipelining on
Description: When generating HDL code from a Simulink model that has code reuse on and uses clock-rate pipelining, an error during code generation could occur that looks like this:? Error using hdlcoder.pirctx/reduceMatchingDelays  Unexpected Error during HDL Code Generation? Assertion Failed With Debug Message:?pir_transforms/MatchingDelayReducer.cpp:341:n->getNumInstances() == 1??
Workaround: Use the?hdlset_param?function to disable ReduceMatchingDelays.

Summary: HDL IP core generation using Xilinx Vivado fails as of January 1, 2022
Description: Since January 1, 2022, IP core generation fails when the synthesis tool is set to Xilinx Vivado. The following error message can be observed in the HDL Workflow Advisor Task 3.2:? Failed Task "Vivado IP Packager" unsuccessful. See log for details. Generated logfile: hdl_prj\hdlsrc\<myModel> \workflow_task_VivadoIPPackager.log  The log file at \hdl_prj\hdlsrc\<myModel>\workflow_task_VivadoIPPackager.log  reports an error message similar to the following: bad lexical cast: source type value could not be interpreted as target  ? ?while executing"rdi::set_property core_revision 2201031458 {component component_1}"  ? ?invoked from within  "set_property core_revision 2201031458 [ipx::current_core]"  ? ?(file "vivado_ip_package.tcl" line 57)
Workaround: Installation instructions  Log into an account with the appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps. On Mac systems, the folder name will have a .app extension, e.g. /Applications/MATLAB_R20XXa.app  Exit MATLAB.Locate the following file under your <MATLAB> folder and rename the file to "IPEmitterVivado_backup.p":<MATLAB>\toolbox\hdlcoder\hdlcommon\+hdlturnkey\+ip\IPEmitterVivado.pWindows Systems:?You might be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when renaming this file.  Download and unzip the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report.In the unzipped folder, navigate to the subfolder that matches the release you are using. For example, if you are using the R2021b release, open the R2021b subfolder.If you do not see a subfolder that matches the release you are using,?contact MathWorks Technical Support.In this folder, there is a newer version of the?IPEmitterVivado.p file. Copy this file and paste it in the location mentioned above.Windows Systems:?You might be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when pasting this file.Restart MATLAB.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Aggregating coverage data for model and subsystem harnesses might error
Description: Performing unit-to-system test coverage aggregation might error if all the following conditions are true:  You have a test case for the top-level model. You have test cases for 2 or more subsystem harnesses. One of the subsystem harness test cases includes a change that causes coverage data to not be compatible for aggregation with the top-level model. An example of such a change is altering a variant control or changing a signal datatype  When you run your test cases using Simulink Test Manager, MATLAB shows an error similar to: Operands to the logical AND (&&) and OR (||) operators must be convertible to logical scalar values.  Use the ANY or ALL functions to reduce operands to logical scalar values.   Alternatively, you might see a warning such as:   Warning: Object 239 is not in the list of objects to be saved => saved as zero.   > In SlCov. CoverageAPI. saveCoverage>save_a_model (line 384)   In SlCov. CoverageAPI. saveCoverage (line 135)   In cvsave (line 19)

Summary: Error occurs when closing an App Designer app while execution is paused due to a call to uiwait in the startupFcn for the app
Description: The below error occurs when closing an app that has the HandleVisiblity  property of the figure set to 'callback' ?when execution is paused due to a call to uiwait  in the app's startupFcn . This is likely to be the case for multiwindow apps where the primary app launches a secondary app that calls?uiwait  in its?startupFcn .Warning: The following error was caught while executing 'onCleanup' class destructor:  Error using matlab. ui. Figure/ setInvalid or deleted object.   Error in appdesigner. internal. service. AppManagementService>@()set(fig, 'HandleVisibility', 'callback') (line 150)?c = onCleanup(@()set(fig,  'HandleVisibility',  'callback'));  Error in onCleanup/ delete (line 80) obj. task();
Workaround: There are two workarounds:In the app with the call to?uiwait?in the?startupFcn, change the HandleVisiblity property of the figure to 'on'.?If it is necessary to keep the HandleVisiblity?of the figure?set to 'callback', instead, in the app with the call to uiwait in the startupFcn do the following:Add a CloseRequestFcn callback to the figure.In the function body of the CloseRequestFcn callback, replace the call to delete(app) with uiresume(app.UIFigure).In the primary app, when calling the secondary app, get a handle to the secondary app and delete the secondary app on the next line.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: MATLAB crash at startup caused by an invalid preferences settings file
Description: MATLAB can crash at startup if there is an invalid settings file in the user's preferences folder.
Workaround: Remove the contents of the user's preferences folder.

Summary: MATLAB crashes during model update with Variant Connector, Simscape Bus and Variant Subsystem block with connection port
Description: MATLAB crashes during model update phase when all belowconditions are met: Model contains Simscape Bus  block and VariantSubsystem  with connection port. Model contains Variant Connector  block or PropagateConditions outside of Variant Subsystem  is on for Variant Subsystem . In the?Variant Subsystem  block, portsname on choice block and its corresponding port name on variant subsystem blockdiffers in whitespaces only.  Sample crash snippet : [? 0]0x00007f51b47e1eca sltopo::XformableTopo::adjacentPorts(slPort const*) const[? 1]0x00007f51b193e507 sltopo::adjacentPortsOf(slPort const*, sltopo::XformableTopoconst [? 2]0x00007f51b193e878 HierStringPropagator::PropagateHierStringFromBlk(SLBlockconst*)?
Workaround: Configure the Variant Subsystem such that the name of the ports on choice block matches the corresponding port name on variant subsystem block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: When generating HDL code using the "MATLAB Datapath" architecture of a?MATLAB function block, HDL Coder can produce incorrect code for logical indexing expressions used in size identification functions
Description: When generating HDL code using the "MATLAB Datapath" architecture of a?MATLAB function block, HDL Coder can produce incorrect code for logical indexing expressions. A logical indexing expression used inside of a size identification function can result in incorrect code generation. Some examples of size identification functions are?length ,?numel , and?size .  For example, this expression produces incorrect generated code: numel(x(x>10))

Summary: The gradCAM function returns different output when you specify class labels individually or in an array
Description: If you call gradCAM   with an array of labels, the function rescales the maps across all labels to the range [0, 1]. Therefore, the gradCAM   function can return different maps when you call it with an array of labels compared to when you call it? individually for each label. net = squeezenet;?  X = imresize(imread("sherlock.jpg"),[227 227]);?  labels = ["Labrador retriever","golden retriever"];?  mapBothLabels = gradCAM(net,X,labels);  mapFirstLabel = gradCAM(net,X,labels(1));  % Compare the maps for the first label.  isequal(mapBothLabels(:,:,1),mapFirstLabel)  The rescaling of the map to the range [0, 1] is unnecessary and not consistent with the published Grad-CAM algorithm.?

Summary: Generated HDL code for RS Decoder block includes a combinational loop
Description: While running HDL synthesis for the RS Decoder block, the synthesis tool returns combinational loop warnings. This warning results in incorrect timing analysis. For reliable behavior on hardware, this condition should be avoided in HDL designs.

Summary: Performance regression in access, link, and fieldOfView functions when using satellite scenario
Description: A performance regression was introduced in the access, link, and fieldOfView functions. The regression is noticeable when calling these functions repeatedly in a loop, as demonstrated by the following code: % Create a satellite scenario object  sc = satelliteScenario;  % Add 16 satellites  sat = satellite(sc,ones(1,16)*10000000,zeros(1,16),ones(1,16)*80,[0:15]*22.5,zeros(1,16),repmat([45 135 225 315],1,4));  % Time the creation of 16 satellites?  tic  for idx = 1:16  ? ?for idx2 = idx:16  ? ? ? ?access(sat(idx),sat(idx2));  ? ?end  end  toc ?

Summary: Group-specific variants and doses are not included when copying a Fit Data program in SimBiology Model Analyzer
Description: If you create a copy of a Fit Data  program, the group-specific variants and doses specified in the original program are not copied over.

Summary: Distributed pipelining might not preserve design delays in certain cases even if PreserveDesignDelays is enabled
Description: PreserveDesignDelays   is an optimization option that prevents?DistributedPipelining   from moving design delays. However in certain cases DistributedPipelining   ignores?PreserveDesignDelays   and moves design delays.?  This occurs when:  Design delays are placed directly before the output ports of subsystems that have?DistributedPipelining  ?enabled. Design delays are placed directly after the input ports of subsystems that have?DistributedPipelining  ?enabled.
Workaround: Identify the delay/port pairs described above that causes this bug.Place a zero-latency, wiring block, such as data-type conversion block, between the delay and the port, so that they are not directly adjacent to each other.

Summary: Simulink Edit time - 'From' Block has a 'warning' highlight when referring to 'Goto' Block inside a variant subsystem library link
Description: Given a Simulink model with Edit-time violations enabled, with the following conditions, ? 1) Top-level From and Goto  blocks in global scope ? 2) A variantsubsystem, where each subsystem contains a library link ? 3) Each library linkcontains nested From  and Goto  blocks which refer to the corresponding top-levelblocks in global scope? The top-level ˇ°Fromˇ± block will be highlighted in orangewith the warning message "No Matching Goto block". Running thesimulation will not produce warning or error messages in the Diagnostic Viewer.

Summary: HDL Workflow Advisor errors out when the input port of AXI4-Stream Slave is mapped to a vector and the output port of AXI4-Stream Master is mapped to a scalar.
Description: HDL Workflow Advisor errors out?in the task?Set Target Interface  when?following conditions are true: Input port of the AXI4-Stream Slave interface is mapped to a vector. Input port Interface options  are set as 'Sample packing dimension  to?All ', 'Packing mode ?to?Bit Aligned ' . Output port of the AXI4-Stream Master interface?is mapped to a scalar.  ? ?The following error message?is generated in the workflow advisor log :  "Unrecognized function or variable 'InterfaceOptionsOutp'" ??

Summary: Incorrect error during code generation of Variant Subsystem with startup variant activation time
Description: Variantblocks (Variant Subsystem , Variant Source , Variant Sink ) in startup variantactivation time with variant control expressions containing TRUE /FALSE  keywordsthrows the below error message during code generationUnexpected or internal error encountered in "in CGIR: B:\matlab\src\cg_ir\export\include\cg_ir\tag\VariantIfExprToSLSymbolicExprTag.hpp line 61: In getSymbolicExprId: A if expr without tag found!

Summary: Aerospace 3DOF body axes blocks impose positive initial velocity
Description: The Aerospace Blockset 3DOF equations of motion blocks require that the?Initial Velocity ?parameter be positive for the body axes (i.e. 3DOF (Body Axes), Simple Variable Mass 3DOF (Body Axes), and Custom Variable Mass 3DOF (Body Axes)) blocks.?
Workaround: Try changing the inertial frame of reference of the block into one for which the initial velocity is positive.?

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Matrix gain (K*u) operations with scalar output might produce simulation mismatch between the original model and generated model
Description: HDL code generation for a Simulink model might result in a numeric mismatch between the original and generated model when all the following conditions are met: Operations in a Simulink model involve performing the matrix gain (K*u) operation and the result type is scalar. The gain factor K is a tunable parameter.
Workaround: Convert the matrix gain value K to a product component with the first input connected to a constant. Then set the constant block value to the gain factor K.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Inconsistent simulation and code generation results when input to log or log10 is negative
Description: When a Simulink model contains computation of log  or log10   and the input to this computation is negative, The result of this computation is likely NaN  in Normal mode, and The result of this computation might be -inf  in Accelerator mode, Rapid Accelerator mode, and generated code.  This computation of log  or log10 might come directly from a Math Function block, or indirectly from other logic that computes   log  or log10.     This issue affects Simulink, Simulink Coder, and Embedded Coder.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Signal Multiresolution Analyzer does not update frequencies for all decompositions when Time is changed
Description: The Level Selection ?pane in the Signal Multiresolution Analyzer  app has a column that shows the frequency bands of the selected decomposition. If you change Time, the app only updates the frequency bands of the selected decomposition. The app does not update the frequency bands of the other decompositions. For example, suppose you have MODWT and EMD decompositions in the Decomposed Signals ?pane and are working in samples. If you select the MODWT decomposition and specify a sample rate, the app updates the frequency bands in the Level Selection  pane and expresses them in hertz. If you then select the EMD decomposition, the app updates the frequency units but not the frequency values. The reported values are still in cycles/sample.
Workaround: Select the decomposition, then change and set Time again. The app will update the Level Selection pane with the correct frequencies.

Summary: Assertion failure when generating code with incompatible variable sized matrices and?EnableImplicitExpansion?is turned off
Description: In rare cases, when generating code, MATLAB Coder may produce an assertion failure for dll/lib/exe  targets, if the MATLAB program contains the coding pattern satisfying the following conditions: Binary operation with one variable-sized operand, for example, A - B where B is variable-sized. Size of operand B is known at compile-time. Sizes of A and B are not compatible. EnableImplicitExpansion ?is turned off.  The assertion error contains the following text: Assertion at B:\matlab\src\cgir_support\dom_core\analysis\range\RangeValueMath.cpp line 230: In x_binary: Assertion failed: aLeftRange.isScalar() || aRightRange.isScalar() || aLeftRange.getNumberOfValues() == aRightRange.getNumberOfValues()  For an example of the issue, please see the attached example.

Summary: MATLAB may crash if invalid inputs are given to the Grid Surface block?
Description: If the input?X Grid Vector  or Y Grid Vector ?parameter is invalid, then it could cause the application to terminate when running the simulation. For example, the input?X Grid Vector  parameter?is invalid if it contains Inf  or NaN  entries, or if number of elements in the vector is not equal to the number of rows in the?matrix to?Z Heights  parameter.
Workaround: You can use the?griddedInterpolant?function to check the validity of inputs to the?X Grid Vector,?Y Grid Vector, and Z Height parameters. For example:F = griddedInterpolant({xGridVector, yGridVector}, zHeights)If the inputs are invalid, the griddedInterpolant?function issues an error message that highlights the problem.Passing valid data to the Grid Surface block will avoid the issue.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: C++ code generation might produce an incorrect initial value setting for a root outport when using function prototype control
Description: C++ code generation might generate an incorrect initial value for a root outport for a model that meets these conditions: Uses function prototype control Propagates a non-zero constant sample time to a root outport
Workaround: Do not use function prototype control on models where an outport has a constant sample time.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when inspecting component ports during simulation
Description: When simulating or debugging a System Composer model, MATLAB might crash when inspecting a component port using the Property Inspector.

Summary: Math Function block with reciprocal functiongenerates an incorrect HDL code for the?fixed-point inputs that have fractionallength greater than word length
Description: The generated HDL code for a Simulink model might be incorrect when the following settings are applied: The model contains a Math Function?block with reciprocal? function  Architecture is s et to? Math  in the HDL block property for Math Function Reciprocal block  The inputs to the block are fixed-point type with fractional length greater than word length?, such as? sfix8_E13 numerictype(0, 8, 13)  Output data type  parameter is set as other than 'Inherit: Inherit via internal rule'   The incorrect HDL code can result in simulation failures in ModelSim when verifying the generated code.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: During fast restart or runtime update diagram, Simulink reports an error when replacing a Simulink.Signal object with another signal object with the same property values
Description: When performing a fast restart simulation or an update diagram during runtime, Simulink reports an error when a Simulink.Signal  object is replaced with another one with the same property values. The reported error is similar to:The Simulink signal object 'SignalObject1' for output port 1 of block 'model/testBlock' was changed during simulation.
Workaround: If you use initialization scripts before every fast restart simulation run, consider these alternatives:Separate out the Simulink.Signal objects into a separate MAT-file and load them only once before the first simulation run.Write a wrapper around the Simulink.Signal object creation function that can check for equivalent signal objects already in the workspace and not replace them.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a

Summary: fscchi2 and fsrftest might?cause MATLAB to crashwhen data that is neither double nor single is passed
Description: The functions fscchi2  and fsrftest  currentlysupport only double and single types. They do not support other types such assparse, complex, integer, but do not check for them. Passing X data of any ofthese unsupported types might cause MATLAB to crash.
Workaround: Convert the data toeither double or single before passing it to fscchi2?orfsrftest.

Summary: Polyspace Bug Finder might error during the Compilation part with the message: The process "ps_cxx_fe" received the signal 11
Description: If you run a Polyspace Bug Finder analysis after activating the MOVE_OPERATION_USES_COPY Checker, you might encounter an error during the?Compilation ?part of the analysis. The error message in the log file is?Error: The process "ps_cxx_fe" received the signal 11 . The error does not mean that the entire analysis failed. Only the compiled files having this error do not produce results. You can still see the Polyspace Bug Finder results for all the other compiled files.
Workaround: Deactivate the MOVE_OPERATION_USES_COPY Checker.

Summary: SimBiology projects saved with an empty plot between R2019b and R2020b do not load in later releases
Description: SimBiology projects saved with empty plots between R2019b and R2020b might error when you load them in later releases.
Workaround: Open the project in the release it was created in, delete any empty plots, and resave the project.

Summary: Warning when loading pretrained ivectorSystem?while using speakerRecognition
Description: When you attempt to download and use the pretrained ivectorSystem ?while using speakerRecognition , the following unnecessary warning appears: Warning: While loading an object of class 'audioFeatureExtractor':      A field named 'pFeaturesToExtract' doesn't exist.   You can ignore this warning.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Initialization code might be incorrect for?a model containing a block that has an output with non-zero initial conditions
Description: The generated code in the initialization function might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a block or a subsystem that has an output with non-zero initial conditions. The model also contains a Bus Creator block inside a Conditional or Enabled subsystem. The output with non-zero initial conditions is connected to the input of the Bus Creator block.

Summary: Code generation might fail for C Caller or C Function blocks with input argument of type const char*
Description: Code generation might fail for a model that contains a C Caller block or a C Function block with input argument of type const char*.  Code generation fails? with the assertion:CGIRassertion 'In handleCastExpr: unhandled CastExpr
Workaround: Change the type of the function argument from const char* to char*.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when calling Simulink.findVars?function in STF_make_rtw_hook file?during code generation
Description: Generating code using a customized build process that executes the Simulink.findVars  function in a?STF_make_rtw_hook ?file causes MATLAB to crash.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code might return incorrect value when querying the port indices?of?maxPooling2dLayer
Description: Suppose that you generate code for a deep learning network that contains the?maxPooling2dLayer ?for MKL-DNN, cuDNN, or TensorRT target. In such situations, the generated code might return? incorrect values when querying the port?indices ?of the ?maxPooling2dLayer .

Summary: Running multiple test cases with iterations in Fast Restart throws errors.
Description: Running test cases that use the same model but different test harnesses errors when using Fast Restart with iterations.

Summary: Polyspace compilation issues when using compiler armclang
Description: If you run an analysis using compiler armclang  in Polyspace version R2021b, that analysis might fail to compile if your project uses CMSIS headers. You see an error message similar to this: error: #error directive: "ACLE intrinsics support not enabled."| #error "ACLE intrinsics support not enabled."|? ^  The issue occurs because these macros are not defined in Polyspace R2021b:? __ARM_ACLE:  Defines the ARM C Language Extensions (ACLE) level used by the compiler. ARM headers do not compiler if this macro is undefined. __ARM_ARCH : Specifies the version of the Arm instruction set architecture. __ARM_ARCH_PROFILE : Specifies the profile of the target architecture.
Workaround: Use option -D to manually define the macros:-D __ARM_ACLE=200 -D __ARM_ARCH=7?-D __ARM_ARCH_PROFILE='M'

Summary: Boolean type custom attributes are imported from DOORS Next as enumerated type
Description: When importing requirements from IBM DOORS Next to Requirements Toolbox, requirements with custom attributes of boolean data type are imported as enumerated data type instead. This is viewed as a combo boxbased custom attribute instead of a checkbox based custom attribute.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model containing an Enabled subsystem that has an Discrete-Time Integrator block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets these conditions:The model contains an Enabled subsystem. The Enable port?block has the?States when enabling ?parameter set to?reset.  The subsystem contains an Discrete-Time Integrator block that has the Initial condition source  parameter set to external .  A Constant block serves as the external initial condition source of the Discrete-Time Integrator block.?
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 7, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Linearization of Simscape model can return NaN
Description: Simscape models involving modecharts or events may produce NaN for the linearization if the model has a conditionally high differential index.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: When training data is a single cell array with multiple observations, Experiment Manager fails to generate a confusion matrix
Description: When you use a single cell array with multiple observations as training data, Experiment Manager trains the network correctly, but fails to generate a confusion matrix.

Summary: Sub-check a?of?JMAAB check for db_0146?incorrectly flags blocks inside linked-library blocks
Description: Model Advisor?check Check position of conditional blocks and iterator blocks  ?(ID :?mathworks.jmaab.db_0146) ?sub-check  a ? incorrectly flags blocks inside linked-library blocks.

Summary: PickAndPlaceWorkflowInGazeboUsingPointCloudProcessingExample live script stalls
Description: The example?PickAndPlaceWorkflowInGazeboUsingPointCloudProcessingExample ?live script stalls on line 15 and the planner never finishes planning to "scanning pose2". The following diagnostic message is seen in the live script before it stalls: line 14??% Task 1: Build world  line 15 exampleCommandBuildWorldROSGazeboScene(coordinator);?        Moving to scanning pose 1        Now planning...        Waiting until robot reaches the desired configuration        Capturing point cloud 1        Getting camera pose 1        Moving to scanning pose 2        Now planning...         Ultimately, the planner reaches MaxIterations  and the following error is seen:  Error using shorten  Expected path to be nonempty.    Error in robotics.internal.validation.validateNumericMatrix (line 21)  validateattributes(M, {'single','double'}, {'nonempty','real','2d',varargin{:}}, ...  Error in manipulatorRRT/shorten (line 178)  ? ? ? ? ? ? robotics.internal.validation.validateNumericMatrix(path, ...  Error in exampleCommandMoveToTaskConfigROSGazeboScene (line 58)  ? ? ? ? shortenedPath = shorten(planner, path, 15);  Error in exampleCommandBuildWorldROSGazeboScene (line 26)  ? ? ? ? exampleCommandMoveToTaskConfigROSGazeboScene(coordinator, ...

Summary: Software Quality Objectives report shows incorrect PASS/FAIL statuses
Description: When you generate a Software Quality Objectives (SQO) report from results that are stored in Polyspace Access, the report might show incorrect PASS/FAIL statuses compared to those shown in the Polyspace Access interface. This might occur when you define multiple custom SQO definitions in Polyspace Access.

Summary: The JMAAB check for jc_0501?reports warning for comments
Description: The Model Advisor check?Check entry formatting in State blocks in Stateflow charts (id: mathworks.jmaab.jc_0501)  incorrectly ? reports a warning for C comments in Stateflow Charts.

Summary: Out-of-memory error for two-output call to svd in some cases
Description: For an m -by-n  matrix X  with n >> m , it is possible for these svd  calls to throw an out-of-memory error: [U,S] = svd(X);[U,S] = svd(X,'econ');[U,S] = svd(X,0); The error occurs in cases where it is not possible to allocate a temporary matrix of size n -by-n .
Workaround: Use the command[U,S,~] = svd(X,'econ');instead, padding the matrix S with zeros if necessary.

Summary: Possible simulation failure when a flexible body with three or more frames is part of a kinematic loop.
Description: When a flexible body has three or more frames, and that flexible body is part of a kinematic loop, the following error may be thrown during the simulation: Kinematic constraints cannot be maintained. Check solver type and consistency tolerance in the Simscape Solver Configuration block. Check Simulink solver type and tolerances in Model Configuration Parameters. A kinematic singularity might be the source of this problem.  This can occur when there is in fact no constraint violation.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Simulink simulations run in a parallel pool with the Bit Error Rate Analysis app can result in an unexpectedly high BER
Description: The Bit Error Rate Analysis  app can run Simulink simulations in parallel when Acceleration > Parallel pool  is set to Auto  and a Parallel Computing Toolbox license is available.
