When running in this parallel simulation configuration, the app results in a constant bit error rate (BER) of approximately 40%.

Summary: Generating code for a subsystem that takes input from a muxed source might cause invalid index error
Description: When you generate code for a model that contains a subsystem that takes input from a muxed source (for example, a Mux block or a virtual Bus Creator block), the code generator might throw an error like the following: Internal error while creating code interface description file: codeInfo.mat. Aborting code generation.     Caused by:     Invalid index.?
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: UTF-8 characters outside of ASCII range emitted as unexpected characters
Description: The presence of non-ASCII characters in a null terminated string causes unexpected characters to be generated?in MATLAB Code: function x = foo()   x = [char(124) char(125) char(126) char(127) char(128) char(0)]; % <------- char(128) is non-ASCIIend>> codegen foo  The code generator throws this warning:foo.c:18:31: warning: initializer-string for array of 'char' is too long? and produces this code in foo.c :static const char_T cv[6] = "|}~?";??// <-------- notice the unexpected character in generated code
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 2, R2022b

Summary: MATLAB might throw an error when applying a coverage filter
Description: When you apply a coverage filter in the Coverage Results Explorer, you might get an error that looks like:  Error occurred when evaluating apply callback.   This error can happen for models containing charts, subsystems, or custom library blocks.

Summary: Some gas blocks do not correctly update parameter visibility
Description: The Variable Orifice ISO 6358 (G) and Pressure ReducingValve (G) blocks do not correctly update parameter visibility. This occurs for all settings of Valve parameterization  for the? Variable Orifice ISO 6358 (G) block. For the Pressure Reducing Valve (G) block, this occurs when you set?Valve parameterization? to?Cv coefficient (USCS) ,?Kv coefficient (SI) , or?Restriction area ?and switch?Opening parameterization  between?Linear ?and?Tabulated data .
Workaround: To correctly update the parameters, find and execute the command that matches your settings.?If you set the valve or orifice parameterization?to?Cv coefficient (USCS)?and would like to set?Opening parameterization?toLinear:?>> set_param(gcb, ˇ®iso_opening_spec_CvˇŻ, ˇ®fluids.gas.enum.iso_opening_spec.linearˇŻ);Tabulated data:?>> set_param(gcb, ˇ®iso_opening_spec_CvˇŻ, ˇ®fluids.gas.enum.iso_opening_spec.tabulatedˇŻ);If you set the valve or orifice?parameterization??to?Kv coefficient (SI)?and would like to set?Opening parameterization?to?Linear:?>> set_param(gcb, ˇ®iso_opening_spec_KvˇŻ, ˇ®fluids.gas.enum.iso_opening_spec.linearˇŻ);Tabulated data:?>> set_param(gcb, ˇ®iso_opening_spec_KvˇŻ, ˇ®fluids.gas.enum.iso_opening_spec.tabulatedˇŻ);If you set the valve or orifice?parameterization?to Restriction area?and would like to set?Opening parameterization?toLinear:?>> set_param(gcb, ˇ®iso_opening_spec_areaˇŻ, ˇ®fluids.gas.enum.iso_opening_spec.linearˇŻ);Tabulated data:?>> set_param(gcb, ˇ®iso_opening_spec_areaˇŻ, ˇ®fluids.gas.enum.iso_opening_spec.tabulatedˇŻ);

Summary: Incorrect simulation results in rapid accelerator mode for?a model containing a Function-Call Subsystem block
Description: Incorrect simulation results are possible in rapid accelerator mode for a model that meets these conditions: The model contains a Function-Call Subsystem block. The Function-Call Subsystem has?an inplace specification (that is, the same input and output variable).
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Change of layers when constructing a dlnetwork with both a ConcatenationLayer?and DepthConcatenationLayer objects
Description: When you create a?dlnetwork?object from a layer graph containing?  both ConcatenationLayer ?and DepthConcatenationLayer ?objects, then the software returns a network containing only ConcatenationLayer ?objects or only? DepthConcatenationLayer ?objects. This does not affect the underlying behavior of the network when passing data through the network. Code that depends on the layer class names can lead to unexpected results.
Workaround: When you create a dlnetwork object, replace instances of depthConcatenationLayer with?concatenationLayer and set the concatenation dimension to 3.

Summary: MATLAB Parallel Server jobs on Windows HPC Pack 2019 frequently appear failed on completion
Description: Jobs submitted using Parallel Computing Toolbox to a MATLAB Parallel Server cluster running Windows HPC Pack 2019 will often appear to have failed at completion. This might happen for every job or only for some jobs. The job and tasks will have state 'failed'  in both MATLAB and in the HPC Cluster Manager, even if the submitted work has completed successfully. Any outputs are still available via the OutputArguments  property of the Task  object.
Workaround: To fetch the outputs of a job that is failed:outputs = [job.Tasks.OutputArguments].';

Summary: HDL Coder generates an internal assertion when Flatten Hierarchy is turned On for the Filter blocks.
Description: HDL Coder generates the following internal assertion when you turned On?Flatten Hierarchy ?for the Filter blocks. Compilation errors when generating code for: <Block_path> ? Error during MATLAB code compilation: Error Path: B:\matlab\src\cgir_hdl\emlinterface\EMLInterfaceForHDL.cpp:407 Error Location: (@Row: --> 15 @Column: --> 36) Undefined function or variable '<variable_name>' . The first assignment to a local variable determines its class.?
Workaround: Consider disabling Flatten Hierarchy. To disable hierarchy flattening by using the HDL Block Properties dialog box:Right-click the subsystem.Select?HDL Code?>?HDL Block Properties.Turn Off?Flatten Hierarchy block property.?

Summary: execute function returns uninformative error message when multiple artifacts are dirty
Description: If you collect metrics programmatically using a metric.Engine  object, the execute  function returns an uninformative error message when you have multiple artifacts with unsaved changes.
Workaround: Save all open models, test cases, requirements, and requirement link files.

Summary: jc_0732?check does not?report Stateflow event names that match state names
Description: The Model Advisor check?Check uniqueness of Stateflow State and Data names ?(id: mathworks.jmaab.jc_0732)  does not? report Stateflow event names that match state names.?

Summary: Code generation report fails to generate
Description: The code generation report does not generate correctly when the configuration parameter 'CheckMdlBeforeBuild'  is set to Warning  or Error . This error can cause SIL/PIL simulation failure.
Workaround: Set the configuration parameter?'CheckMdlBeforeBuild'?to 'Off'.

Summary: Model with custom code or simulated in SIL or PIL mode shows incorrect decision coverage in specific cases
Description: If your model contains custom C/C++ code, for instance, in an S-Function or C Caller block, the coverage results might show fewer number of decisions than you expect if the?Structural coverage level ?is set to?Decision  and?the Relational boundary  option is disabled. You might see the same issue when you collect coverage results after a simulation in SIL or PIL mode (which uses code generated from the model).  The reason for fewer decisions is that certain types of code constructs are not instrumented for decision coverage till you select a higher?Structural coverage level ?such as?Condition Decision ?or?Modified Condition Decision Coverage ?(or enable relational boundary coverage).
Workaround: Set the Structural coverage level to Condition Decision or Modified Condition Decision Coverage (MCDC), or enable the Relational Boundary option.?

Summary: Incorrect normal-mode simulation results when a Variable Selector block is in a model that is referenced multiple times
Description: Simulating a model hierarchy in normal mode might produce incorrect simulation results when all these conditions apply: The model hierarchy contains multiple Model blocks that reference the same model. These Model blocks are configured to simulate the referenced model in normal mode. The referenced model contains a Variable Selector block.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: jc_0802?check not reporting comparison with literals
Description: If you use MATLAB Action Language in your Stateflow chart, t he Model Advisor check?Check for implicit type casting in Stateflow ?(id: mathworks.jmaab.jc_0802)? does not report ?comparing a variable and a literal value which are of different data type.? ?

Summary: JMAAB check for?jc_0611?does not report warning for Product blocks?with?multiplication?operators
Description: Model Advisor check Check signs of input signals in product blocks ?(id:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0611)? does not report warning for Product  blocks with multiplication  operators. Also, it reports incorrect warning for Product  blocks whose input signal data types are not Fixed-point?  data types.  Warning :  The following product blocks have input signals with different sign bits.? This warning message is incorrect as the input data types are not?Fixed-point?data types.?

Summary: The JMAAB check for?jc_0731?does not report use of slash in?Stateflow state name
Description: The Model Advisor check?Check usage of State names ?(id: mathworks.jmaab.jc_0731) ? does not report state names in Stateflow that contain a slash (/) character.?

Summary: Root-level model compilation fails when a model with a Function-Call Feedback Latch block is referenced multiple times
Description: For root-level model that references multiple instances of another model that contains a Function-Call Feedback Latch block, the following error occurs when the root-level model compiles: The model <model name> has changed between the time when two instances of this model were compiled. This is not allowed for models referenced in Normal Mode.?   This error occurs because the name of an underlying subsystem of the Function-Call Feedback Latch block changes at every compilation.?

Summary: Simulink Test Manager incorrectly reports test failure when using verify keyword
Description: When you use verify signals only and no logged signals, Simulink Test Manager incorrectly reports a test failure regardless of the verify  outcome.
Workaround: To work around this issue, log an output signal in the model.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a

Summary: MATLAB might stall during model update?with Variant Connector and Simscape Bus block
Description: MATLAB might stall during model update phase Compiling: refreshing bus cache : Completed when allbelow conditions are met: Model contains Variant Connector  and Simscape Bus  block. Simscape Bus  block have more than one port onits children (elements) side and all ports on children side connected together (makingloop).

Summary: Simulink model configured with concurrent execution and explicit task partitioning fails to generate ROS node
Description: When the explicit task partitioning is done using models referenced with Model   blocks, following error occurs during code-generation: Function TAR tried to add two files as ˇ°rtw_linux.hˇ±  When the explicit task partitioning is done using Subsystem  blocks, following error occurs during code compilation: ?error:?ˇ®using element_type = class <model name>ModelClass {aka class <model name>ModelClass}ˇŻ has no member named ˇ®stepˇŻ  ? mModel->step();

Summary: Hyperlinks created in MATLAB R2021b that link to a location within the same document do not work in a previous version of MATLAB
Description: If a hyperlink that links to a location within the same document is inserted into a live script in MATLAB R2021b, the hyperlink does not work when the live script is opened in a previous version of MATLAB, for example, R2021a. When the hyperlink is clicked, the cursor does not move. If the live script is opened in MATLAB versions R2021b and later, the hyperlink works correctly.

Summary: JMAAB check for?db_0129?does not report?transition lines that are diagonally oriented
Description: The?Sub-ID d? of Model Advisor check?Check for Stateflow transition appearance ?(db_0129_d: Check orientation of transition lines)? does not report warning for?transition lines that are diagonally oriented and are not part of flowchart loops.

Summary: Incorrect warning message when merging coverage results in the Model Testing Dashboard
Description: When the Model Testing Dashboard merges coverage results for test cases that contains multiple iterations, the dashboard shows a warning message with an incorrect number of test case results. For example: 5 out of 4 results for 'model_name' have been dropped due to merged conflicts. Make sure all results originate from?the same model and tool version.

Summary: Local clusters are not supported in MATLAB Web App Server
Description: When you use the 'local' cluster from the Parallel Computing Toolbox in web applications deployed to MATLAB Web App Server, you see the error: 'Local' clusters are not supported in MATLAB Online.

Summary: The JMAAB check for jc_0802 does not analyze Simulink Function inside Stateflow Chart
Description: The Model Advisor check??Check for implicit type casting in Stateflow ( id : mathworks.jmaab.jc_0802)  does not analyze the Simulink Functions  inside Stateflow Chart .
Fixed In: R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Loading the initial state in rapid accelerator for a model may error when a virtual bus only exists in the referenced model
Description: Loading the initial state in rapid accelerator for models with a virtual bus only in the referenced model will error with the following: ?"Data type mismatch in the initial state for model"   This issue is only observed when all of the following conditions are met: The 'SaveFormat' is 'Dataset' The initial state being loaded is in Dataset format A virtual bus only exists in the referenced model

Summary: Quickly  navigating in and out of MATLAB Function blocks?and Stateflow MATLAB functions may cause disappearance of function body
Description: When you enter and exit the editor used by MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB functions in Stateflow charts before the editor fully loads, the function body might disappear. ?
Workaround: Before navigating out of the editor, wait until it fully loads.The behavior has been improved in R2022a and R2021b Update 3, and the bug is expected to occur less frequently in those releases.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Designfiles that load stateful DAG networks with non-initial states might generateincorrect code
Description: The code generator might produce incorrect code if your design file satisfies all  of these conditions: In the design file, you load a stateful DAGNetwork ?object from a MAT-file by using the? coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork  function The loaded stateful DAG network ?has non-initial states In the design file, you call the resetState ?function on this DAGNetwork  object  A stateful DAG network ?is one that that contains LSTM, BiLSTM, or GRU layers. A stateful DAG network has non-initial states if it has been updated by using the predictAndUpdateState  or classifyAndUpdateState  function. If you save an updated DAGNetwork  object to a MAT-file and then load the object from this MAT-file at a later time, those updated states are retained. To check if your DAGNetwork  object net  has non-initial states, call: net2= resetState(net);?  andthen compare the states of the layers of net2  and net . For LSTM and BiLSTM layers, the state properties are HiddenState  and CellState . For GRU layers, the stateproperty is HiddenState .?
Workaround: Toenable code generation for your stateful DAG network, reset its states beforeattempting code generation. To do this for a DAGNetwork object net, call:net= resetState(net);?Savethe resulting DAGNetwork object net?to a MAT-file and use this new MAT-file for code generation.?

Summary: Using extractFeatures on SIFTPoints returns duplicate valid points
Description: While extracting features from?SIFTPoints , duplicate points are created. In the following workflow for instance,?validPoints ? contains some duplicate?SIFTPoints :  points = detectSIFTFeatures(image);  [features, validPoints] = extractFeatures(image, points);
Workaround: The?duplicate points have orientation as 2*pi. The following modified feature extraction workflow shows how to remove the duplicate points:points = detectSIFTFeatures(image);[features, validPoints] = extractFeatures(image, points);%% Now remove duplicate points using one of the following ways:% 1. Remove duplicates using a tolerance on orientationtol = 0.001;validPoints(abs(validPoints.Orientation - 2*pi) < tol) = [];% OR% 2. Remove duplicates using single precisionvalidPoints(single(validPoints.Orientation) == single(2*pi)) = [];?

Summary: JMAAB check for?db_0125?does not report warning for data objects in State Transition Tables
Description: Model Advisor check Check definition of Stateflow data ?(id:?mathworks.jmaab.db_0125)? does not report warning for data objects used in State Transition Tables .

Summary: The JMAAB check for na_0021, na_0031, and na_0037 are not available in JMAAB By Task folder
Description: The Model Advisor check?Check usage of character vector inside MATLAB Function block?(id: mathworks.maab.na_0021), Check usage of enumerated values?(id: mathworks.maab.na_0031),  and ?Check use of single variable variant conditionals?(id: mathworks.maab.na_0037)  do not appear in the JMAAB By Task folder in Model Advisor
Workaround: These checks can be run from the By Task > MAB folder in Model Advisor.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: The JMAAB check for na_0004?reports blocks that are excluded
Description: The Model Advisor check?Check for Simulink diagrams using nonstandard display attributes?(id: mathworks.maab.na_0004)? reports blocks that are excluded using the exclusion feature of Model Advisor.

Summary: Missing collision meshes for Universal Robots
Description: The Universal Robot models UR3, UR5, and UR10 have missing collision meshes. This leads to incorrect results when using checkCollision function to check for collisions between the robot and its environment or itself.
Workaround: Workaround 1: Get the collision mesh files for Universal Robots from Robot Library Data support package.This workaround is applicable only for R2021b.?Steps:Download and install the Robotics System Toolbox Robot Library Data support package.Use the importrobot function to create a rigidBodyTree model of the robot with the mesh, robot = importrobot("universalUR10.urdf");Workaround 2: Get the collision mesh files for Universal Robots from the GitHub repository.This workaround is applicable for versions R2021b and earlier.?Steps:Clone the Universal Robots GitHub repository.Use the importrobot function to create a rigidBodyTree model of the robot with the mesh, robot=importrobot("universalUR10.urdf", 'MeshPath', <path to ur_description folder in repo>);NOTE: For version R2021a and earlier, this workaround will load only the collision meshes and not the visual meshes.

Summary: While running analysis on a pcbStack created with single metal layer and two dielectric layers, the top metal layer is being removed and a rectangular ground plane is added below the dielectric layers.?
Description: During analysis of a pcbStack?created with single metal layer and two dielectric layers, the top metal layer is being removed and a rectangular ground plane is added below the two dielectric layers.?   Example:  ??? p = pcbStack;  ??? % Create a stackup of single metal layer between two FR4 dielectric layers.??  ??? p.Layers = { dielectric('FR4'), p.Layers{1}, dielectric('FR4')}; ??? % Assign the feed location at the center of the second layer.  ??? p.FeedLocations = [0,0,2];  ??? % Run analysis and mesh.?  ??? figure; impedance(p,1.7e9);  ??? % Notice that in the mesh plot a ground plane is added? and the metal layer in the middle is removed. ??? figure; mesh(p);?

Summary: On Windows 11, Polyspace Bug Finder might error out during the Function Analysis part of the analysis and provide only partial results
Description: If you run a Polyspace Bug Finder analysis on a Windows 11 host, you might encounter an error during the Function Analysis  part of the analysis.The error message in the log file is exception Fail(Fail: failure in job) raised.  The error does not mean that the entire analysis failed. You can still see the Polyspace Bug Finder results, but the results are from the previous level, Pre-Analysis . Because higher levels detect more defects, the results of Pre-Analysis  might contain fewer than the actual number of defects (more false negatives).

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur when comparing anunsigned variable with a signed constant
Description: The generated code might be incorrect if itmeets all of the following conditions: CastingMode   property is set to Standards . TheMATLAB code in which an unsigned variable?is compared with a signedinteger constant using?relational operator?(>, <, >=, <=, ==. !=). TargetLang   is set to C++ .  In the below example, an unsigned variable iscompared with a signed constant. Sample MATLABcode:  function [output] = func_swa(input)  output = cast((input == 10),'double');   end  Inthe generated C++ code, an input not equal to zero wrapper is added which givesunexpected results. Generated C++code:  double func_swa(unsigned char input)   { return static_cast<double>((input != static_cast<unsigned char>(0)) == 10ULL);   }
Workaround: Set the CastingMode property to a value other than?Standards.

Summary: In Signal Editor, graphically removing data points of Boolean data type does not work as expected
Description: The erase and delete graphical editing tools do not work on signals of Boolean type. In some cases, the data points disappear and then return.

Summary: LPV System Block produces incorrect answer for LTI arrays with irregular grid and unsorted data in the SamplingGrid property.
Description: The LPV System block interpolates the model matrices and the offset data according to the chosen interpolation rule. However, when the LTI array corresponds to an irregular grid, the interpolation is always performed using the nearest neighbor approach. In this case, if the SamplingGrid  data is not monotonic for each variable, the interpolation may not be performed correctly. Example:?? sys = rss(2,1,1,1,3);  sys.SamplingGrid = struct('Alpha',(1:3)','Beta',[20 30 10]')
Workaround: Sort the systems in the LTI array based on their SamplingGrid field values before using it in the LPV System block.GridInfo =ltipack.SamplingGrid.getGridStructure(sys.SamplingGrid)sys = reshape(sys(:,:,GridInfo.SamplePerm),GridInfo.GridSize);?Reorder the offsets in states, state derivatives, inputs and outputs in a similar way (if present). For example, if the InputOffset is non-empty, reorder the data as follows:?InputOffset?= reshape(InputOffset(:,:,GridInfo.SamplePerm),GridInfo.GridSize);?

Summary: Lookup Table Dynamic block using Simulink.AliasType objects?might be unnecessarily stubbed during analysis
Description: If a model contains a Lookup Table Dynamic  block which uses Simulink.AliasType  objects, Simulink Design Verifier stubs the Lookup Table Dynamic  block and the model is incorrectly marked as partially compatible.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 2, R2022b

Summary: J1939 Transmit block does not inherit discrete sample time
Description: The J1939 Transmit block sample time is always set to continuous even if the inputs to the block are discrete in time. The block does not inherit discrete sample time.?This can unintentionally result in a multi-rate model.

Summary: Analysis may terminate without producing results when performing Test Generation with LongTestcases strategy
Description: Simulink Design Verifier analysis might terminate without producing results when all of the following conditions are true: Test Generation is being executed with a Test Suite Optimization parameter value of LongTestcases  At least one block or function in the model is affected by automatic stubbing.  MATLAB displays the following error message:Internal error in Simulink Design Verifier back end.

Summary: JMAAB check for?jc_0009 gives incorrect result for signal connected to From block
Description: The Model Advisor check?Check for propagated signal labels  ?(id: mathworks.jmaab.jc_0009)  does not report the signal connected to From block when the Show?propagated signal ?parameter is not selected and the corresponding signal connected to?Goto block has signal label.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: MISRA C:2012?check?Check for unsupported block names?does not report?block names in inactive variant subsystems
Description: MISRA C:2012?Check for unsupported block names?(id:?mathworks.misra.BlockNames)? does not report warning for invalid block names in inactive variant of a Variant Subsystem.

Summary: The JMAAB check for jc_0641?reports Enable and Trigger blocks inside Subsystem blocks
Description: The Model Advisor check?Check for sample time setting?(id: mathworks.jmaab.jc_0641)? reports Enable  and Trigger  blocks for setting sample time other than -1 . When inside Subsystem, the sample time setting for these block is disabled and user cannot modify them.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: The JMAAB check for?jc_0641 does not report trigonometric?function block
Description: The Model Advisor check?Check for sample time setting (id: mathworks.jmaab.jc_0641)? does not report trigonometric function blocks even if the sample time is not set to -1.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: JMAAB check for?jc_0752?does not report warning for parentheses in condition actions
Description: Model Advisor check Check usage of parentheses in Stateflow transitions ?(id:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0752)? does not report warning for p arentheses in a single line?having characters (comments or transition action) before and after curly braces.

Summary: The JMAAB check for?jc_0644 does not report Outport block
Description: The Model Advisor check?Check type setting by data objects  ?(id: mathworks.jmaab.jc_0644)? does not report an Outport? block that has a data type set on it even though the data type is specified in a signal object.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: The JMAAB check for?jc_0602?reports Inport blocks with prefixes
Description: The Model Advisor check?Check for consistency in model element names  ?(ID: mathworks.jmaab.jc_0602)  reports Inport  blocks if the connecting signal name and the Inport block name do not match even though the only difference in the names is that the Inport  block name has prefix specified as a check input parameter which is an exception to the rule.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: JMAAB check for db_0042 incorrectly flags hidden Simulink blocks
Description: Model Advisor?check? Check position of Inport and Outport blocks  ( ID: ?mathworks.jmaab.db_0042 ) incorrectly flags hidden Simulink blocks inside Stateflow charts.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Custom attribute values are deleted when you move a requirement by clicking and dragging
Description: If a requirement has custom attributes and you move the requirement in the Requirements Editor ?by clicking and dragging, the software removes the custom attributes.

Summary: Opening project archive fails with PathNotFound exception
Description: Opening a project archive (.mlpro j) fails if both  conditions are true: The archived project references another project. The referenced project is stored in at least two-level deep subfolder inside the top-level project root.  MATLAB errors with the following message:?Caught "std::exception" Exception message is: fl:filesystem:PathNotFound

Summary: The MTPA Control Reference block and the ACIM Control Reference block do not limit the negative Iq Reference output to the specified Max current
Description: The MTPA Control Reference block andthe ACIM Control Reference Block in Motor Control Blockset output Id reference current and Iqreference current. When the Iq reference is negative and the motor is operating in the field weakeningregion, these blocks do not limit the output Iq reference according to the specified Max current.

Summary: JMAAB check for db_0129?does not report?transition lines that overlap one another
Description: The?Sub-ID b? of Model Advisor?check?mathworks.jmaab.db_0129 (db_0129_b:?Check for transition lines that overlap one another)? does not report warning for?transition lines that do not overlap one another entirely.

Summary: Empty XPath context node might crash MATLAB
Description: Passing an empty context node to the evaluate  method of a matlab.io.xml.xpath.Evaluator  object might crash MATLAB. For example, the following script crashes MATLAB: d = matlab.io.xml.dom.Document('book');?nl =?getChildNodes(d);n = item(nl,1);  % n is an empty object of type Node.b = evaluate(matlab.io.xml.xpath.Evaluator,'/',n);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for dlarray indexing operations might produce incorrect answer
Description: Code generated for indexing operations on dlarray   objects using parenthesis might produce incorrect answer if the?dlarray  object is permuted (directly or indirectly) before indexing. The following are some example MATLAB code patterns that might be affected by this issue. Example 1: dlX  = dlarray(ones(5, 5, 16, 'single'));dlX  = permute(dlX, [3 1 2]);dlY  = dlX(:, :, 1:4); Example 2: dlX = dlarray(reshape(1:24, [2 3 4]));dlX = permute(dlX, [3 1 2]);dlX(1, 1, 1) = 1;
Workaround: Before indexing into the dlarray object, invoke the extractdata method. After the indexing operation, reconstruct the?dlarray object.Modified example 1:dlX  = dlarray(ones(5, 5, 16, 'single'));dlX  = permute(dlX, [3 1 2]);x = extractdata(dlX);dlY = dlarray(x(:, :, 1:4));Modified example 2:dlX = dlarray(reshape(1:24, [2 3 4]));dlX = permute(dlX, [3 1 2]);x = extractdata(dlX);x(1, 1, 1) = 1;dlX = dlarray(x);

Summary: The Clean Missing Data task can generate plot code that produces an indexing error in the Live Editor.
Description: In the Clean Missing Data task, the generated plot code can have an incorrect index. This can result in either an error or the plot visualizing the incorrect data.? The bug occurs in the case where all of these are true: The input data is a table or timetable The output format is "Create new table from selected variables" The specified variables are not the left-most variables in the table

Summary: Simulation 3D Lidar block returns point cloud data from areas that do not have objects
Description: The Simulation 3D Lidar block returns point cloud data for samples in areas that do not have objects.?The block incorrectly processes points that are on different objects, returning point cloud data from the space between the objects.

Summary: Increase in build times for Simulink Real-Time models that have a large number of blocks
Description: For Simulink Real-Time models that have a large number of blocks, you might observe that the build times have increased in R2021b compared with R2021a.

Summary: Code Generation Failure for FIR Interpolation Blocks in Cascade when 'DSP Intel AVX2-FMA' Code Replacement Library (CRL) is Used
Description: When you use the 'DSP Intel AVX2-FMA' SIMD Code Replacement Library (CRL) to generate code from a model that contains a cascade of FIR Interpolation blocks or from a MATLAB? function that contains a cascade of dsp.FIRInterpolator System objects, the build process fails with this error: error: expected ';' before 'b_FIRInterpSglCmplx' error: 'b_FIRInterpSglCmplx' undeclared (first use in this function); did you mean 'FIRInterpSglCmplx'?? ?? Build error: C compiler produced errors. See the Build Log for further details.? Code generation failed: View Error Report

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulation result might be incorrect for a MATLAB Function block inside a For Each subsystem
Description: Simulation result?might be incorrect for a model that meets these conditions:The model contains a For Each subsystem. The For Each subsystem contains a MATLAB Function block. Using the Ports and Data Manager, an input and an output of the MATLAB Function block are configured to share the same variable. The input of the MATLAB Function block that satisfies the above condition is directly connected to the inport of the For Each subsystem. The simulation mode is set to Rapid Accelerator .

Summary: Exporting PWM Reference Generator block to a version earlier than R2021b?produces broken library link
Description: Suppose that you create a Simulink model in R2021b by including the PWM Reference Generator block from Motor Control Blockset. If you export this model to an earlier version of Simulink, the exported model displays a broken library link for the PWM Reference Generator block.? In such situations, when the model is saved in the earlier format, the correct behavior is that the model export process should replace the PWM Reference Generator Block with a Space Vector Generator block.

Summary: JMAAB check for jc_0451 use of unary minus on Boolean is not detected
Description: Model Advisor?check?Check usage of unary minus operations in Stateflow charts (id :?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0451) ?does not flag the usage of unary minus on Boolean data types when the action language is C.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Saving a dlnetwork object in a MAT file reverts the OutputNames property to the default values
Description: If you change the OutputNames  property of a dlnetwork  object and save it in a MAT file, then the software does not save the updated OutputNames  property. When you load the network, the OutputNames  property contains the default values.
Workaround: After you load the dlnetwork object, manually set the OutputNames property to the appropriate names. For example, to specify that the output layers of the network net are the layers with names "out1" and "out2", usenet.OutputNames = ["out1" "out2"];

Summary: The specified key is not present in this container error when trying to import requirements from IBM DOORS 9
Description: When importing a module from IBM DOORS 9 (also sometimes referred to as IBM Rational DOORS) into Simulink Requirements , the process does not complete, and an error dialog box appears that says:?The specified key is not present in this container .? This happens with DOORS modules that contain DOORS tables.

Summary: JMAAB check for?jc_0762 gives inconsistent warnings
Description: Model Advisor check  Prohibition of state action and flow chart combination (id: mathworks.jmaab.jc_0762)  gives inconsistent number of warnings before and after compiling the model.

Summary: Sub-check?b of?JMAAB check for?jc_0121?does not flag when the Sum block is part of feedback loop
Description: Model Advisor?check?Check usage of Sum blocks   (ID :?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0121) ?sub-check  b ?does not flag a feedback loop violation when the first input to the Sum block has a negative sign, ?and the feedback loop only connects through the second input to the Sum block.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: If target.Processor?object is added to internal database in R2021a,?target?package functions cannot be used after upgrade to R2021b, R2022a, or R2022b
Description: Suppose you add a?target.Processor ?object?to an internal database in R2021a and then upgrade to R2021b, R2022a, or R2022b. After the upgrade,?you are not able to use the target  package functions. The functions produce an error. For example, in MATLAB R2021a, you run: processor = target.create("Processor", "Name", "ExampleProcessor");  target.add(processor, "UserInstall", true);   Then, after an upgrade to MATLAB R2022a, you run: target.get("Processor", "ExampleProcessor");   The function produces this error:? Error loading Target Repository file ".../R2022a/TargetFramework/userregistrations.xml". Type 'target.profiling.XCPBuffer' has not been found in the registry.
Workaround: Navigate to the?userregistrations.xml?file in the?MATLAB?preferences folder.fullfile(prefdir, 'TargetFramework', 'userregistrations.xml')From each?target.Processor?element in the file,?remove this line:    <XCPBuffer type="target.profiling.XCPBuffer"/>The following processor definition provides an example:  <target.Processor type="target.Processor">? ? <XCPBuffer type="target.profiling.XCPBuffer"/> <--- REMOVE THIS LINE? ? <Name>ExampleProcessor</Name>  </target.Processor>
Fixed In: R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: MISRA-C:2012 14.2 false positive on second expression when loop counter comparison is done on an addition
Description: In a for -loop, when the loop counter is added to an extra variable to do the test, Polyspace incorrectly raises a violation of MISRA-C:2012 14.2. For instance: for(i = 0; (i+a)<b; i++) //False positive  Starting in R2022a, Polyspace does not flag the use of binary operations in a for  loop.
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder Server, Polyspace Bug Finder

Summary: MISRA-C:2012 20.7 false positive in variable declarations
Description: Polyspace improperly raises a MISRA-C:2012 20.7 violation when a variable declaration is defined as a macro. Such macros are not expressions and operator precedence is not relevant .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for models containing custom TLC S-function blocks
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model under the following circumstances: The model contains custom TLC S-Function blocks that create and accept expressions in the generated code. The output port of the TLC S-Function block that creates an expression is connected to the input port of a Selector block, selecting elements other than the first element. The input port of the TLC S-Function block?that accepts an expression?is connected to the output port of the Selector block.  The generated code for the Selector block incorrectly selects the first element, and the element according to the start index.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Setting an ASCII format string with delimiters in a TCP/IP Receive block results in an error
Description: If you specify the ASCII format string  parameter with delimiters in a TCP/IP Receive block, the following unexpected error occurs: The ASCII format string entry must be a valid numeric conversion specifier in the form of a character vector or string.
Products: Instrument Control Toolbox, Industrial Communication Toolbox

Summary: MISRA-C:2012 8.13 false positive when accessing data by using a local copy of a nonconst pointer parameter
Description: Polyspace raises an incorrect violation of MISRA-C:2012 8.13 when a nonconst  pointer is passed to a function, and its pointed data is modified by using a local copy of the pointer. In this example, a false positive violation of MISRA-C:2012 8.13 occurs on parameter bar . Because the data pointed to by  bar  is modified by the local copy  ptr ,? ?bar cannot be a const . typedef struct{??? int a;??? char text[20];} myStruct;void foo(myStruct* bar){??? char* ptr;??? ptr = bar->text;??? *ptr = 'a';}
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder Server, Polyspace Bug Finder

Summary: Multirelease equivalence tests with incomplete first simulation produce passing results
Description: Multirelease equivalence tests for a scenario in which the first simulation fails to complete and the second simulation completes successfully incorrectly report passing results.?

Summary: App Designer MLAPP file may be corrupted by a custom component that contains an SLRT System Log component
Description: If you create a custom App Designer component that contains an SLRT System Log component and then save your App Designer app that uses this custom component, the custom component may corrupt the MLAPP file and make the app unusable.

Summary: Security Issue: Default configuration of matlab.io.xml.dom.Parser?with document type definitions (DTDs) is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attack
Description: The XML markup parser class matlab.io.xml.dom.Parser  parses documents with document type definitions (DTDs) by default, which makes the parser vulnerable to an XML External Entity (XXE) attack if you try to parse a malicious document. A malicious DTD might cause the parser to disclose potentially sensitive information in files local to the machine running the parser or other internal content that the parser has access to, for example, via http  requests. The following is an example of a malicious DTD that attempts to disclose a password file: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>  <!DOCTYPE foo [  ? <!ELEMENT foo ANY >  ? <!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM "file:///etc/passwd" >]>  <foo>&xxe;</foo>?   In some cases, the DTD might instigate a denial-of-service attack by accessing a local resource and causing it to not stop returning data.
Workaround: Configure the XML Parser to not parse documents with DTDs:p = matlab.io.xml.dom.Parser;?p.Configuration.DisallowDoctype = true;?or??p.Configuration.AllowDoctype = false;?Only parse documents with DTDs from trusted sources.?

Summary: Polyspace analysis fails when using standard headers on Unix systems
Description: When you use the Polyspace desktop interface to analyze a project generated with polyspace-configure , the -dos  option is enabled by default. With this option, the paths of included files are resolved in a case insensitive manner. For instance, inc/myHeader.h  resolves to inc/myheader.h . This can create conflicts with standard headers on Unix systems where paths are case sensitive, which might cause the analysis to fail. The error message that you see depends on the system header that conflicts with the resolved case insensitive path.? For example, if you have empty file inc/Time.h  and this main.cpp  file:?     #include <ctime>    int main() {        return 0;     } The file compiles successfully on a Unix system if you use g++ -Iinc main.cpp .? If you generate a project with polyspace-configure g++ -Iinc main.cpp  and then run an analysis in the Polyspace desktop interface on this project, you see an error message similar to this: ?Error: ?the global scope has no "clock_t"  ? ?? using ::clock_t; ????????? ? ? ? ^  The error occurs because ctime  includes system header <time.h> , which is incorrectly resolved to case insensitive path inc/Time.h (inc/time.h) .
Workaround: Turn off the -dos option before you run the analysis:In the Configuration pane, go to Environment Settings under the Target & Compiler node.Uncheck the option Code from DOS or Windows file system.
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder Server, Polyspace Bug Finder

Summary: Assertion error when using custom code in relational expressions
Description: When you try to generate code from a Stateflow chart that contains a relational expression?using custom code, Stateflow might throw the following error: Unexpected or internal error encountered in "in CGIR: B:\matlab\src\cg_ir\factory\ConstFactory.cpp line 587: In createConst0: don't know how to create a zero constant of the specified type".  For example:? if (customCodeVar > chartLocalVar) {??  // code?  } else {?  // code?  }  Here, customCodeVar  is been defined in custom code and chartLocalVar   is been defined in the chart.?
Workaround: Enable Configuration Parameters > Simulation Target >?Import Custom Code

Summary: While importing from OpenDrive, guardrails and freeway gantries appear offset from source OpenDRIVE data
Description: When the standard GuardRail.rrext extrusion is mapped while importing from OpenDRIVE, the imported guardrail appears vertically offset from the source data. The resulting Prop Curve is placed at the location where the metal part of the guardrail ought to be. When a freeway gantry is mapped while importing from OpenDRIVE, the imported gantry appears laterally offset from the source data. The resulting Prop Point is placed such that the center of the gantry is over the <object> location from the source OpenDRIVE data.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: The estimateGeometricTransform2D?function returns anincorrect rigid transformation
Description: The generated code for the?estimateGeometricTransform2D   function incorrectlyreturns an identity transformation when setting the transformation type to?'rigid ' . Syntax that returns incorrect output in generated code:? tform = estimateGeometricTransform2D(matchedPoints1,matchedPoints2,'rigid')
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a

Summary: Connected Switch and Scope blocks produces error in XCP-based external mode simulation
Description: If a model contains a Switch block with inputs that are virtual buses and the Switch block connects to a Scope block, when you run an XCP-based external mode simulation, the simulation produces an error when Simulink tries to connect to the target application. For example: Error using coder. oneclick. TargetHook/ extModeConnect  External Mode Open Protocol SignalSelect command failed  Error in on_target_one_click   You might also observe the error if the model contains a block that uses a similar block connection?pattern internally. For example, a PID autotuner block from the Simulink Control Design library.
Workaround: Remove the Scope block and use the Simulation Data Inspector or a Dashboard library block to display the signal.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Using a Data Store Read block with a GetSet storage class that feeds into a Prelookup block or Interpolation Using Prelookup block might generate extraneous set function calls
Description: When you map a global Data Store block to a GetSet  storage class and you connect a Data Store Read block directly to a Prelookup block or Interpolation Using Prelookup block, the code generator might produce set? function calls in addition to get  function calls. The set  function calls are unnecessary for a Data Store Read block and can reduce performance.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: MATLAB Compiler does not package files associated?with certain support packages
Description: When applications that use files from certain support packages are packaged using Application Compiler, Library Compiler apps, the mcc  command, or the compiler.build  family of functions, the support package files are excluded. The list of support packages affected by this problem is: DCAM Hardware GenICam Interface Kinect for Windows Sensor NI Frame Grabbers OS Generic Video Interface Point Grey Hardware Teledyne Dalsa Sapera Hardware
Workaround: Use the includedSupportPackages.txt file generated by the mcc command to identify the support package that was included in the compilation. Execute the following commands, substituting the support package name listed in the includedSupportPackages.txt file for <spkgname>.>> spkgList = matlab.depfun.internal.DeployableSupportPackages;>> spkg = getSupportPackage(spkgList, '<spkgname>');>> getIncludeList(spkg)Include the output of getIncludeList(spkg) as an additional file during compilation.If using the mcc command, use the -a option and specify the additional file generated above.If using the Application Compiler or Library Compiler apps, click the Settings button and include the?-a?option and specify the additional file generated above in the Additional parameters passed to mcc field.If using one of the compiler.build.* functions, use the AdditionalFiles option to specify the additional file generated above.

Summary: AUTOSAR-C++14 A2-7-3 false positive on documented declarations that are local to a function
Description: Polyspace incorrecly raises violations of AUTOSAR-C++14 A2-7-3 on documented local declarations.

Summary: Simulation of a Simulink model with multiple MATLAB Function blocks loading similar networks might produce incorrect answers
Description: Consider a Simulink model with multiple MATLAB Function blocks that load networks differing only in weight values. Simulation of such a model might produce an incorrect answer, if the networks share the same name. For example, suppose that?net1.mat  and net2.mat  contain networks that differ by weights only and are loaded in two different MATLAB Function blocks. The two blocks contain these two MATLAB functions respectively: MATLAB Function block 1?  function scores1 = fcn(u)persistent netCif isempty(netC)    netA = coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork('net1.mat', 'network');endscores1 = predict(netA,u);end MATLAB Function block 2 ? function scores2 = fcn(u) persistent netDif isempty(netD)    netB = coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork('net2.mat','network'); % network name is same as in first MATLAB function blockendscores2 = predict(netB,u);end  Simulating a model that contains both these MATLAB Function blocks might produce an incorrect answer.?
Workaround: Specify unique network names for unique networks.MATLAB Function block 1?function scores1 = fcn(u)persistent netCif isempty(netC)    netA = coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork('net1.mat', 'network1');endscores1 = predict(netA,u);endMATLAB Function block 2?function scores2 = fcn(u) persistent netDif isempty(netD)    netB = coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork('net2.mat','network2');% network name is different from first MATLAB function blockendscores2 = predict(netB,u);end Alternatively, do not provide any network name. MATLAB and Simulink will automatically create unique network names.?MATLAB Function block 1?function scores1 = fcn(u)persistent netCif isempty(netC)    netA = coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork('net1.mat');endscores1 = predict(netA,u);endMATLAB Function block 2?function scores2 = fcn(u) persistent netDif isempty(netD)    netB = coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork('net2.mat');endscores2 = predict(netB,u);end
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a

Summary: Inaccurate eye area listed in the Time Domain tab of Signal Integrity Viewer app.
Description: In the Time Domain tab of the Signal Integrity Viewer  app, the eye area is listed as Eye Area (V*ps) . The actual values listed in the time domain eye area column are in V*ns, and smaller by a factor of thousand.

Summary: Compatibility check for Software-in-the-Loop (SIL) test generation analysis fails
Description: While generating the test cases for Embedded Coder generated code, the SLDV compatibility check unexpectedly fails when both of the following conditions are true: Model contains inputs (or parameters) having their custom storage class set to ImportedExternPointer  Those inputs (or parameters) are not scalar

Summary: asbQuadcopter example project generates error during compilation when VSS_VEHICLE is set to 0
Description: The  asbQuadcopter ?example project generates errors during compilation when?VSS_VEHICLE ?is set to 0. This error is generated because starting in R2021b,?opcond.OperatingReport ?no longer supports partial name compatibility. However, the?linearAirframe ?model of the?asbQuadcopter ?example uses the partial name Input ?instead of the correct full name,?Inputs . As a result, setting the vehicle variant VSS_VEHICLE  to 0 causes the model to fail.
Workaround: Open the linearAirframe model in the project within linearAirframe/linearAirframe.slx.Open the linear subsystem and change all opreport.Input?calls to opreport.Inputs.Save the modified model.

Summary: Exporting a System Composer model by using the systemcomposer.exportModel?function might produce an error
Description: Exporting a System Composer model by using the?systemcomposer.exportModel ?function might produce an error in either  of these situations: The System Composer model contains physical ports and physical connectors. Error using systemcomposer.internal.exportModelClass/addConnection2tableAn error occurred when concatenating the table variable 'PortIDs' using VERTCAT.   The System Composer model has a requirement linked to multiple components. Index exceeds the number of array elements. Index must not exceed 1.Error in systemcomposer.internal.exportModelClass/addRequirementLinksToTable

Summary: Unable to use local cluster when username on Windows contains non-ASCII characters
Description: On Windows, the local cluster fails to start parpool or independent jobs if your username contains characters that are not in the basic ASCII range. This includes usernames that have extended ASCII characters such as "?". Attempting to use the local cluster results in failures that do not give much information, for example: >> parpool('local')  Error using parpool (line 151)  Failed to initialize the interactive session.
Workaround: Use the "-c" startup flag to override the default license path of MATLAB to one that contains only ASCII characters. The default license path covers all locations where a license file might be found: one of these locations contains the username and this needs to be modified.1) Obtain the current default license path using the following command in MATLAB:feature('lmsearchpath') 2) Look for paths in this string that contain your username. There should be exactly one such path, typically located at:C:\Users\%USERNAME%\AppData\Roaming\MathWorks\MATLAB\R2021b_licenses 3) Obtain the ASCII-safe version of this same path. This can be done in MATLAB using the command:!dir /X C:\Users\Alongside the path for your username, this command will list an ASCII-safe version that should end with a "~1" (or a "~2", etc). For example, username "Bj?rnson" might have the ASCII-safe version "BJRNSO~1".4)? Use the "-c" startup flag of MATLAB to use a license path where all references to your username have been modified to use ASCII-safe versions. For more information on how to do this, see:https://uk.mathworks.com/matlabcentral/answers/102520-how-do-i-change-the-license-search-location-for-matlab

Summary: Running a Model Advisor check multiple times with different input parameters using ModelAdvisor.run results in the check not running
Description: When you run the same Model Advisor check multiple times with different input parameters using ModelAdvisor. run , ?the check runs only once and does not run again for a different set of input parameters.

Summary: compiler.package.docker fails with an error "COPY failed" when the value stored in the USER environment variable has a backslash "\" in it
Description: The MATLAB Compiler function "compiler.package.docker"  will fail when the value stored in the USER environment variable has a backslash "\" in it.? The error might look similar to: When creating the Runtime image, 'docker build' failed with the following log:  Sending build context to Docker daemon? 2.048kB  Step 1/3 : FROM matlabruntimebase/r2021a/release/update7  ?---> 00b2297ae2d3  Step 2/3 : COPY ./v910 /opt/matlabruntime/v910  COPY failed: stat /var/lib/docker/tmp/docker-builder680483356/v910: no such file or directory
Workaround: Instead of running compiler.package.docker, execute the following in MATLAB instead:clear classesoriguser = getenv('USER');oc = onCleanup(@()setenv('USER',origuser));newuser = strrep(origuser,'\','_');setenv('USER',newuser) % USER now has no backslashtry? ? compiler.package.docker(r)? ? clear occatch err? ? clear oc % when done? ? throw(err)end

Summary: sysctl utility reports error after target computer migrates from OnTime to QNX Neutrino RTOS
Description: After you migrate a target computer from the OnTime RTOS to the QNX Neutrino RTOS, the QNX sysctl  utility reports an error. The target computer RTOS migration is not affected. The target computer boots and successfully updates the RTOS.
Workaround: After the target computer migration, do a target update from MATLAB.

Summary: Incorrect validation loss when using pixelClassificationLayer with class weights
Description: When training a network using the trainNetwork  function, the software computes the incorrect validation loss when the network contains a PixelClassificationLayer  object with a numeric ClassWeights  property and the MiniBatchSize  training option is strictly less than the number of observations in the validation data.
Workaround: Set the MiniBatchSize training option to the number of observations in the validation data using the trainingOptions function.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: In single clock mode, the timing controller can be incorrectly generated when requesting Oversampling and not utilizing Clock-Rate Pipelining.
Description: In rare instances, the timing controller generated by HDL Coder might not be appropriately clocking the design. The issue is evident during simulation of the HDL code because all or parts of the design have the clocking gated to 0. The issue occurs when the model has a base rate which is not used inside the DUT Subsystem. The model base rate, the fastest rate in the DUT, and oversampling factor all must have a certain relation in order for the timing controller to not be generated correctly. For example, if the model base rate is 1, the fastest rate in the DUT is 4, and the oversampling factor is 2, this issue can occur. The issue does not occur in most cases where an oversampling request creates an unused clock.
Workaround: To avoid the issue, choose one of these two workarounds:Alter the sample time of elements outside the DUT so that the DUT contains the fastest rate reported by the Simulink Timing Legend.Remove the oversampling request by setting Oversampling to 1, its default.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: When doing horizontal concatenation followed by vertical concatenation
Description: MATLAB Coder might generate incorrect code if your MATLAB code satisfies?all ?of these conditions: Generates code using row major layout. Completely initializes a variable-size array p . Writes to a piece of p . Concatenates p  horizontally. Concatenates p  vertically.   Incorrect generated code uses wrong indices to access p .? For example consider the following MATLAB code: function p = foo()p = [1 1];coder.varsize('p',[2 3]);p(1,1) = 2;p = [p, 1];p = [p; ones(1,3)];end In the generated code, function foo  uses out-of-bounds indices to access p : void foo(const emlrtStack *sp, real_T p_data[], int32_T p_size[2]){   (void)sp;   p_data[1] = 1.0;   p_data[0] = 2.0;   p_size[1] = 3;   p_size[0] = 2;   p_data[3] = 1.0;   p_data[6] = 1.0;   p_data[7] = 1.0;   p_data[8] = 1.0;} Output p  has size 2x3 but foo  is writes to p  using indices 6, 7, and 8 at the end.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: visadevlist and visadev functions return an error or incomplete instrument list
Description: The visadevlist  function might not list any available instruments when it is unable to fully identify all of the instruments found during device discovery. Similarly, the visadev  function might treat a resource string as invalid when it cannot fully identify the resource, such as when an instrument does not respond to a *IDN?  query.  For example, the visadev  function might error when attempting to use a Keithley SCS4200: >> v = visadev("GPIB0::17::INSTR");Resource string is invalid or resource was not found. In this case, the instrument failed to respond to a "*IDN?"  query, which returns an error.
Products: Instrument Control Toolbox, Industrial Communication Toolbox

Summary: Models created in SimBiology Model Builder?might not appear in SimBiology Model Analyzer
Description: If you create a model in the SimBiology Model Builder  app, and then open SimBiology Model Analyzer , the model sometimes does not appear in the workspace browser of Model Analyzer .
Workaround: Open both Model Analyzer and Model Builder?apps before creating a model in Model Builder.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a

Summary: HDL code generation failure from a top-level model that contains a protected model
Description: HDL code generation for a Simulink model might fail if both  these conditions are true:? You attempt to generate code for a top-level model that contains a protected model The?.slx ?file for the protected model is open or on MATLAB path  If code generation fails, you get this error: The protected model has been changed since it was created,  or the file has been corrupted.  To use this protected model,  you need to recreate it.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a

Summary: ?xil_interface.c build error during SIL/PIL simulation
Description: Consider a model with a logged signal that is a matrix and maps to AUTOSAR typed per-instance memory (ArTypedPerInstanceMemory ). When you run a software-in-the-loop (SIL) or processor-in-the-loop (PIL) simulation of the model, during the build process you might see a memory access error for?xil_interface.c .

Summary: parquetread might incorrectly import columns whose PhysicalType is Date32
Description: parquetread  incorrectly imports Date32  values that exceed the date 9-Jan-2038 03:14:07 as the datetime  9-Jan-2038 03:14:07.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 2, R2022a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results for accelerator and rapid accelerator simulations for models containing continuous integrator blocks with limits enabled and wide signals.
Description: For models containing continuous integrator blocks with limits enabled, simulation results in accelerator and rapid accelerator may be inaccurate when using the block with a wide signal.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code coverage might provide wrong coverage values on first run
Description: Processor-in-the-Loop (PIL) code coverage can give wrong coverage values on some embedded targets (like TI C2000) if the following conditions are true: The cross-compiler does not follow the ANSI C zero initialization rule of the uninitialized global variables. The hardware does not perform RAM initialization at startup.
Workaround: The incorrect behavior is observed during the first run only. You can work around this issue by re-analyzing coverage (without resetting the target) and ignoring the first run.

Summary: Showing enable port on Function-Call Generator block prevents implicit expansion of output signal
Description: When you configure a Function-Call Generator block to show the enable port, Simulink might throw unexpected dimension mismatch errors.? For example, if the block has the Show enable port  parameter selected, the Number of iterations ?parameter is specified as a scalar, and the block output is connected to a Demux block with two or more outputs, Simulink might throw a dimension mismatch error.
Workaround: Specify the Number of iterations parameter of the Function-Call Generator block as an N-element vector, where N is the intended dimension of the downstream signal. For example, if the Function-Call Generator block output is connected to a Demux block with three outputs, and you want to generate two function-call events at each time step, specify the Number of iterations?as [2 2 2] rather than 2.

Summary: Calling Python class methods error out when called from command-line and in some workflows from scripts.
Description: MATLAB displays this error when calling Python class methods from the command-line: Unable to return the number of named arguments if not in an active function.  This can occur for class methods that require an import statement in Python for access. For example, when you use this Python module in MATLAB: *** mymod.py *** class mycls:   def __init__(self) -> None:       pass   def myfun(self):       print(2)*** EOF mymod.py *** MATLAB displays: >> pobj = py.mymod.mycls();  >> pobj.myfun()Error using py.mymod.mycls/myfunUnable to return the number of named arguments if not in an active function. In some cases you can wrap the call in a MATLAB function, but this is not a verified workflow for all cases.

Summary: Database Explorer app crashes during test of data source connection
Description: MATLAB crashes when you test a data source connection using the JDBC Data Source Configuration, MySQL Data Source Configuration, and PostgreSQL Data Source Configuration dialog boxes from the Database Explorer app. The crash only occurs when the password field is left blank before pressing the Test  button.
Workaround: To test a data source connection with no password, enter?one or more characters in the?password field, and then delete them. The crash will not occur.

Summary: Incorrect Polyspace project generation when using polyspace-configure with compilation database
Description: If you generate a? Polyspace project or analysis options file by using polyspace-configure  with a compilation database (CCDB), the generated project or options file is incorrect if both of these are true: The CCDB "directory" fields contain folder paths with spaces in them.  The CCDB "command" fields contain relative paths.   For example, if you generate a Polyspace options file by using polyspace-configure  with this CCDB: [{? "directory": "\/home\/folder with spaces\/project\/src",? "command": "gcc -I..\/ main.c",? "file": "\/home\/folders with spaces\/project\/src\/main.c"}]  The generated options file contains this erroneous entry for the include folder path (-I ): -options-for-sources /home/folder with spaces/project/src/main.c;-I /home/folder with spaces/project/src/spaces/project/;  ...  Instead of the correct entry: -options-for-sources /home/folder with spaces/project/src/main.c;-I /home/folder with spaces/project/ ; ...  When you run an analysis with the generated options file, you see an error message similar to this: line 1: warning: could not find include file "foo.h"
Workaround: Try one of the following to work around this issue:Rename? your project folder to remove spaces in the folder path. For instance:/home/folder with spaces/project -->?/home/folder_without_spaces/projectUse absolute paths in the "command" field of the CCDB. For instance:[{? "directory": "\/home\/folder with spaces\/project\/src",? "command": "gcc -I\"\/home\/folder with spaces\/project\" main.c",? "file": "\/home\/folders with spaces\/project\/src\/main.c"}] ?Fix the incorrect entries manually in the generated options file.

Summary: When running in batch mode, MATLAB sometimes shows a Java error message when shutting down
Description: When running MATLAB with the batch  command flag, a Java error message displays when MATLAB is shutting down. The message begins with: java.lang.ExceptionInInitializerErrorat com.mathworks.mde.editor.plugins.editordataservice.debug.DebuggerInstaller.initializeListeners(DebuggerInstaller.java:78) at com.mathworks.mde.editor.plugins.editordataservice.debug.DebuggerInstaller.(DebuggerInstaller.java:64)   This message is benign and can be ignored.?

Summary: Code generated for TensorRT target might cause MATLAB to crash at run time if the deep learning network uses?convolution2dLayer?and large inputs
Description: Code generated for deep learning networks that uses the TensorRT library might cause MATLAB to crash at run time if the network uses?convolution2dLayer ?and image inputs with large dimensions (width*height*channel*batch). The following error message is thrown in the MATLAB command window: ?### Error occured when invoking TensorRT API ###?Tensor: (Unnamed Layer* 1) [Convolution]_output at max batch size of 128 exceeds the maximum element count of 2147483647  For standalone applications, the execution of the program might terminate abruptly with the same error message.
Workaround: Reduce the batch size of the network input.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for deep learning network containing custom layers might produce incorrect results if?Inline between Mathworks functions is set to 'Never'
Description: GPU code generated for a deep neural network might produce incorrect results if both  these conditions are true:? The deep neural network contains a custom layer that produces two or more outputs ?In the code configuration object or the GPU Coder app, you set the?Inline between MathWorks functions ?parameter to?'Never'   For example, consider a network with the following layer graph:? lgraph = layerGraph([    imageInputLayer([28 28 1])    multiOutLayer(20, 'multiOut')    additionLayer(2, 'Name', 'add')    regressionLayer]);lgraph = connectLayers(lgraph, 'multiOut/out2', 'add/in2');? Suppose that?multiOutLayer ?is a custom layer that you defined and included in the network. This layer produces two or more outputs. For more information, see?Define Custom Deep Learning Layer for Code Generation .? The code generated for this network might produce incorrect results if you use the following code configuration parameters: cfg = coder.config('mex');??cfg.DeepLearningConfig = coder.DeepLearningConfig('TargetLibrary', 'cudnn'); % or 'tensorrt' cfg.InlineBetweenMathWorksFunctions = 'Never';

Summary: MATLAB might crash while rearranging contents of a large block diagram
Description: Rearranging contents of a large Simulink block diagram might cause MATLAB to crash because of smart editing features.
Workaround: Disable smart editing features. In the Simulink Toolstrip, on the Modeling tab, select Environment > Simulink Preferences. In the Editor pane of the Simulink Preferences dialog box, clear Enable smart editing features.?

Summary: createInputDataset might return uninformative error message
Description: When using createInputDataset  on incorrectly configured models, the resulting error message is generic and uninformative.

Summary: Expected output field may not be present in the Simulink Design Verifier results for model having output ports as n-dimensional vector
Description: After the completion of Simulink Design Verifier analysis, the results will not have the expected output field if:There is an output port in the model which is a n-dimensional vector, and It is represented using either row matrix [1 n] or column matrix [n 1].

Summary: Compatibility check and block replacement API fails with error about incorrect block execution priorities for models with Initialize Functions and model references
Description: Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check fails with error about incorrect user-specified block execution priorities if the model consists of following types of blocks:Initialize Function  Model Reference  Global data such as Data Store Memory  or Simulink.Signal  objects  Similarly, the replacement model generated by sldvblockreplacement  API for such models also fails to compile with the same error about incorrect user-specific block execution priorities: The sample error message reported in such cases is as follows:  Unable to honor user-specified block priorities in task 0 to schedule block execution with a discrete or continuous sample time
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 5, R2022b

Summary: undefined reference compilation failure when building model reference hierarchy with MinGW
Description: Consider a top model that references a middle model that in turn references a bottom model. When you build the top model, a compilation failure might occur if all of these conditions apply:? The selected compiler is MinGW.? The build process for the middle model includes custom source code. The bottom model calls a function that is implemented in the custom source code included for the middle model.  You might observe a linker error. For example: undefined reference to my_custom_code_function  .
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Using mixed signal and message ports in the same reference component in an architecture model might produce incorrect error messages
Description: Compiling an architecture model that contains reference components with mixed signal and message input or output ports might produce incorrect error messages.
Workaround: Instead of mixing signal and message ports in the same reference component, place all signal input or output ports in one reference component and message input or output ports in another reference component.

Summary: JMAAB check for na_0001?does not report?~?and?^?used in logical operations
Description: The?Sub-ID a? of Model Advisor?check?mathworks.jmaab.na_0001 (na_0001_a:?Usage of bitwise operators in Stateflow )? does not report warning for bitwise operators? ~ ? and?^  ?used in logical operations in C action language ?chart   with?Enable C-bit operations   disabled.

Summary: Design Verifier compatibility check fails for a multi-rate model containing bus element ports
Description: Ifa model contains function-call subsystems with distinct sample times and buselement ports, Design Verifier fails to construct the correct block replacement model,and compatibility checking fails.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 3, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Update to Stateflow output may generate incorrect code
Description: When an output of a Stateflow Chart is resolved to a Simulink.Signal  object with GetSet  storage class may lead to incorrect code generation.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2021b Update 2, R2022a

Summary: MATLAB can crash when calling getadjacencymatrix or when using the Model Debugger on a very large SimBiology model
Description: MATLAB can crash when calling?getadjacencymatrix  on a SimBiology model that contains hundreds of reactions using null as reactant or product. The?Model Debugger for SimBiology  also relies on getadjacencymatrix  and can crash when used with such a model.
Workaround: Replace null reactants and products with a dummy species. You can adapt the following code, which updates a SimBiology model stored in variable modelObj by replacing null with a dummy species named NULL, while ensuring the reaction rate remains the same.reactions = modelObj.Reactions;nullPattern = regexpPattern('\<null\>');for i = 1:numel(reactions)    reactionString = reactions(i).Reaction;    if contains(reactionString, nullPattern)        allSpecies = [reactions(i).Reactants; reactions(i).Products];        oneSpecies = allSpecies(1);        oneCompartment = oneSpecies.Parent;        nullSpecies = sbioselect(oneCompartment, 'Name', 'NULL');        if isempty(nullSpecies)            nullSpecies = oneCompartment.addspecies('NULL', 'Units', oneSpecies.Units);        end        nullSpeciesQualifiedName = nullSpecies.PartiallyQualifiedName;        reactionString = replace(reactionString, nullPattern, nullSpeciesQualifiedName);        rate = reactions(i).ReactionRate;        reactions(i).Reaction = reactionString;        reactions(i).ReactionRate = rate;    endend
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: MATLAB crashes when running rapid accelerator simulation of model that has dashboard block connected to output of enabled subsystem
Description: When you simulate a model in rapid accelerator mode, MATLAB crashes if the model contains an enabled subsystem with an output that terminates in a sink block and is connected to a dashboard block. The stack trace contains these lines:? [??0] 0x00007ffb9ed3de25 SLCompBlock::getCompOutputPortBufferIndex at b:\ matlab\ src\ simulinkblock\ blk_core\ slcompblock. cpp:1162   [??1] 0x00007ffba6dec2f7 locValidateSourcePortInAccelMode at b:\ matlab\ src\ sl_link_bd\ engine\ slruntimedataaccess. cpp:772  [??2] 0x00007ffba6de99d8 locGetExecBlockToDriveRTD at b:\ matlab\ src\ sl_link_bd\ engine\ slruntimedataaccess. cpp:1484   [??3] 0x00007ffba6de8fe1 locContructListener at b:\ matlab\ src\ sl_link_bd\ engine\ slruntimedataaccess. cpp:860   [??4] 0x00007ffba6deaac7 locRegisterRuntimeSignalAccess at b:\ matlab\ src\ sl_link_bd\ engine\ slruntimedataaccess. cpp:420  [??5] 0x00007ffba6dee109 SLLinkBd::registerRuntimeSignalAccess at b:\ matlab\ src\ sl_link_bd\ engine\ slruntimedataaccess. cpp:261

Summary: Unable to authorize code generation for encrypted protected model
Description: Authorizing code generation for an encrypted protected model might fail when the encrypted protected model includes a protected model report. The Password entry for model dialog box rejects passwords for code generation even when the correct password is entered. The corresponding error is: The supplied password is incorrect! Please try again.   As a result of being unauthorized, code generation fails when a model hierarchy uses this encrypted protected model.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Variant Subsystem with model block as choice may cause missing?initialization?in the generated code
Description: Variant Subsystem with model block as choice may cause missing initialization when referenced models drive root outports with constant sample time.? This issue can be reproduced when all below conditions are met:Variant Subsystem with code-compile  activation time. Variant Subsystem has model block as choices. Inside the referenced models of model block, we have blocks with constant sample time (such as Constant  block) directly connected to outport.  ?
Workaround: Create a wrapper atomic subsystem on the model choice within the Variant Subsystem.Add a signal conversion block at the output of the model choice which is fed from constant rate. This ensures that the output of the wrapper subsystem is fed from the output of the signal conversion block.

Summary: Unnecessary?.lib?and?.exp?files created in code generation folder when building model reference simulation target on Windows
Description: When you build a model reference simulation target on Windows, the build process creates modelName _msf.lib  and modelName _msf.exp  files in the code generation folder, in addition to the mex  file modelName _msf.mexw64 . The .lib  and .exp  files are not required.

Summary: Using UAV Scenario Configuration?block?in model throws error during simulating: "There should be only one active scenario configuration block in the model hierarchy..."?
Description: The following workflow leads to the error:? 1. Simulate model that contains UAV Scenario Configuration ?block? 2. Change?UAV Scenario Configuration ?block path to be alphabetically behind its previous block path. For example: Rename the UAV Scenario Configuration  block with a name that is alphabetically behind?the original block name. Move the UAV Scenario Configuration  block into a subsystem with name that is alphabetically behind?the original block name.  3. Simulating the model again would result in the following error:? "Caused by: There should be only one active scenario configuration block in the model hierarchy. ?The current active scenario configuration block is <BLOCKPATH>."?
Workaround: Run "clear all" in command line

Summary: Missing line number in table indexing error messages
Description: If your script or function includes an invalid indexing expression on a table or a timetable, then the error message displayed in the Command Window does not include the line number for the invalid expression. For example, if your file yourfunc.m  indexes into a table using a row number that is outside of the table, then this error message is displayed without a line number. >> yourfunc(t)Error using yourfuncRow index exceeds table dimensions.
