Workaround: To examine the value of the HKEY_CURRENT_USER Windows registry key and ensure that you have access to the location it specifies, use this Windows command.reg query "HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\User Shell Folders" /v PersonalThe command should produce output like this:HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\User Shell FoldersPersonal ? ?REG_EXPAND_SZ ? ?C:\Users\<username>\DocumentsIf the registry value does not point to a valid and accessible location, use this command to reset the value or point to a desired accessible location.reg add "HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\User Shell Folders" /v Personal /t REG_SZ /d "C:\Users\<username>\Documents" /f

Summary: Physical Signal blocks in a loop might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: Some combinations of Simscape Physical Signal blocks in a loop might cause MATLAB to crash during model compilation.
Workaround: Add a?PS Signal Specification block to the physical signal loop to prevent the crash.

Summary: Severed Tank (TL) block connections when opening models from earlier releases
Description: When first opening saved models from earlier releases, those containing the Tank (TL) block may have severed connections. This happens when a branching line connects to a Tank (TL) block port.
Workaround: Open the model in an earlier release to use as a reference, and reestablish the connections if needed.?

Summary: In SimBiology Model Builder you cannot drag and drop blocks from the palette onto the diagram
Description: In the SimBiology Model Builder  app, blocks cannot be dropped onto the diagram from the palette after creating a plot in the Simulation Tool.

Summary: Missing interface associations might not be cleared when you click Fix for owned interfaces with data elements typed by a shared interface
Description: When System Composer models report a warning that there are missing interface associations upon model load, the warning message contains the options to Clear  or Fix  the missing associations. If ports have locally-defined composite data interfaces with data elements that are typed by a missing shared interface, neither fix-it option will repair the missing associations.

Summary: The MaxStepSize and?InitialStepSize options of the dlode45 function have no effect.
Description: When you set the MaxStepSize  or InitialStepSize  options of the dlode45  function, the software uses the default values instead.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when converting a subsystem with Simscape blocks to a referenced model
Description: Converting a subsystem that contains Simscape blocks to a referenced model might cause MATLAB to crash when the Simulink Toolstrip updates its context.

Summary: Running analysis on subsystem harness model using? Simulink Design Verifier errors out and results in model incompatibility
Description: When an external subsystem harness is created using Simulink Test and the component under test contains Combinatorial Logic block  and Stateflow chart , Simulink Design Verifier errors with an error message Invalid Object Id  and deems the model incompatible.
Workaround: Convert the subsystem harness, and all other harnesses owned by the same harness owner model to internal, ORSet the Synchronization Mode of the harness to Synchronize on Push and Rebuild Only. The synchronization mode of a harness can be altered either by using the API sltest.harness.set or with the Harness Properties dialog.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation with enumeration and variant blocks with startup activation
Description: Consider the below example modelling scenario which can lead to incorrect code generation:classdef EngType < Simulink.IntEnumType??  ? ? ?enumeration?? ? ? ? ? ? ? Big(1),??? ? ? ? ? ? ? Small(2)?? ? ? end?end  Also, consider usage of Simulink.Parameter, like below:? V = Simulink.Parameter(int32(1))?V.CoderInfo.StorageClass = 'ExportedGlobal'?  and if Variant control is used as V==EngType.Small  and if? EngType_small  is created? as MATLAB variable in global workspace, then incorrect code generation is observed as : if (V == 1 || V == 2 ) { ? ? ?out? = < code for out>} Here the values of enums literals are inlined in the code, which is incorrect.

Summary: Error occurs when importing HERE HD Live Map data on Linux systems
Description: If you are using a Linux system, this error can occur when you try to import HERE HD Live Map data using the Scene Builder Tool : ERROR: There was an issue downloading data. Ensure you are connected to the internet.  Error Code: 9
Workaround: From a Linux terminal, navigate to your local installation folder of RoadRunner and enter these commands:1. cd bin/glnxa642. sudo ln -s libssl.so.1.0.0 libssl.soThe second command creates a symbolic link, libssl.so, that points to the file, libssl.so.1.0.0, because libssl.so is the expected OpenSSL library file name.

Summary: Model protection might delete some variables from data dictionaries
Description: Creating a protected model might delete some variables in the data dictionaries that are used by the model when all of the following conditions are true: In the Create Protected Model dialog box, Contents ?is set to?Protected Model (.slxp) and dependencies in a project . An open data dictionary references the data dictionaries that are used by the model being protected.  The variables that are not used by the protected model are deleted from the data dictionaries.
Workaround: Close all the open data dictionaries before model protection.?
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code that removes all edges from a graph or digraph may also erroneously remove all nodes
Description: If a graph or digraph has a non-zero number of edges and all of those edges are removed using the rmedge  method in generated code, then the nodes of the graph will also be removed. For example: g = graph(1,2); g = rmedge(g,1);?  Executing the above code in MATLAB returns a graph g  with 2 nodes. But if C or C++ code is generated and executed, then g  has 0 nodes.
Workaround: The correct graph (with no edges but all nodes intact) can be produced by replacing the graph or digraph with a new instance constructed with no edges. In this example the variable in contains the list of edges to remove from g. All elements of in are unique.if numel(in) == g.numedges  nodes = g.Nodes;  edges = g.Edges;  g = graph(edges(false,:),nodes);else  g = g.rmedge(in);end

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Using single-rate sharing/streaming and input pipelining on a subsystem that has a nD lookup table block can cause wrong answer
Description: A Modelsim simulation failure may occur if these conditions are met on a subsystem:  The subsystem contains a nD lookup table block with an input pipeline greater than 1 The sharing factor or streaming factor on the subsystem is set to a value greater than 1 Clock-rate pipelining is used for the subsystem

Summary: Unselect all the parameters in the parameter configuration dialog for standalone FMU export does not work
Description: In the parameter configuration dialog, unselect all the parameters will not work. It will still try to export all the parameters.

Summary: Data does not plot on map visualization in Simulation Data Inspector and Record block
Description: When you plot data on a map visualization in the Simulation Data Inspector or a Record block, the map does not display the route.

Summary: rosgenmsg fails to get CMake version on Windows
Description: When running rosgenmsg  on a Windows machine, you might see the following error: Generating MATLAB interfaces for custom message packages...Error using ros.internal.utilities.getCMakeBinaryPath (line 24)Could not find CMake in your system. Please install CMake version 3.15.5 or higher and rerun thecommand.Error in ros.internal.CatkinBuilder.getCMakePath (line 83)[aPath, aVersion] = ros.internal.utilities.getCMakeBinaryPath(ros.internal.CatkinBuilder.CMAKEMINVERSION);Error in ros.internal.ROSProjectBuilder (line 408)h.CMakePath = h.getCMakePath();Error in ros.internal.CatkinBuilder (line 25)h@ros.internal.ROSProjectBuilder(varargin{:});Error in rosgenmsg (line 287)builder = ros.internal.CatkinBuilder(genDir, pkgInfos{iPkg});

Summary: xcpDummyDoubleVariable is not a global variable?error from XCP-based external mode simulation
Description: An XCP-based external mode simulation might fail with the following message: ?External Mode Open Protocol Connect command failed  ?Caused by:  ? DWARF Parser internal error: Parser::describeSymbol xcpDummyDoubleVariable is not a global variable    A likely reason is that the linker optimizes away the variable xcpDummyDoubleVariable  from the external mode target application. Although the code generated from the model does not use the variable, Simulink requires the variable to connect and interact with the target application.
Workaround: If your toolchain provides an option that prevents a variable from being removed, modify the build configuration so that it specifies the option for xcpDummyDoubleVariable.

Summary: Error when executing an installer for a MATLAB Production Server deployable archive with Excel integration
Description: When executing an installer for a MATLAB Production Server deployable archive with Excel integration you might encounter one of two errors: System.ArgumentException: Illegal characters in path. ?? An error identifying a missing DLL or registry entry.  ?The first error is returned if the deployable archive was created using the Production Server Compiler app.? ?

Summary: MATLAB crashes when an interface in a System Composer model used as a type for an element in an owned interface is deleted
Description: When deleting a value type or data interface that is being used as the type for a data element in a System Composer model, MATLAB crashes. The data element must be an element of an owned data interface on a port.

Summary: Simulink model cannot be simulated in 'Export Simulink Model to Standalone FMU' example
Description: In Simulink Compiler example 'Export Simulink Model to Standalone FMU', fmudemo_export_fuelsys_top ?cannot be simulated due to the following error:? Invalid setting in? 'fmudemo_export_fuelsys_top/fuel_rate_control' for parameter 'PumpCon'.

Summary: Calling?findNodes?with a 3-D geometry where some of its face normals point inward?crashes MATLAB?
Description: MATLAB crashes when you call the findNodes  function to find mesh nodes for a geometry that has face normals pointing inwards. Typically, the face normals point outwards, away from the solid.
Workaround: Unzip the attached checkIncorrectNormals.zip?in the folder that contains the STL file. Run the following command in MATLAB:checkIncorrectNormals(<STLFileName>)This command throws an error message for STL files with incorrect normals. In this case, do one of the following:Change the face normal values in the STL file and then import the updated STL file.Recreate the STL file with the correct face normals in the software originally used to create the STL file.

Summary: MATLAB might crash or simulation result might be wrong when you use the From Workspace block to load large data
Description: MATLAB might crash or the simulation results might be wrong when you use the From Workspace block to load data where the product of the data type size in bytes and the number of time steps for which data is specified exceeds?2147483647. The crash or incorrect results occur when any of these conditions is met: Interpolation is disabled for the From Workspace block. Zero crossing detection is enabled for the From Workspace block. The From Workspace block is configured to holdthe final workspace data value after loading the last sample in the workspacedata, and the simulation includes simulation times outside of the time rangefor which input data values are defined in the workspace data.
Workaround: Use a root-level Inport block to load the input data.

Summary: System-Level Condenser Evaporator (2P-MA) and System-Level Heat Exchanger (2P-2P) blocks allow nonphysical parameter values
Description: When modeling systems using the System-Level Condenser Evaporator (2P-MA) or System-Level Heat Exchanger (2P-2P) block, you can expose the Nominal rate of heat transfer ?parameter?by setting Heat transfer capacity specification ?to Rate of heat transfer . ?In this configuration, these blocks do not generate an error when you to specify nonphysical values where the outlet temperature of the hot fluid is less than the outlet temperature of the cold fluid.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code might be incorrect for a model with Product of Elements block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a Product of Elements block with CollapseMode  set to All dimensions .? Output data type differs from the input data type for Product of Elements block.  The code generated results in loss of precision when the elements are multiplied.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 1, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Import Tool may incorrectly interpret numeric values in comma-separated files on systems with Decimal Separator set to comma?
Description: The Import Tool incorrectly interprets numerical values in text files when the following conditions are true: The system Decimal Separator is comma The delimeter used when importing the file is set to comma?
Workaround: In the Import Tool, go to "Delimiter Options", and change the Decimal Separator to period.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: Simulink might crash when compiling models that use Model blocks and Simulink.LookupTable objects
Description: Simulink might crash when compiling a model that uses Model blocks and Simulink.LookupTable  objects. This crash might occur under these conditions:? The Simulink.LookupTable  objects are created in the base workspace. The model is opened and closed intermittently while the lookup table objects remain in the base workspace.  The generated stack trace contains lines similar to: <matlabroot>\bin\win64\sl_prm_engine.dll+00939716 SlBaseObject::setOutOfDateInAllBDs+00000036<matlabroot>\bin\win64\sl_prm_engine.dll+00935791 SlBaseObject::lateBinding+00000943<matlabroot>\bin\win64\sl_prm_engine.dll+01646145 SlStructType::doLateBinding+00000865<matlabroot>\bin\win64\sl_prm_engine.dll+00935415 SlBaseObject::lateBinding+00000567...?<matlabroot>\bin\win64\libmwsimulink.dll+12854307 SlBdDomainListener::~SlBdDomainListener+00012835

Summary: Additional Alias type named ZCSigState generated in PLC code
Description: If the Simulink Model contains a Delay  block with an external reset in a subsystem that is configured to preserve Alias types in the generated code, the code will might contain a type called ZCSigState  which is not defined anywhere.  If Preserve Alias Types  option is unchecked in the PLC Code Generation settings then the code will not contain ZCSigState , instead it would get replaced by USINT.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Using gpucoder.reduce with the optional dimension argument might produce incorrect results for row-major inputs
Description: When performing GPU optimized reduction along a specified dimension using gpucoder.reduce , the generated code might produce incorrect results when the inputs are in row-major layout.   This is an example of the syntax that might trigger this issue:?  [out1,out2, ...] = gpucoder.reduce(...,'dim',reductionDim);?

Summary: Error message text does not wrap in the Command Window
Description: When an error with a long message is thrown, the text of the error message in the report will not wrap to the Command Window. This happens for long messages having trailing whitespace. ?>>?msg = repmat('my long message ', 1, 20); >> error('my:id', msg);? % Message text does not wrap, because it contains trailing whitespace.
Workaround: Ensure that the message text does not end with whitespace.>> msg = strip(msg);?>> error('my:id', msg);? % Message text now wraps, since it does not contain trailing whitespace.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Inconsistent simulation and code generation results when a periodic Function-Call Subsystem is inside an export-function model and the subsystem has blocks using absolute or elapsed time
Description: Simulation or code generation produces incorrect results as silent wrong answer when the following conditions are true: A periodic Function-Call Subsystem is inside an export-function model. Inside the subsystem there are blocks using absolute or elapsed time (for example, digital clock block). The subsystem is executed conditionally (not based on every sample hit).   The strict periodic function call check should be enforced when a periodic function-call subsystem is inside an export-function model and the subsystem contains blocks with absolute or elapsed sample. This means the subsystem must be executed on every sample time hit when it's in an export-function model.

Summary: MATLAB may crash when linearizing a model that contains a block substitution within a multi-instanced model reference hierarchy
Description: Substituted or tunable blocks can cause MATLAB to crash when linearizing Simulink models using?linearize ,?slLinearizer , or?slTuner . MATLAB crashes when you linearize a model in which all of the following conditions are true:  The model contains a model reference hierarchy at least two levels deep, that is, a model reference that contains another model reference. At least one of the model references is referenced more than once (multi-instanced) and simulated in normal mode. At least one substituted or tunable block is inside a model reference.

Summary: Polyspace as You Code extension in Visual Studio Code fails to start on Linux systems
Description: When you open a Visual Studio Code (VSCode) instance on a Linux system, the Polyspace as You Code extension fails to start. If you go to the OUTPUT ?pane and select Polyspace as You Code from the drop-down, you see an error message similar to this: Could not link to common plugin library! This issue occurs when the version of the GNU C++ standard library (libstdc++.so ) installed on the machine is older than the version that Polyspace as You Code expects.
Workaround: There are 3 workarounds for this issue depending on whether you can upgrade your Linux system and, if not, use VSCode locally or remotely. In the following steps, POLYSPACEROOT is the path to your Polyspace as You Code installation folder, for instance /usr/local/PolyspaceAsYouCode/R2021bIf you can upgrade your? Linux system:Upgrade libstdc++.so to libstdc++.so.6.0.25 or more recent.If You Use VSCode Locally:In this mode, VSCode and the files that you want to analyze are on the same machine:Exit all VSCode instances on the machine.Export the following environment variable:export LD_LIBRARY_PATH="$POLYSPACEROOT/polyspace/sys/os/glnxa64"Use this command to check that LD_LIBRARY_PATH lists several libraries including libstdc++.so:ls "$LD_LIBRARY_PATH"Start VSCode from the shell where LD_LIBRARY_PATH was exported.If the problem persists, check which version of libstdc++.so is loaded in the VSCode extension host process:Get the PID of the extension host. For instance, in a bash shell use this command:PID=$(ps aux | grep code | grep extensionHost | awk '{print$2}')Use the PID to list the loaded shared objects of the corresponding process and check that the?libstdc++.so?path is the same as the one set in LD_LIBRARY_PATH:cat /proc/$PID/maps | grep libstdIf You Use VSCode with remote-ssh:In this mode, you open VSCode on your local machine and use remote-ssh to connect to another machine where the files that you want to analyze are located.Start VSCode and establish a remote-ssh connection.On the remote-machine open a shell to identify the path of the server.sh script used to start the VSCode server:ps aux | grep vscode-server | grep server.shThe command outputs something similar to thisjdoe? ?195100? 0.0? 0.0? ?2388? ?764 ?? ? ? ? S? ? 16:54? ?0:00 sh /home/jdoe/.vscode-server/bin/899d46d82c4c95423fb7e10e68eba52050e30ba3/server.sh --start-server --host=127.0.0.1 ...Open file?server.sh?in a text editor and set?LD_LIBRARY_PATH?as shown below:#!/usr/bin/env sh## Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.#case "$1" in? ? ? ? ?--inspect*) INSPECT="$1"; shift;;esacROOT="$(dirname "$0")"# Add following line:export LD_LIBRARY_PATH=$POLYSPACEROOT/polyspace/sys/os/glnxa64$ROOT/node" ${INSPECT:-} "$ROOT/out/vs/server/main.js" "$@"Exit VSCode on the local machine.Kill all vscode-server processes on the remote machine:ps aux | awk '/vscode-server/ { print $2}' | xargs killRestart VSCode on the local machine and re-establish the remote-ssh connection.

Summary: ModelAdvisor.run does not populate the Type field correctly in generated results
Description: The ModelAdvisor.run  function returns analysis results in a ModelAdvisor.SystemResult  object. This object contains a field Type , meant to denote the type of system that was input to ModelAdvisor.run . It could be either a library, model or subsystem. The Type  field is not updated correctly when calling the ModelAdvisor.run  function.

Summary: In the Live Editor, displayed matrices are incorrect when display format is a style other than short
Description: In the Live Editor, when you display matrices in a format other than the default display format short , then matrices are still displayed in the?short?format and do not match the Command Window display.

Summary: Help button in parameter dialog does not open documentation for EtherCAT Get Device State block and EtherCAT Set Device State block
Description: For the EtherCAT Get Device State block and the EtherCAT Set Device State block, the help button in the parameter dialog box does not open the reference page for the block.
Workaround: Use the links in the R2021b Simulink Real-Time release note to open the documentation for these blocks.

Summary: AMI models to support clock forwarding do not always display receiver jitter
Description: When simulating with AMI models that support clock forwarding, if the AMI parameter Rx_Use_Clock_Input ?is set to Times  or Waves , the statistical results do not include Rx jitter. The incorrect results include the statistical eye diagram, inner/outer eye contours, and associated eye metrics such as BER, inner eye height and inner eye width. The time domain results are still correct.

Summary: Attributeslost after updating requirements imported from IBM DOORS Next
Description: When you import requirements from IBM DOORS Next, requirement attributes are imported as requirement properties or custom attributes in Simulink Requirements. When you update requirements in the imported requirement set, the attribute values are not imported and previously known values disappear from the imported data.

Summary: Incorrect Unsatisfiable?or Dead Logic objectives for Simulink Function with multiple callers
Description: If you have Simulink Functions with multiple callers, incorrect?Unsatisfiable  (or? Dead Logic ) ?objectives may be reported for the blocks inside the Simulink Function  when: Atleast one Unsatisfiable  objective is associated with any one of the multiple callers for any of the Simulink blocks inside the Simulink Function . In such scenarios, the Unsatisfiable  objective may be generalized as though it were Unsatisfiable  for all callers. Unsatisfiable  objectives that occur for Stateflow , MATLAB Function  Blocks, and MATLAB System  Blocks inside of the Simulink Function  are not affected.

Summary: MATLAB crash in function GetSortedList of module sl_mcos_api
Description: MATLAB might crash with a stack trace that contains a line similar to:?[  0] 0x00007fffe1d3134f <matlabroot>\bin\win64\sl_mcos_api.dll+00529231 GetSortedList+00000063

Summary: Unexpected behaviors when using Widebus capabilities in Parallel Link Designer app
Description: When using Widebus capabilities in the Parallel Link Designer  app results in unexpected behaviors. These behaviors include, but not limited to:? - Changing designator properties (name, part, model, or type) in sheet associated with Widebus sheet. - Projects with more than one Widebus sheet. - Deleting designators not using the Widebus Groups dialog. - Deleting victim designators on Widebus sheet.? - Copying a Widebus sheet.? - Sweeping length of a w-line on a Widebus sheet.? - Sweeping model in sheet associated with Widebus sheet.? - Wrong receiver model used when clock forwarding on a Widebus sheet.  As a result, you might observe: - Exceptions in the GUI - Errors when simulating - Invalid simulation results?

Summary: Build failure for a model that uses half-precision data type
Description: When you use half-precision data type with the?Casting modes ?model configuration parameter set to Standards Compliant , the build process might fail with this error:  Y:\to_zap\misra\codegen\dll\xHalfStorageClass\xHalfStorageClass.c(55): error C2440: 'type cast': cannot convert from 'real16_T' to 'real16_T' Y:\to_zap\misra\codegen\dll\xHalfStorageClass\xHalfStorageClass.c(55): error C2198: 'halfToFloat': too few arguments for call NMAKE : fatal error U1077: '"C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\2019\Professional\VC\Tools\MSVC\14.27.29110\bin\HostX64\x64\cl.EXE"' : return code '0x2' Stop. The make command returned an error of 2

Summary: Polyspace Access fails to start after upgrading to R2021b?from R2020b or R2021a
Description: Polyspace Access might fail to start after you upgrade to release R2021b from release R2020b or R2021a. The issue occurs if you configured the User Manager  to use the internal directory to manage users in these prior releases. When you inspect the logs for the User Manager Server  and the?User Manager DB  containers, you see messages similar to these: User Manager Server    pq: update or delete on table "identity" violates foreign key constraint "password_id_source_fkey" on table "password" User Manager DB    2021-09-15 19:51:35.452 UTC [34] ERROR:??update or delete on table "identity" violates foreign key constraint "password_id_source_fkey" on table "password"  2021-09-15 19:51:35.452 UTC [34] DETAIL:??Key (id, source)=(admin, mw.umdb) is still referenced from table "password".
Workaround: To fix the issue, follow these steps:?Download the attached SQL script to the host machine where you installed Polyspace Access.Copy the script into the User Manager DB container by running the following command:   docker cp <your download path>/g2584352_ebr_fix.sql usermanager-db-main:/tmpThe User Manager DB container must be running before you execute this command.?<your download path> is the path of the folder when you download the script in step 1.Run the SQL script in the?User Manager DB?container:  docker exec -it usermanager-db-main psql --username um --dbname umdb -f /tmp/g2584352_ebr_fix.sqlIf you changed the default value (um) for the Internal directory database username setting, enter that new value for the --username option.On the Cluster Dashboard page, click Restart Apps.You do not need to run the script again if, later, you restart or delete all the apps.

Summary: trainNetwork throws an error when training a sequence-to-sequence network with datastores that output cell arrays.
Description: When training a sequence-to-sequence network (for example, a network with LSTM layers all with OutputMode  set to "sequence" ), the trainNetwork  function throws an error for datastores that output cell arrays: Error using trainNetwork (line 184)Invalid training data. For a network with 1 inputs and 1 output, the datastore read function must return a cell array with 2 columns, but it returns an cell array with 1 columns.
Workaround: Convert datastores that return cell arrays to datastores that return tables using the transform function.For example, to transform the datastore ds that outputs cell arrays to a datastore that outputs tables, useds = transform(ds,@table);Some datastores may require using a custom transform function that performs additional processing, such as datastores with mutliple outputs.

Summary: Simulink Test qualification test suites tBLT and tCustCritTol produce unexpected results
Description: The following Simulink Test qualification test cases might incorrectly fail due to an issue with the test case callbacks. In addition, the baseline results do not match the expected results. SLT_AST_066  in tBLT.mldatx  SLT_AST_010_AEQ  in tCustCritTol.mldatx  SLT_AST_030_AEQ  in tCustCritTol.mldatx  SLT_AST_130_AEQ  in tCustCritTol.mldatx
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: Warning might occur and data might not log to MAT file when model loads workspace data using Inport blocks
Description: When you simulate a model that loads workspace data using Inport blocks and has the Log Dataset data to file ?parameter enabled, data does not log to the specified MAT file and this warning occurs: Logging to MAT file not supported for models that include From File or To File blocks or stream input data from a file. Save data logged to the workspace to a MAT file using the save function after simulation.
Workaround: To avoid the warning, clear the Log Dataset data to file?parameter and remove any To File or From File blocks from the model.To create a MAT file that contains the logged data, use the save function to save the data logged to the workspace to a MAT file after the simulation completes.

Summary: MATLAB might crash during code generation when a model contains a parameter with symbolic dimensions that has non-ASCII characters
Description: MATLAB might crash during code generation when a model contains a parameter with symbolic dimensions that has non-ASCII characters such as for example, '\n' .
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Cell indexing with smooth parenthesis ()?inside?coder.cstructname?or?coder.varsize?might cause MATLAB to crash with message "In with_size: Assertion failed: !isCell()"
Description: When using coder.cstructname  or coder.varsize ?with cell array argument , cell indexing with smooth parentheses ()  might cause MATLAB to crash with the assertion: In with_size: Assertion failed: !isCell()  Example MATLAB code lines that might trigger this assertion: coder.varsize('c1(:)',[1 10]);? ? ? ? ? ?% c1 is a cell array  coder.cstructname(c2(:),'myStructName'); % c2 is a cell array
Workaround: Use content indexing with curly braces {}?to access the cell array elements, or no indexing to access the cell array itself.For example, to make the elements of the cell array?c1?variable-size, or to name the first element of the cell array?c2:coder.varsize('c1{:}', [1 10]);? ? ? ? ? ?% c1 is a cell arraycoder.cstructname(c2{1}, 'myStructName'); % c2 is a cell arrayAlternatively, to make the cell array?c1?itself variable-size, or to name the cell array?c2?itself:coder.varsize('c1', [1 10]);? ? ? ? ? ?% c1 is a cell arraycoder.cstructname(c2, 'myStructName'); % c2 is a cell array

Summary: Generated code for deep learning network with transposedConvolution2dLayer?might throw a runtime error for cuDNN target library on the Windows platform
Description: Generated code for deep learning network with?transposedConvolution2dLayer ?might throw a runtime error for cuDNN target library on the Windows platform.  For example, running the Generate Digit Images on NVIDIA GPU Using Variational Autoencoder ?example on the Windows platform throws the following error when executing the generated MEX: CUDA error 700 (illegal memory access)?
Workaround: In the cuDNN deep learning code configuration object, set the AutoTuning?property to false.For example:??cfg = coder.gpuConfig('mex');cfg.DeepLearningConfig = coder.DeepLearningConfig('cudnn');cfg.DeepLearningConfig.AutoTuning = false;?

Summary: Metrics Dashboard and model metrics API use incorrect bin edges for metric distributions when the metric results are close in value
Description: If metric results are close in value, the Metrics Dashboard and model metrics API place the results into a single distribution bin, but use a bin edge that is less than the maximum result value. For example, consider a model that has 2 blocks at the top level and a subsystem that contains 8 blocks. When you collect the metric for calculating the number of blocks in the model, the metric result values are 2 top-level and 8 subsystem-level blocks, but when you get the metric distribution, the bin edges are 2 and 7.
Products: Requirements Toolbox, Simulink Check, Simulink Coverage

Summary: Rapid accelerator or Simulink Compiler deployed simulation stalls after an error in postStepFcn callback
Description: The stall happens only when simulation is running in rapid accelerator mode, or when it is running in an executable compiled with Simulink Compiler. The conditions for the stall are: Simulation is running with a Simulink.SimulationInput  object as input to the sim command, i.e. out = sim( simInput ) ; The specified Simulink.SimulationInput  object has a postStepFcn  set on it After the last simulation step is done, the postStepFcn  callback function errors (this is the user-authored MATLAB code)
Workaround: Ensure that the postStepFcn callback does not create an error. For example, use a try-catch statement around the body of the function.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect answer may be generated with models that have blocks with long parameter names
Description: If a block in a model has a long parameter name and there are either multiple instances of that model in the root model or there are multiple different referenced model blocks with that block, then generating the code for the root model may result in incorrect answers from the executable generated from the generated code. If there is not enough room within the identifier length (default 31 characters) for each parameter name to be fully mangled, the code generator generates instances with conflicting parameter names.
Workaround: Increase the default length of the identifier from 31 to a higher value.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 2, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Some props from the Asset library may appear skewed or rotated when imported into Unreal.
Description: When importing RoadRunner scenes into Unreal, some prop assets from the Asset Library may be skewed or on their sides.? Reproduction Steps:  Create a new scene in a project that includes RoadRunner Asset Library?? Drag a building Prop from the Assets/Buildings folder into the scene Scale and rotate the building? Export to Unreal? Import your scene into Unreal?
Workaround: Customers can use third party 3D modeling software to fix the up axis on the exported scene

Summary: Unexpected error from sbiofit during fitting when variants have no content
Description: If all variants specified in the Variants  name-value argument of sbiofit  have no content, the fit might error with a message like the following: All fits failed while performing an unpooled fit in SBIOFIT.    Caused by:    An error occurred while fitting group "a".        Index in position 1 exceeds array bounds.    An error occurred while fitting group "b".        Index in position 1 exceeds array bounds.    An error occurred while fitting group "c".        Index in position 1 exceeds array bounds.
Workaround: Omit the Variants name-value argument, or specify the value as [].

Summary: Incorrect prediction results for some custom bitstream configurations.
Description: The FPGA prediction returns incorrect results for?custom bitstreams that have a high ConvThreadNumber ?(64 and above) and a low FCThreadNumber ?(8 and below).
Workaround: Try decreasing the ConvThreadNumber?or increasing the FCThreadNumber?of their custom processor configurations.?Bitstreams with a high ConvThreadNumber?(64 and above) provide numerical accuracy when the FCThreadNumber?is at least as large as the square root of the ConvThreadNumber.??If the ConvThreadNumber?is 64, FCThreadNumber?of 8 and above will provide numerical accuracy.??If the ConvThreadNumber?is 256, FCThreadNumber?of 16 and above will provide numerical accuracy.

Summary: fsolve exceeds the default MaxFunctionEvaluations?tolerance.
Description: When calling  fsolve  with no options argument [x,fval,exitflag] = fsolve(fun,x0);?  ?fsolve  can exceed the default?MaxFunctionEvaluations  tolerance  (100*numberOfVariables). ?
Workaround: To ensure that?fsolve honors the?MaxFunctionEvaluations tolerance,?pass a default options object.options = optimoptions('fsolve');[x,fval,exitflag] = fsolve(fun,x0,options);?

Summary: JMAAB check for na_0002:?sub-checks do not correspond to correct sub-ids
Description: Model Advisor?check?mathworks.jmaab.na_0002 (Check fundamental logical and numerical operations) ?sub-check  a  incorrectly corresponds to the sub-id  b  of the guideline and vice-versa.

Summary: The customization done in sl_customization for leaf blocks did not have an effect in Simulink Quick Insert.
Description: The function sl_customization  allows you to hide blocks and containers from Library Browser. You can specify the path of the container or leaf block with the keyword Hidden to hide.?For example: %To hide the leaf block Constant in Library Browser cm.LibraryBrowserCustomizer.applyFilter({'Simulink/Sources/Constant','Hidden'}); %To hide all the blocks in the container Sinks.cm.LibraryBrowserCustomizer.applyFilter({'Simulink/Sinks','Hidden'}); The behavior is as expected in Library Browser, but the customizations done in sl_customization  for leaf blocks did not have an effect on Simulink Quick Insert, as a result the blocks appear in the result set.

Summary: Error might occur when opening a Simulation Data Inspector session file that contains a sparklines plot
Description: When you open a Simulation Data Inspector session file that contains a sparklines plot, this error occurs if the MLDATX file was saved using a Save As operation:? Error using open (line 143) STD exception 'class mwboost::exception_detail::ctone_impl<class? mwboost:unknown_exception>': 'Unknown exception' was caught?

Summary: Stubbed constructs are incorrectly treated as returning the same value each time they are called in that time step
Description: When a stubbed function is read inside of a loop or inside of a function that is called multiple times per time step, the stubbed function returns the same value each time it is called for that time step.For example, if you had a MATLAB Function block with the following code: for i = 1:10  y(i) = rand();end the rand() ?incorrectly returns the same value for each iteration of the loop.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a

Summary: Deep learning networks fail to deploy to Arria10 SoC over Ethernet
Description: When deploying a deep learning network to the Arria10 SoC over Ethernet using the "deploy" function,  it fails with the error: Error using dnnfpga. hardware. Target/ connectToBitstream (line 224)  Connection to the bitstream is no longer valid caused by error:Failed to find device: mwipcore_ddr0:mm2s0.
Workaround: You can use the JTAG interface instead of Ethernet to deploy your deep learning network

Summary: Reading large data strings with?writeread?on a VISA-GPIB connection?might miss some samples
Description: For certain instruments, the data returned by the?writeread  function on a VISA-GPIB connection might miss some samples. This can happen when periodic polling of the instrument for available data interrupts existing data transfers.
Products: Industrial Communication Toolbox, Instrument Control Toolbox

Summary: Upload to Polyspace Access fails after database restore
Description: When you restore the Polyspace Access database from a backup, subsequent uploads to Polyspace Access might fail.?? The log of the Polyspace Access ETL  container contains an error message similar to this: -!> [09-09-2021 at 13:51:32.135]-[com.polyspace.util.io.FolderWatchDog] *.datasource.etl.UploadManager.manageStoredRun(...) at 598| ClassCastException: Cannot cast java.lang.Integer to java.lang.Long

Description: MATLAB crashes when a variant parameter object with startup activation time has a variant condition expression that contains an integer type of variant control variable and enumerated type of operands?For example, consider a Simulink model uses kv  with following definition:V = Simulink.VariantControl('Value', 1, 'ActivationTime', 'startup');??kv = Simulink.VariantVariable('Choices', {'V == EngType.Small', 3, 'V == EngType.Big', 6});?  Generating code for the Simulink model with Simulink coder or Embedded Coder will result in MATLAB crash with stack trace similar to: Abnormal termination:Access violation? Summary: JMAAB check for?jc_0772?does not report transitions with c, c++ comments
Description: Model Advisor?check Check usage of transition conditions in Stateflow transitions? (id:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0772)? does not report warning for transitions with symbols  //   and  /* */   used in C action language chart .

Summary: C/C++ code generated from trackerJPDA might fail with the error: Size overflow error on dimension 2
Description: The JPDA tracker bounds the number of clusters to the smaller of: Maximum number of detections, specified by the?MaxNumDetections ?property Maximum number of tracks, specified by the?MaxNumTracks ?property  When multiple sensors feed detections from a large number (close to the bound) of tracks to the tracker, the number of clusters generated in the tracker can exceed this limit. In this case, the generated code might fail with the error message:? "Size overflow error on dimension 2" .

Summary: Attitude Profile?block might output quaternion rotation that does not maintain alignment of primary alignment vector with primary constraint vector under certain conditions
Description: The?Attitude Profile ?block might output a quaternion rotation that does not maintain alignment of the primary alignment vector with the primary constraint vector, depending on the secondary alignment and constraint vectors defined in the block. This issue is observable when you use the block output as an input to the block in a feedback loop that does not contain a controller.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect Stateflow animation displayed for multi-instanced model reference
Description: When a Stateflow block is used in a multi-instanced model reference, the state highlighting and animation should show the result from the chart in the currently visible model. Instead, the state highlighting might show the result from a different instance.

Summary: JMAAB check for?jc_0009?does not report?subsystems that do not have propagated signal labels
Description: The?Sub-ID a? of Model Advisor?check?jc_0009_a:?Check Signal name propagation for subsystems?(id:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0009)  does not?report warning for? subsystems that do not have propagated signal labels. This issue happens when the setting to display propagated signal labels for all signals (Diagnostics ->Signals ->Propagated Signal Labels)  is turned off  after enabling it once .

Summary: JMAAB check for?jc_0009?does not report subsystems with RTWSystemCode parameter set as Auto
Description: The?Sub-ID a? of Model Advisor?check jc_0009_a:?Check Signal name propagation for subsystems?(id:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0009)? does not report warning for? subsystems with RTWSystemCode  parameter set as Auto  . This is incorrect as subsystems where reusable functions   are set only can be excluded as per the guideline.

Summary: Running the model in rapid accelerator mode generates an error with MinGW compiler on Windows
Description: A Simscape model can error out during simulation if the following conditions are true:? The model is running on Windows and using MinGW compiler? The model is running in rapid accelerator mode Simscape logging is enabled for the model  The following error is generated because of a missing library: Unable to build a standalone executable to simulate the model <model_name> in rapid accelerator mode.?
Workaround: The possible workarounds are :1. To log the data for Simscape blocks in rapid accelerator mode, use the?mex -setup?command?and choose the Microsoft Visual C++ compiler.2. If you have to use the MinGW compiler and run the model in rapid accelerator mode, disable Simscape logging. Open?the model Configuration Parameters dialog box, go to the Simscape pane, and set?Log simulation data to None.

Summary: Passing static class method to?coder.getArgTypes might produce an error
Description: Suppose that: The MATLAB class my_class  has a static method ArgumentTypeTest ??  This static method calls your code generation entry-point function  my_fun   If you attempt to determine the input types of?my_fun ?by running this command, you might get an error:types = coder.getArgTypes('my_class.ArgumentTypeTest', 'my_fun')?  The error message is: Error evaluating 'my_class.ArgumentTypeTest'.?  Caused by:  Unrecognized function or variable 'tbc'.
Workaround: Create a wrapper function that calls the static method. Pass this wrapper function to?coder.getArgTypes.?For example, create this wrapper function around the?ArgumentTypeTest?function used in the description section:function ArgumentTypeTestWrappermy_class.ArgumentTypeTest();endPass the wrapper function ArgumentTypeTestWrapper to coder.getArgTypes.types = coder.getArgTypes('ArgumentTypeTestWrapper', 'my_fun')

Summary: Code generation for deep learning network with nnet.keras.layer.FlattenCStyleLayer or nnet.onnx.layer.FlattenLayer might cause MATLAB crash
Description: Code generation for a deep learning network with?nnet.keras.layer.FlattenCStyleLayer ?or?nnet.onnx.layer.FlattenLayer ?might cause MATLAB to crash if the output of these layers?is added or concatenated with another input of ' SSC(B) ' format by using an  additionLayer ,  depthConcatenationLayer  or  concatenationLayer .

Summary: Error when importing requirements from IBM DOORS Next
Description: When you import requirements from IBM DOORS Next, you might see this error message: Cannot modify an unresolved proxy . When this error occurs, the?import process might fail to complete and the Requirements Editor  does not refresh. You can close the error message and close the Requirements Editor  and re-open it. The imported requirement set is visible but some properties might remain unassigned.

Summary: parquetread returns the wrong datetime values when importing TIMESTAMP columns with associated time zones
Description: parquetread ?returns the wrong datetime ?values when importing columns whose physical type is either?TIMESTAMP_MILLIS ?or?TIMESTAMP_MICROS ?and has an associated time zone. The datetime  arrays returned by parquetread  are shifted by the UTC offset associated with the time zone. See the example below for more details. Create a datetime  array whose TimeZone  is set to "America/New_York" . >> NewYorkDatetimes = datetime(2021, 11, 3, 9, 0, 0, "TimeZone", "America/New_York") + hours(0:5)'; >> t = table(NewYorkDatetimes) t =  6x1 table     NewYorkDatetimes     ____________________    03-Nov-2021 09:00:00   03-Nov-2021 10:00:00   03-Nov-2021 11:00:00   03-Nov-2021 12:00:00   03-Nov-2021 13:00:00   03-Nov-2021 14:00:00>> parquetwrite("example.parquet", t); The variable NewYorkDatetimes  is shifted up by 4 hours, which is the UTC offset for America/New_York . >> t2 = parquetread("example.parquet")  t2 =  6ˇÁ1 table     NewYorkDatetimes     ____________________   03-Nov-2021 13:00:00   03-Nov-2021 14:00:00   03-Nov-2021 15:00:00   03-Nov-2021 16:00:00   03-Nov-2021 17:00:00   03-Nov-2021 18:00:00  The corresponding values in t2  and t  are all off by 4 hours.  >> t2.NewYorkDatetimes - t.NewYorkDatetimesans =   6ˇÁ1 duration array   04:00:00   04:00:00   04:00:00   04:00:00   04:00:00   04:00:00
Workaround: To correct the issue, you can use tzoffset to compute the duration values required to undo the UTC offset applied to the datetime values.>> t = parquetread("example.parquet");  >> offset = tzoffset(t.NewYorkDatetimes);>> t.NewYorkDatetimes = t.NewYorkDatetimes + offset  t =  6ˇÁ1 table      NewYorkDatetimes    ____________________     03-Nov-2021 09:00:00    03-Nov-2021 10:00:00    03-Nov-2021 11:00:00    03-Nov-2021 12:00:00    03-Nov-2021 13:00:00    03-Nov-2021 14:00:00

Summary: Unable to select Global Configuration when configuring MATLAB session for IBM DOORS Next integration
Description: When you use?slreq.dngConfigure ?to configure? a MATLAB session for IBM DOORS Next integration, you select a project and configuration to work with. However, you can only select a local configuration to work with and cannot select a global configuration.

Summary: Polyspace report generation fails when using custom template
Description: When you generate a Polyspace report that uses a custom code metrics template which applies a filter on file metrics, the report generation might fail with an error similar to this:? In component Code Metrics Summary : Index in position 1 exceeds array bounds. Index must not exceed 1.?

Summary: RoadRunner might crash when using the Slip Road Tool.
Description: RoadRunner might crash if you use the Slip Road Tool, often when dragging a new slip road onto a junction.? This is a common action when creating junctions with slip lanes or freeway offramps.

Summary: Gas properties plot does not display data when the temperature vector values do not exactly match the valid temperature range
Description: The right-click context menu plot for the Gas Properties (G) block does not display data when: Gas specification  is Semiperfect  Valid temperature range parameterization  is  Specified minimum and maximum temperatures  The value of Minimum valid temperature ?is not equal to the first element of Temperature vector ?or t he value of?Maximum valid temperature ?is?not equal to?the last element of?Temperature vector
Workaround: Adjust the parameter values so that?the?Minimum valid temperature?and?Maximum valid temperature?match?the first and last elements of the?Temperature vector, respectively.

Summary: sum or prod with the?"omitnan" option may fail to return computed NaN values
Description: When sum  or prod  are called using the "omitnan"  option, all elements of the input array that are NaN (Not-a-Number) are ignored. However, if the computation of adding or multiplying the non-NaN elements of the input array results in a NaN, this value should still be returned. For example,?sum([NaN Inf -Inf], "omitnan")  returns a computed NaN value.  In some rare cases, instead of such a computed NaN being returned, another number is returned. The cases in which this bug occurs may vary from release to release and also based on the number of threads used, as these factors can change the internal choice of how the data is split into blocks during the calculation. After R2016b, this bug also affects the nansum  function.

Summary: Generated cosimulation model fails to load if the design has a large number of outputs
Description: The HDL Coder product allows for generation of an HDL cosimulation test bench model while generating HDL code.? This option is described in Generate a Cosimulation Model .? The generated cosimulation model has a scope to compare each design output with the corresponding output from the HDL cosimulation block.?When the design includes a large number of outputs, the generated cosimulation model may fail to open and may crash the machine because it runs out of resources trying to open all of the scopes.

Summary: Using VISA-related functionality on Intel-based macOS Big Sur 11.4 or 11.5.2?might crash MATLAB
Description: MATLAB might crash on Intel-based macOS Big Sur 11.4 or 11.5.2 if the Instrument Control Toolbox Support Package for National Instruments VISA and ICP Interfaces is installed. Impacted MATLAB releases install NI-VISA 20.0 with this support package. The crash can occur when using any of the following VISA-related functions, apps, or Simulink blocks: visa  visadev  visadevlist  instrhwinfo  Test and Measurement Tool Query Instrument?block To Instrument?block  For example, the following line of code crashes MATLAB: instrhwinfo("visa","ni")
Workaround: To work around this issue, use the following steps to install the NI-VISA 21.0 driver on your Intel-based macOS Big Sur 11.4 or 11.5.2 machine:Download and install NI-VISA 21.0 from NI-VISA version 21.0 for macOS.Restart your machine, as directed by the NI installer.Allow NI-VISA to load system extensions by following the instructions for "System software blocked" from the?macOS User Guide.Apply the workaround for NI bug number 1555478 mentioned in the NI documentation. Upgrading to NI-VISA 21.0 alone will not solve the issue. See https://www.ni.com/en-us/support/documentation/bugs/21/ni-visa-21-0-known-issues.html.Restart your machine, as directed by the NI installer.
Products: Industrial Communication Toolbox, Instrument Control Toolbox
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: Generate mapped fuel cell calibration button produces a warning
Description: When you click the Generate mapped fuel cell calibration button, a warning occurs. This warning does not affect generating the mapped fuel cell calibration.

Summary: New Library Browser customizations do not take effect when Library Browser is first opened
Description: When the Library Browser is customized in an sl_customization.m  file for the first time before the Library Browser is opened, or when an existing customization is changed before the Library Browser is opened, the customizations do not take effect.
Workaround: In the MATLAB Command Window, enter sl_refresh_customizations after opening the Library Browser.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model with Iterator subsystem with Assignment block might produce incorrect simulation and code generation results
Description: Simulating your model in accelerator or rapid accelerator mode or generating code from your model might produce incorrect results when the model meets all of the following conditions: The model contains a reusable subsystem whose output is an input to a For Iterator or While Iterator subsystem. The For or While Iterator subsystem contains an Assignment block. The immediate data source of the input initialization signal (Y0) ?port?of the Assignment block is located outside the For or While Iterator subsystem but inside the reusable subsystem. The aforementioned immediate data source is either (1) a Mux block or (2) a Bus to Vector block whose input is a virtual bus signal.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Copy block with the?Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization?parameter selected before the input initialization signal (Y0)?port?of the Assignment block.

Summary: Saturate on Integer Overflow coverage is missing from several Simulink Coverage qualification test suites
Description: The DO Qualification Kit does not collect the Saturate on Integer Overflow coverage metric for the following test cases: TC_MC_EML_7 -? For this test case,?coverage is not collected for the MATLAB Function in mEML_saturate.slx  TC_MC_SL_1? TC_MC_SL_6? TC_MC_SL_13 TC_MC_SL_14?  ?Coverage reports affected by this issue include: mEML_saturate_CoverageReport.html  mContinuous_CoverageReport.html  mLookupTables_CoverageReport.html  mLookupTablesFxp_CoverageReport.html  mSignalAttributes_CoverageReport.html  mSignalRouting_CoverageReport.html

Summary: Incorrect elevation for some roads imported from Zenrin Japan Map API 3.0 (Itsumo NAVI API 3.0) into RoadRunner
Description: Whenyou import the Zenrin?Japan Map API 3.0 (Itsumo NAVI API 3.0) datausing SD Map Viewer Tool , some links in SD Map  preview showsudden rise or fall in elevation. Roads built from such links lookunrealistic in scene editing canvas.

Summary: 1D - Direct Lookup Table(n-D) block that has Diagnostic for out-of-range input parameter set to 'Warning' or 'None' generates incorrect HDL code.
Description: The HDL code generated for your Simulink model might be incorrect when your model has these parameter settings for?Direct Lookup Table(n-D) ?block: Number of table dimensions  ? is set to '1'. Diagnostic for out-of-range input?  is selected as 'Warning ' or 'None '.  The incorrect HDL code may cause simulation failures in ModelSim when verifying the generated code.

Summary: HDL code generation errors out when using a masked subsystem that contains the self-modifiable masks
Description: HDL code generation may error out if the DUT has a masked subsystem that contains self-modifiable mask. The following error message is generated by indicating the top level model:? "The destination port already has a line connection."?

Summary: Loading an App Designer app with a grid layout might result in an inactive breakpoint
Description: Loading an App Designer app with a breakpoint or setting a breakpoint in a loaded appmight result in the breakpoint becoming inactive. Inactive breakpoints aredisplayed in gray.This issue might occur in apps that contain a grid layout,and occurs most often in apps with many components in the grid layout.
Workaround: Tomake an inactive breakpoint active, modify and save the app, and thenset the breakpoint again.
