Description: Model Advisor?check?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0741 (Timing to update data used in state chart transition conditions) ?does not report?warning?for states with multiple action labels for an action which updates transition condition variable in  during  action.

Summary: JMAAB check for?jc_0734?displays an incorrect warning for states with words that start with action labels
Description: Model Advisor check Number of state action types   (id:? ?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0734)? displays the following warning for states with words that start with action labels.  Displayed warning :?  The following Stateflow States have repeated action types:  Stateflow State ???? LabelString  test/Chart/A ? ? ? ? A  en:  data =[entryData:1:10]

Summary: JMAAB check for jc_0733?does not report states?having action types written out of order with spaces
Description: Model Advisor?check?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0733 (Order of state action types) ?does not report?warning?for states with? action types written out of order and with blank spaces.

Summary: Missing Rte prefixes for AUTOSAR.Parameters with SystemConstant storage class
Description: The generated code is missing Rte  prefixes when all the following conditions are met: The model is configured for AUTOSAR code generation. The model uses AUTOSAR.Parameter  with SystemConstant  storage class as a parameter in a block such as Constant block.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Extra node when comparing Simulink models containing blocks or annotations that have the same name as a system, block, annotation, or line parameter
Description: When comparing Simulink model files, an extra node may appear in the comparison report when all  these conditions are true:? You are using the comparison tools on the MATLAB desktop or publishing the comparison report as HTML, PDF, or Word files. You added or deleted a block or annotation. The name of the added or deleted block or annotation matches the name of a system, block, annotation, or line parameter.  In the comparison report, the extra node appears on the opposite side to the block or annotation that has the same name as a parameter.
Workaround: Rename the added or deleted block or annotation. Use a name that does not match the name of any Simulink system, block, annotation, or line parameters.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: Simulink Editor might crash when the quick insert menu is used in architecture diagrams
Description: Using the quick insert menu in architecture diagrams might cause the Simulink Editor to crash.
Workaround: Use the toolstrip or element palette to add new diagram elements.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Permute function is scalarized incorrectly
Description: In rare cases, incorrect code generation for the permute function may occur when all of the following conditions are met: The input matrix to the permute function has at least 3 dimensions. Exactly one dimension of the input matrix is variable-sized with an upper bound. At least one dimension?of the input matrix is fixed with the same size as the upper bound. Additional dimensions of the input matrix are fixed and are either size 1 or are the same size as the upper bound. The permute  function rearranges the fixed and variable-sized dimensions. MATLAB Coder is configured with InlineBetweenUserAndMathWorksFunctions  set to 'Always '.  Consider the following MATLAB code:     function out = foo(in,upperLimit)    assert(upperLimit>=2 && upperLimit<=3);    out = permute(in(2:1:upperLimit,:,:),[2,3,1]);    end MATLAB Coder generates:    --SNIP--     out_size[0] = 2;    out_size[1] = 1;    out_size[2] = (int)upperLimit - 1;    plast = (int)upperLimit - 1;    for (i = 0; i < plast; i++) {      out_data[2 * i] = in[3 * i + 1];      out_data[2 * i + 1] = in[3 * i + 2];    }     --SNIP-- which is incorrect when in is a 3-by-2-by-1 matrix and upperLimit is 2. This is because we assign elements 1 and 2 from in into elements 0 and 1 of out. However, the correct generated code would have assigned elements 1 and 4 from in into elements 0 and 1 of out. For example, the correct code may look like:    --SNIP--     plast = (int)upperLimit - 2;    for (i = 0; i < 2; i++) {      for (k = 0; k <= plast; k++) {        in_data[k + ((int)upperLimit - 1) * i] = in[(k + 3 * i) + 1];      }    }    out_size[0] = 2;    out_size[1] = 1;    out_size[2] = (int)upperLimit - 1;    plast = ((int)upperLimit - 1) << 1;    if (plast - 1 >= 0) {      memcpy(&out_data[0], &in_data[0], plast * sizeof(double));    }     --SNIP--  See attached example.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2021b Update 2, R2022a

Summary: The off-boresight gain of Gaussian antenna in the satellite scenario is over-predicted.
Description: The gain of Gaussian antennas held by the transmitters and receivers created either by default or using the?gaussianAntenna ?function are over-predicted in the off-boresight direction. As a result, the link between the transmitter and receiver may be incorrectly predicted to be closed when one of them holds a Gaussian antenna that is not pointing directly at the other. This bug affects  linkIntervals ,  linkPercentage ,  linkStatus , and  ebno ?functions when the Gaussian antenna is not directly pointing at the next transmitter or receiver in the communications link created using the  link ?function.  For example, in the following code snippet, the Gaussian antenna belonging to the receiver that is mounted on the ground station gimbal is pointing at the satellite. However, the Gaussian antenna belonging to the transmitter that is mounted on the satellite is not. Therefore, the gain computed for the latter is over-predicted, resulting in the?linkStatus ?function returning  true ?corresponding to the scenario  StartTime .  % Create a satellite scenario.  startTime = datetime(2021,11,8);  stopTime = datetime(2021,11,9);  sampleTime = 60;  sc = satelliteScenario(startTime, stopTime, sampleTime);  % Add an equatorial orbit satellite. Add a transmitter to the satellite. The transmitter by default holds a Gaussian antenna, and points towards nadir.  sat = satellite(sc, 10000000, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0);  % Add a ground station. Add a gimbal to the ground station. Add a receiver to the gimbal. The receiver by default also holds a Gaussian antenna. Point the gimbal at the satellite.  gs = groundStation(sc,0,-30);  rx = receiver(gim);?  tx = transmitter(sat);  gim = gimbal(gs);  pointAt(gim,sat);  % Create a link between the transmitter and the receiver.  lnk = link(tx,rx);  % Determine the link closure status at the scenario start time.?  s = linkStatus(lnk,sc.StartTime);

Summary: MATLAB crashes during code generation when an Outport block is accessed by a State Writer block and connects to a Model block
Description: MATLAB crashes during code generation when all the following conditions are true: An Outport block in a conditionally executed subsystem directly connects to a Model block. The same Outport block is accessed by a State Reader or State Writer block.

Summary: Simulation results mismatch between normal and software-in-the-loop (SIL) modes for model with Data Store Read and Write blocks
Description: Simulation results for a model in normal mode vs. software-in-the-loop (SIL) mode might not match when all of the following conditions are present: The model has a Data Store Read and a Data Store Write block. The Data Store Read and Data Store Write blocks are connected to each other through one or more blocks that can be collapsed or removed during block reduction. The model configuration parameter?Block reduction ?is selected.  Inconsistency between the two modes?in the relative execution orders of the Data Store Read and Data Store Write blocks might cause a mismatch in simulation results.
Workaround: Use either of these workarounds:Clear the model configuration parameter?Block reduction.Control the relative execution orders of the Data Store Read and Data Store Write blocks explicitly, for example by setting block priorities, to force their execution orders to be the same for both simulation modes.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Fixed-point operations that have types with non-zero bias and slopes smaller than 2^-900 or larger than 2^900 may produce different answers between simulation and code generation
Description: Fixed-point operations involving types with non-zero bias and extremely small or large slopes (less than 2^-900 or greater than 2^900) may produce incorrect simulation and generated code. Only fixed-point types with these extreme slopes are susceptible to this bug.?

Summary: eigs function may be slower
Description: Calling the?eigs  function may be slower compared to other releases. This regression typically affects cases where many eigenvalues are requested, or where the matrix being passed in is very large.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model containing a Merge block that feeds into a Unit Delay block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of these conditions:The model contains a Merge block and a Unit Delay block. The output of the Merge block feeds into the Unit Delay block. The?Initial condition ?parameter of the Unit Delay block and the Initial output  parameter of the Merge block have different values.?

Summary: Logging Stateflow data of type int64 or uint64 results in assertion
Description: Simulink throws an assertion error when you update or run a model with Stateflow data of int64  or uint64  type configured for logging (Log signal data  checkbox is selected).

Summary: Configuration Parameters will not allow changing solver to OdeN when doing solver change with fast restart
Description: Configuration Parameters  will not allow changing the solver to odeN for models with these conditions. Model has a mass matrix. Model is initialized in fast restart using an implicit solver. OdeN integration method is explicit.??  This warning will appear in the MATLAB command window. For fast restart initialized using an implicit solver and the model having a mass matrix,  changing solverto odeN with an explicit Integration method 'ode3' is not supported.  The new solver odeN will not be used.  Set the Integration method for odeN to an implicit solver.  Valid methods are 'ode14x' and'ode1be'.
Workaround: You can change the solver by first setting the integration method for odeN to an implicit solver such as 'ode14x' or 'ode1be'.set_param(model,'ODENIntegrationMethod','ode14x');You can then set the solver to odeN from the Configuration Parameters.

Summary: AD936x, FMCOMMS5, and E3xx receiver and transmitter blocks not available in the Simulink library browser
Description: These blocks from the Communications Toolbox Support Package for?Xilinx Zynq-Based Radio and?Communications Toolbox Support Package for?USRP Embedded Series Radio are not available in the Simulink library browser or when typing to search on the canvas.AD936x Receiver AD936x Transmitter? FMCOMMS5 Receiver? FMCOMMS5 Transmitter? E3xx Receiver E3xx Transmitter?
Workaround: To access the AD936x Receiver, AD936x Transmitter, FMCOMMS5 Receiver, and FMCOMMS5 Transmitter blocks, open the Simulink library manually by opening the model sdrzynq_lib.slx with the following command:open_system('sdrzynq_lib')To access the E3xx Receiver and E3xx Transmitter blocks, open the Simulink library manually by opening the model e3xx_lib.slx with the following command:open_system('e3xx_lib')

Summary: Linearization of model involving Simscape network might return all-zero for the Simscape part
Description: Linearizing models involving Simscape networks might return all-zero for the Simscape part. The issue manifests when the mass matrix is nonconstant and some elements of the matrix are zero at the relevant operating point or snapshot.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: Some heat exchanger blocks might exhibit incorrect specific heats under certain conditions
Description: The Simple Heat Exchanger Interface (G) block might incorrectly calculate specific heat when you set the Gas Specification ?parameter to Real . The block calculates a specific heat at constant pressure (cp) value of 1 kJ/kg/K, which might limit the value of the specific dissipation to an incorrect theoretical maximum.  This bug also affects the Simple variant of these blocks: Heat Exchanger (G) Heat Exchanger (G-G) Heat Exchanger (G-TL)?
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: mdlTerminate?and mdlCleanupRuntimeResources?not called during model simulation for startup variant inactive block?
Description: Simulink model and block methods are executed in sequence?SetupRuntimeResources (mdlSetupRuntimeResources ), Start(mdlStart), Terminate (mdlTerminate ) and CleanupRuntimeResources (mdlCleanupRuntimeResources ).  But when a block is inactive by a Variant block whose?Variant Activation time ?is set to?startup , only SetupRuntimeResources?and Start methods are executed, while terminate and CleanupRuntimeResources methods are not executed.

Summary: Non virtual buses using update diagram mapping options might return incorrect error message
Description: Non virtual buses using the update diagram mapping options might return an incorrect error message as a result of the mapping operation.

Summary: A2L and ARXML generation might not be supported for models with lookup tables and breakpoint parameters which are fed through input ports
Description: A2L and ARXML generation might not be supported when the following conditions are true: The model contains a Prelookup block. The Prelookup block has a breakpoint with Specification  set to Explicit values ?and Source ?set to  Input port .
Workaround: For the Prelookup block, change the Source parameter to Dialog and specify the breakpoint data values in the provided field.

Summary: Import of File Log data from a target computer can report errors
Description: If a folder on the MATLAB path has the same name as the real-time application on the target computer from which File Log data is being imported, MATLAB reports an error. The error reports that MATLAB cannot change the current folder to a temporary folder on the development computer.
Workaround: Remove the folder with the same name as the real-time application.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 2, R2022a

Summary: The JMAAB check for?mathworks.jmaab.ar_0002?reports auto generated folder names which violate the guideline
Description: The Model Advisor check?Usable characters for folder names?(mathworks.jmaab.ar_0002)? reports auto generated folder names which violate the guideline. Example: The folders generated post code generation which are not compatible with guideline are also flagged.

Summary: Simulation Data Inspector session file with sparklines plot does not open
Description: When you open a Simulation Data Inspector session in R2021b, the file contents do not load if the MLDATX file was created in R2021a and the subplot layout includes a sparklines plot. No error occurs. The data in the session is not loaded into the Simulation Data Inspector, and visualization settings for the session are not applied.

Summary: Increase memory usage on development computer during File Log import
Description: During File Log import of large datasets into the Simulink Data Inspector, memory usage on the development computer may increase considerably. This usage increase may cause long wait times for the import process to complete or may cause MATLAB to close. This issue appears more often on development computers that have less memory installed.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: JMAAB check for?jc_0628?displays an incorrect warning for?Saturation and Saturation Dynamic blocks
Description: Model Advisor check Check usage of the Saturation blocks ?(id: mathworks.jmaab.jc_0628 ) displays incorrect warning for Saturation and Saturation Dynamic blocks with same compiled input and output data types but has?type casting operations in their limits setting.

Summary: JMAAB check for jc_0623?does not report delay blocks used in?continuous type model or subsystem
Description: Model Advisor?check?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0623 (Usage of continuous-time Delay blocks and discrete-time Delay blocks) ?does not report?warning?for? delay ?blocks used in?continuous type model or subsystem as per the guideline.

Summary: Error when a function generated by matlabFunction is called with nonscalar input
Description: This error can happen only if both of these conditions are true: the input to matlabFunction  is not a scalar symbolic expression. the input to the function that is generated by matlabFunction  is also nonscalar.  Then MATLAB might throw the following error. "Error using reshape Number of elements must not change." For example, executing this code results in the error. A=sum(power(sym('a'),1:10));B=sum(power(sym('b'),1:10));C=sum(power(sym('c'),1:10));X=[A;B;C;2*A;2*B;2*C;3*A;3*B];f=matlabFunction(X,'File','Xfun',Optimize = false);f([1,2],[2,3],[3,4]) Meanwhile, f(1,2,3)  works as expected.?
Workaround: In R2021b you can work around this issue by using the name-value argument Legacy=true.For example, when you run the code example in the Description section, replace the call to matlabFunction with this line of code.f=matlabFunction(X,'File','Xfun',Optimize = false, Legacy = true);

Summary: Rebuilding a simulation target in an SLXC file from a previous release might fail
Description: Using SLXC files generated before R2020a_update8, R2020b_update7, and R2021a_update6 to rebuild simulation targets in a later release produces an error.     Unable to update models referenced by '***'.        Caused by:        Error using *** (line **)        Cannot find field dynamicEnumTypeChecksums in ***
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2022a

Summary: Test file containing two or more test cases using different harnesses might crash MATLAB when run in Test Manager
Description: In the Test Manager, if you run a test file that contains two or more test cases and each test case uses a different test harness, then MATLAB might crash.

Summary: Inactive state writer block writing state to an outport block with initial condition may error when it receives propagated variant condition
Description: When?you perform an update diagram on a model, Simulink may throw an error stating that Outport block has inherited initial output value, though initial output value is specified.This error can occur when all below conditions are met: Outport block, with some initial value specified for initial output,  and connected from a block which has variant condition State Writer  block writes to the Outport block with the initial condition and propagated variant condition Outport block is inactive by variant block's whose 'Variant control mode'  is in 'expression' ?mode and 'Variant Activation time'  is not set to?'update diagram'   Sample error message shown below: Block 'model/Function-Call Subsystem/Out1' has attributes or configurations that are not supported by State Writer block 'model/Initialize Function/Subsystem/type_a/State Writer'.  Caused by: Outport block 'model/Function-Call Subsystem/Out1' is inheriting its initial output value from its sources(s). To correct this problem, ensure that the 'Source of initial output value' parameter is set to 'Dialog' and that the 'Initial output' parameter is set to a value different from the empty matrix([]).?

Summary: Collecting code coverage for a model might cause a compiler error
Description: When you collect code coverage for a model, you might get a compiler error similar to the following:  foo.c45:80: error: macro "__MW_INSTRUM_NODE_8" passed 2 arguments, but takes just 1   The error occurs on an instrumented expression when?both  these conditions are true: the expression contains one or more logical operator (&&  or || ) one of the logical operator's operands is an indexing expression involving a function call  For example, the following expression triggers the build error: tab[fun(x)] || (x > 0)

Summary: JMAAB check for jc_0626?does not report Lookup Table blocks not having recommended Interpolation method settings
Description: Model Advisor?check Check usage of Lookup Table blocks  (id:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0626)? does not report warning for Lookup Table  blocks with Interpolation Method setting set to values other than recommended in the guideline.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Large input image sizes causes incorrect output on Deep Learning network FPGA prediction on Xilinx ZC706 board
Description: When you have a convolution layer with large input image size, and very small input feature number, deep learning FPGA prediction may generates incorrect output on Xilinx ZC706 board.?  For example: Input Image size: 500x500 Input Feature number: 1

Summary: 'Show Antenna' check box appearing along with a drop down that contains 'Show Antenna' when plotting the radiation using pattern function.
Description: A checkbox with text 'Show Antenna' is appearing above the dropdown with the same option when plotting radiation pattern.? For example,? Consider plotting the radiation pattern of a dipole antenna.? d = dipole;? pattern(d,70e6)?? The user will see a 'Show Antenna' check box above the drop down that contains the 'Show Antenna' option in the radiation pattern plot of the dipole antenna.?

Summary: Report Explorer Lookup Table component does not report table data and breakpoints specified by Simulink.LookupTable object
Description: To specify table data and breakpoints for a Simulink Lookup Table block, if you use a?Simulink.LookupTable ?object as the?Data specification  , the Report Explorer Lookup Table component does not report the data and breakpoints defined in that?object . Instead, it reports the data and breakpoints as if?Data specification ?were set to?Table and breakpoints . This causes incorrect data to be reported or the following error to occur:? Index exceeds the number of array elements. Index must not exceed 1 .??
Workaround: Set?Data specification?to Table and breakpoints and define the table data and breakpoints using the block dialog. Alternatively, if you are using release R2018a or later, use the Report API?slreportgen.report.LookupTable class to report information about Lookup Table blocks, instead of using the Report Explorer.?
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: Aggregation of coverage data for subsystem into top-model might fail
Description: When you use Simulink Test  to aggregate subsystem-level coverage into the top-model, and one or more of the subsystems receives MCDC for cascaded logic, you get an exception similar to the following:Index in position n  exceeds array bounds (must not exceed m ).  This exception causes the coverage aggregation to fail and you do not receive the cumulative coverage results.

Summary: Generating code when using For Each subsystem with partitioned mask parameter of enumerated data type might crash MATLAB
Description: MATLAB might crash during code generation for a Simulink model when all of these conditions are true: The model contains a For Each subsystem configured to have a partitioned mask parameter. The partitioned mask parameter is of an enumerated data type. The For Each subsystem contains an S-Function block, a MATLAB Function block, or a Stateflow chart. One or more block parameters of the S-Function block, MATLAB Function block, or Stateflow chart reference the partitioned mask parameter.

Summary: On Windows 11, Polyspace Code Prover might error out during the Software Safety Analysis Level 1?part of the analysis and provide less precise results
Description: If you run a Polyspace Code Prover analysis on a Windows 11 host, you might encounter an error during the Software Safety Analysis Level 1  part of the analysis. The error message in the log file is?exception SML_Pre_Basis.Fail("failure in job")(Fail: failure in job) raised . The error does not mean that the entire analysis failed. You can still see the Polyspace Code Prover results, but the results are from the previous level, Software Safety Analysis Level 0 . Because higher levels indicate greater precision, the results of level 0 might contain a greater number of orange checks (unproven results).

Summary: Code generation for Image Processing Toolbox functions imerode and imdilate might cause build warnings
Description: C++ code generated for certain Image Processing Toolbox functions might fail to build and produce linker warnings if both  this conditions are true:? You use?the functions? imerode ?and? imdilate ?in?multiple places in your MATLAB code  You use the? Microsoft Visual Studio compiler on Windows platform  If code generation is successful, the generated code builds with the following warning. Also, the generated MEX might cause a MATLAB crash. morphop2_halide_imerode_float32_host.obj : warning LNK4006: _ZN6Halide7Runtime8Internal30custom_can_use_target_featuresE already defined in morphop2_halide_imdilate_float32_host.obj; second definition ignored  findCorners_mex.mexw64 : warning LNK4088: image being generated due to /FORCE option; image may not run  This issue affects MATLAB Coder and MATLAB Function blocks.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink Coder might generate incorrect code for models with reusable subsystems that have function-call output signals
Description: Simulink Coder might generate incorrect code for models under the following circumstances: The model contains identical atomic subsystems with function-call output signals. The subsystems have auto  or reusable  function packaging. The function-call output signals connect to identical function-call subsystems. The subsystems initiating the function call have data connections to the function-call subsystems. The order of the data connection does not match between the various identical function-call initiator subsystems and function-call subsystem instances.  Simulink Coder might generate one instance for the function-call initiator subsystem, where multiple instances are required.

Summary: SwSystemConstantValueSets contains NaN values in generated ARXML
Description: When code is generated from a model that is mapped to an AUTOSAR component containing variant behavior, the values of the SystemConstants ?in the ARXML appear as NaN. <SW-SYSTEMCONSTANT-VALUE-SET>                       <SHORT-NAME>Default</SHORT-NAME>                        <SW-SYSTEMCONSTANT-VALUES>                            <SW-SYSTEMCONST-VALUE>                                 <SW-SYSTEMCONST-REF DEST="SW-SYSTEMCONST">/SwSystemconsts/MySysConst</SW-SYSTEMCONST-REF>                                  <VALUE>NaN</VALUE>                              </SW-SYSTEMCONST-VALUE>                         </SW-SYSTEMCONSTANT-VALUES>                    </SW-SYSTEMCONSTANT-VALUE-SET>?

Summary: HDL code generation fails for Real Reciprocal HDL Optimized block
Description: HDL code generation fails if a model contains a Real Reciprocal HDL Optimized block with this error message: ˇ°The sample rate 4 of port 'Cout' in network ˇ®Real    Reciprocal HDL Optimized/Shift and cast to output type/Cast to Union of Types'    is different from the expected sample rate inf. Consider inserting a Rate    Transition block before the port.ˇ±
Workaround: Modify the sample time of the Constant block in Shift and cast to output type block from 'inf' to -1.1. Break the library link of Real Reciprocal HDL Optimized block:    a. Right click on the block. Navigate to Library Link then select Disable Link.    b. Right click on the block again. Navigate to Library Link then select Break Link. 2. Open the block by right clicking on the block, navigate to Mask and select Look Under Mask.3. Break the library link of Shift and cast to output type block and open it.4. Double click on the Constant 0 block and change the sample time from 'inf'?to?-1.A fixed block with the library link removed is attached.When updating to a newer version of MATLAB, the block with library link removed needs to be replaced with new block with library link to incorporate the updates.More information regarding Real Reciprocal HDL Optimized block and sample time setting can be found at the following reference pages.https://www.mathworks.com/help/fixedpoint/ref/realreciprocalhdloptimized.html    https://www.mathworks.com/help/hdlcoder/ug/check-for-infinite-sample-time-sources.html

Summary: MATLAB crashes when updating the model a second time during accelerator mode simulation
Description: Compiling a model twice during simulation in accelerator mode causes MATLAB to crash when the model contains a user-written S-function and one of the parameters of the S-Function block references a workspace variable. For example, during an accelerator mode simulation of the model with the simulation time set to inf , clicking Update Model  on the Modeling  tab twice causes MATLAB to crash.

Summary: Missing Simulation 3D Vehicle with Ground Following block Location and Orientation output ports
Description: If the Simulation 3D Vehicle with Ground Following block parameters are not defined, these block output ports are missing:Location  Orientation?
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: JMAAB check for?db_0129?does not report supertransitions crossing states
Description: The?Sub-ID c? of Model Advisor?check?mathworks.jmaab.db_0129 (db_0146_c:?Transition lines shall not cross over states) ?does not report?warnings for? supertransitions crossing states.

Summary: JMAAB check for db_0129 does not report transitions crossing graphical functions or subcharts
Description: The?Sub-ID c? of Model Advisor?check?mathworks.jmaab.db_0129 (db_0146_c:?Transition lines shall not cross over states)  does not report?warning?for transitions crossing graphical functions or subcharts.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when undoing the deletion of a Simulink subsystem component
Description: Simulink subsystem components are used to implement subsystem behaviors in System Composer. You can delete these components on the composition canvas by selecting the component and deleting the component using the keyboard. MATLAB might crash if, after deleting the component this action is undone by using the keyboard shortcut CTRL Z  or selecting the Undo  button from the toolstrip.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Data Store Read block that reads an array of buses that contains a string gives incorrect simulation results
Description: A Data Store Read block gives incorrect simulation results when all of these conditions apply: The Data Store Read block reads the entire data store from a Data Store Memory block. The Data Store Memory block specifies a Simulink.Bus  object data type. The specified Bus  object contains an element with a string  data type. The Data Store Memory block specifies a dimension greater than 1.  The Data Store Read block correctly reads only the first element of the array of buses. The other elements of the array of buses are set to zero.
Workaround: Read individual elements of the array of buses from the data store.

Summary: Unexpected behavior using niftiread?when unzipping files of the same name in a parfor?loop
Description: The function niftread  unzips archived NIfTI files (i.e. files with .nii.gz  extension) tothe location returned by tempdir  in MATLAB before loading data from that filename. Reading multiple zip files with the same name using niftiread  in a parfor  loop can result in an error or loading the incorrect data. This happens because niftiread  unzips all of the files to the same location. Files with the same name are overwritten and only data from the last unzipped file with the same name is read.? For example:? parfor i = 1:n  ? ?data{i} =niftiread('./i/filename.nii.gz');  end ?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Yolov2Resnet18 predict incorrect results when ConvThread Number = 9,?25,?36,?49
Description: Networks output layer that is connected to the convolution layer will produce incorrect inference results when using ConvThreadNumber = 9,25,36,49.
Workaround: There isn't a easy workaround for this issue. Please use other ConvThreadNum. (e.g: 4,16)

Summary: Lookup Table Editor returns Java error message when opened for empty model with no lookup tables
Description: Opening the Lookup Table Editor following these steps returns a Java error message on the MATLAB command line: Open a model that contains no lookup table blocks. In Simulink Editor, click Modeling  tab. In the Modeling , click Lookup Table Editor .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Stateflow charts that use C as the action language do not truncate stringtype(maximum_length) type bus element during simulation
Description: If you use a stringtype(maximum_length)  type for a bus element in a Stateflow chart that uses C as the action language, the string is not truncated during simulation if the string is longer than specified maximum_length .

Summary: Error during IP core generation in SoC Builder when FPGA model has reference models
Description: When you run SoC Builder for a model that contains one or more reference models in the FPGA model, you get this error during the IP core generation step. Could not apply model's setting to the Target Interface Table inTask 1.2 of the HDL Workflow Advisor for the following reason: Cannot connectto model 'soc_hwsw_fpga0'; please try Update Diagram (Ctrl-D). Default or lastvalid setting is applied instead. If you are using the HDL Workflow CommandLine Interface, consider making these changes in the HDL Workflow Advisor andusing 'Export to Script...' to automatically generate the appropriate 'hdlset_param' commands.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: When using any deep learning Simulink block, changes to the specified network MAT-file might not be reflected in the simulation or code generation results
Description: Consider a Simulink model containing a deep learning block that loads a trained network from a MAT-file. If the network in the MAT-file is updated but the rest of the model remains unchanged, Simulink might not rebuild the model when performing simulation or code generation. As a result, the deep learning block might produce incorrect output.

Summary: fwrite silently ignores errors due to disk running out of space
Description: Writing to a file using fwrite ?silently ignores errors associated with the target disk running out of space. As a result, the operation appears to be successful. For example, in this operation, fwrite ?writes to a file on a target disk that is already at full capacity. Yet the operation generates no error and appears to have been successful. >> fid = fopen("hello.txt",'w')fid =?????5>> fwrite(fid,"text")ans =?????4>> fclose(fid)ans =?????0

Summary: Unrecognized field name warning when running a custom training experiment in Experiment Manager
Description: When you use the Experiment Manager app to run a custom training experiment, the MATLAB Command Window might show a warning of the form: Error occurred while executing the listener callback for event MetricsUpdate defined for class experiments.Monitor:  Unrecognized field name "METRIC_NAME" .   ?After this warning, the results table in the app might display incorrect values for the metric.??

Summary: False discovery rate method applies incorrect denoising in wdenoise2 function
Description: Selecting the false discovery rate (FDR) method for the DenoisingMethod  value?in the? wdenoise2 ?function results in an output image with similar noise levels to the input image. FDR denoising is not correctly applied to the  image.? For example:? load flower  wdenoise2(flower.Noisy,2,'DenoisingMethod',{'FDR',0.01});  The denoised image and the original image have the same levels of noise.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: allcycles on a graph or digraph with many nodes is slow
Description: Using allcycles  on a graph  or digraph  with more than 10,000 nodes is slow. The computation may be slow even for graphs that have few or no cycles.
Workaround: Use isdag to determine whether a digraph has no cycles at all. This is faster than the allcycles call.You may be able to usebins = conncomp(g, "OutputForm", "cell");bins(cellfun(@isscalar, bins)) = [];which returns a cell array of all connected components that contain more than one node. The concept of connected components is related to, but not equivalent to, that of cycles.

Description: On a platform with OpenCV4, the following error occurs when generating code for a Simulink model containing a Read Image block:? /home/catkin_ws/src/rosnodeTest/slros_read_image.h: In function 'void decompressImage(const uint8_t*, int, T*, int*, int*)':/home/catkin_ws/src/rosnodeTest/slros_read_image.h:32:40: error: 'CV_BGR2RGB' was not declared in this scope         cv::cvtColor(cvImage, cvImage, CV_BGR2RGB);                                        ^~~~~~~~~~/home/catkin_ws/src/rosnodeTest/slros_read_image.h:32:40: note: suggested alternative: 'CV_RGB'         cv::cvtColor(cvImage, cvImage, CV_BGR2RGB);                                        ^~~~~~~~~~                                        CV_RGB/home/catkin_ws/src/rosnodeTest/slros_read_image.h:36:40: error: 'CV_BGRA2RGBA' was not declared in this scope         cv::cvtColor(cvImage, cvImage, CV_BGRA2RGBA);                                        ^~~~~~~~~~~~
Workaround: As a workaround, install and use OpenCV3.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier incorrectly reuses model representation when?CovLogicBlockShortCircuit or CovMcdcMode parameter value is changed
Description: Simulink Design Verifier incorrectly reuses model representation in following scenario: ModelCoverageObjectives  option for Simulink Design Verifier analysis is set to MCDC  or EnhancedMCDC  and RebuildModelRepresentation  option for Simulink Design Verifier analysis is set to IfChangeIsDetected  and You toggle value of either CovLogicBlockShortCircuit  or CovMcdcMode  and rerun Test Generation analysis

Description: When selecting and deleting multiple points that contain sparse selections, the Signal Editor user interface might display a persistent spinner.

Summary: Incorrect normal-mode simulation results for a referenced model with a parameterized for-each subsystem
Description: Simulating a model hierarchy in normal mode might produce incorrect simulation results when all these conditions apply:? The model hierarchy contains multiple instances of a referenced model that simulate in normal mode. That referenced model contains a parameterized for-each subsystem. That subsystem contains a Sine Wave block.

Summary: Wrong error when there is a type mismatch in block inputs and outputs that have inherited bus types
Description: Stateflow throws the wrong error when a type mismatch is detected when inferring bus types for inputs or outputs of MATLAB Function or Stateflow blocks. The error being thrown is of the form: Error using block2chart>is_an_sflink (line 49)block_diagram does not have a parameter named 'ReferenceBlock' Error in block2chart (line 8) if(is_an_sflink(blockH))Error in sfprivate (line 13)? ? ? ?[varargout{:}] = feval(fcnName,varargin{:});Error in Stateflow.Translate/Simulink_Bus_BusTypePropSrcDstMismatchBusSrc (line 14)? ? chartId = sfprivate( 'block2chart', chartPath );Error in Stateflow.Translate.translate (line 100)? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? sfException = obj.(methodName);   ?

Summary: Invoking Serial Link Designer, Parallel Link Designer, or Signal Integrity Viewer cannot be launched if NVDA is installed.
Description: The Signal Integrity Toolbox? apps (Serial Link Designer , Parallel Link Designer , and?Signal Integrity Viewer ) do not start successfully if the Windows NonVisual Desktop Access (NVDA) is installed in your system. This is true whether you invoke the app from the App Gallery or by typing at the MATLAB command prompt. The MATLAB window may flicker, but otherwise there is no observed behavior. To confirm that installed NVDA is the cause of the error: Attempt to invoke the app by typing: "pr = " followed by the app name (parallelLinkDesigner, serialLinkDesigner or signalIntegrityViewer). As before, the app should not open.  Type si.utilities.stdOutErrFromProcess(pr). The error message should contain the text:  java.lang.UnsatisfiedLinkError: no JavaAccessBridge-64 in java.library.path     ?Pausing NVDA does not avoid the problem.?
Workaround: Before invoking any of the Signal Integrity Toolbox??apps, rename the file .accessibility.properties file in your home directory to some other name. After finishing with the app, rename the file back to .accessibility.properties.

Summary: Selector block configured with the large size matrix as input, generates out of memory?error during HDL code generation
Description: HDL Coder generates an 'Out of memory' ?error when the the input for the selector block is a matrix of large size. In this scenario, Code generation stalls and ends up generating an?'Out of memory '?error.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generation of a column-major model with a MATLAB Function block using a row-major layout might result in silent wrong answer
Description: The generated code for a Simulink model might produce a silent wrong answer when all of these conditions are true:The model has the configuration parameter Array layout   set to Column-major . The model contains a MATLAB Function block that uses a row-major array layout. The MATLAB Function block performs array operations using vectors. For example, matrix indexing using vectors, reshaping a matrix to a vector, or transposing a vector.
Workaround: Use the same array layout for both the model and the MATLAB Function Block.

Summary: Pulse Current Source and Pulse Voltage Source blocks might produce incorrect outputs
Description: When the pulse turns high, if the solver takes atime step greater than the value of the?Pulsewidth, PW ?parameter, thePulse Current Source and Pulse Voltage Source blocks might produce anincorrect?periodic square pulse.

Summary: min?or?max with ComparisonMethod="abs" return incorrect output for array containing realmin
Description: Calling min  or max  to compute the minimum or maximum of an array may return an incorrect result when all of the following criteria are satisfied:The call uses one input array rather than comparing two input arrays  The call uses the comparison method "abs"  The input array is a real single or real double a rray The input array contains the exact value realmax , or realmax('single')  if the input array is single

Summary: Requirement links to test assessments are not recognized by the metrics of the Model Testing Dashboard
Description: When you link a requirement to a test assessment in Simulink Test Manager, the metrics in the?Model?Testing?Dashboard show the requirement as Unlinked . If you collect metrics programmatically using a metric.Engine  object, instances of metric.Result  show the requirement as not linked to test cases in the project.
Workaround: Instead of linking requirements to individual test assessments, link     requirements to?higher-level test cases in Simulink Test     Manager.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: HDL Code generation hangs and runs out of memory for the large Simulink model that uses critical path estimation
Description: HDL Code generation hangs and generates an Out of memory  error for the large Simulink design that has Critical Path Estimation ?setting turned on.
Workaround: Consider disabling?Critical Path Estimation in Model Configurations Properties dialog box, or by using the makehdl(gcb, 'CriticalPathEstimation','off') command.?

Summary: Wavelet Signal Denoiser freezes when selecting or duplicating a signal denoised using 'Universal Threshold' method
Description: Wavelet Signal Denoiser freezes when selecting or duplicating a signal denoised?using the 'Universal Threshold' method. The spinning progress indicator appears and never disappears. The following steps trigger the bug. Load a signal into your workspace: load wecg  Start Wavelet Signal Denoiser. Import the signal into the app. The signal is denoised using default parameters.  Change the denoising method to  Universal Threshold  and denoise.  Duplicate the denoised signal. The Spinning Progress Indicator appears . The app is now blocked.

Summary: Unable to load LibraryLinkageCache.mat when starting Simulink or running parallel simulations
Description: Running parallel simulations by using parsim  might corrupt the file LibraryLinkageCache.mat . If the file is corrupted, the next time you start Simulink or run parallel pool simulations, Simulink reports an error similar to:?Unable to read MAT-file <user_home>/ . matlab/ <release>/ SLLibraryLinkageData/ LibraryLinkageCache. mat. ?Not a binary MAT-file.  Try load -ASCII to read as text.

Summary: Subsystem reference update fails if block pre-copy callback errors during synchronization
Description: When you edit a subsystem reference, the update process that copies the contents to the subsystem file fails if a pre-copy callback of a block errors.? When this occurs, the subsystem file is corrupted.

Summary: Mask parameter specified as a Simulink.Parameter whose value is an expression is treated as a Simulink.Parameter object in mask initialization code
Description: When you set a mask parameter to a Simulink.Parameter  whose value is an expression, the mask initialization code treats the mask parameter as a Simulink.Parameter  with value set to the resolved expression instead of as a MATLAB variable.? For example, if the following lines were evaluated in base workspace,? param = Simulink.Parameter;param.Value = slexpr("A+B");?A = Simulink.Parameter(3.2);?B = Simulink.Parameter(5.8);?  and mask parameter maskPrm  on a masked block is set to a value 'param', then inside the mask initialization code, maskPrm  is treated as Simulink.Parameter  with a value of 9.0 (this is resolved value of 'param'). So, its value can only be adjusted like so: maskPrm.Value = 15.0;?  The expected behavior is that maskPrm  be treated as MATLAB variable, so that its value could be adjusted as follows:? maskPrm = 15.0;??

Summary: Save As might check out a Simulink Test license for models containing test harnesses
Description: Using Save As  to save a model that contains one or more test harnesses might check out a Simulink Test license.
Workaround: Make a copy of the model, open the model, and then save it. Do not use Save As because it might check out a license.

Summary: Mixed-Signal Analyzer app does not import signals with alias name designated in Cadence??ADE.
Description: If the Cadence? ADE output is setup where the signals have alias names, then the waveforms associated with those signals do not get imported in the Mixed-Signal Analyzer  app. The list of signals with alias names are displayed in the MATLAB? command window?with following error message :  There were issues accessing waveforms for following nodes: <list of nodes>

Summary: XCP readSingleValue function is unable to receive messages of more than 7 bytes
Description: The XCP function readSingleValue  does not support receiving messages of more than 7 bytes. When you received a message exceeding 7 bytes, MATLAB errors with the following message: Index exceeds the number of array elements (7) Error in xcp.Channel/readSingleValue (line 992) value = xcp.Utility.unpackMeasurement(m, response(firstBye:lastByte)

Summary: Advisor.Application class?run?method marks a compile check as passed although model compilation fails
Description: Using the run  method of the??Advisor.Application  class on? Model Advisor compile-time checks?wrongly? marks the check status as passed  on a model?that has a compilation error.

Summary: Dependency Analyzer throws an error when exporting a project archive
Description: In the Dependency Analyzer, the "Package As Archive" export option throws an error when both  of these conditions are true:? You opened the Dependency Analyzer from a MATLAB project. The project has files that depend on files stored outside the project root.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: JMAAB check for db_0141?doesn't have sub-id selection
Description: The guideline for the Model Advisor?check?db_0141?:?Signal flow in Simulink models ?has sub-ids but the Model Advisor check doesn't have the input parameters for sub-id selection.

Summary: Antenna radiation patterns in the Satellite Scenario Viewer have incorrect rotation
Description: Radiation patterns displayed in the Satellite Scenario Viewer using the pattern ?function are incorrectly flipped 180 degrees about the Z axis (the Z axis points toward nadir by default). As a result, if the antenna's pattern is asymmetric about the Z axis, the boresight will appear to point in the wrong direction. This issue is purely visual and it does not affect other pattern calculations.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Elementwise writes to matrix fields of global struct and indirect writes to the global struct via function calls may generate wrong answers
Description: The code generator contains an optimization that tries to detect complete assignment of structs where the struct contains matrix fields that are written in an elementwise fashion. Consider the example below: function y = foo()    global globalStruct;    globalStruct.a_int32 = int32(10);    for i=1:500        globalStruct.a_single(i) = single(1);    end    y = bar(2);    globalStruct.a_int32 = int32(30);endfunction y =bar(in_1)    coder.inline('never');    global globalStruct;    global somethingElse    y = somethingElse + in_1 + double(globalStruct.a_int32);? end In function 'foo ' above, globalStruct  contains a matrix field 'a_single ' which is assigned by means of a for-loop. The optimization tries to detect writes to all fields of this struct. In the use case above, it deduces the following lines as a complete assignment of globalStruct :     for i=1:500        globalStruct.a_single(i) = single(1);    end    y = bar(2);?     globalStruct.a_int32 = int32(30);  However, the code generator fails to notice the read of globalStruct  via 'bar ', which leads other optimizations to incorrectly eliminate the required write: ? ? ??globalStruct.a_int32 = int32(10) ; causing wrong answers.

Description: You can?test an AUTOSAR software component ?by using a harness model that references the AUTOSAR component model. Summary: packNGo or findIncludeFiles? might fail while parsing source file
Description: Consider an RTW.BuildInfo  object that specifies generated source code which uses utility functions in the shared location. If you specify the object as an argument for the packNGo  or findIncludeFiles  f unction, you might see an error or warning. For example: ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? -----------------------------------------         RTW:buildInfo:ignoreFindIncludeParseError        -----------------------------------------        While parsing the source file '.../slprj/ert/_sharedutils/rt_sfcn_helper.cpp'        the following error occurred                .../simulink/include/simstruc_compcond.h:329: #error        directive: Must define one of RT, NRT, MATLAB_MEX_FILE, SL_INTERNAL, or        FIPXT_SHARED_MODULE        |  #error Must define one of RT, NRT, MATLAB_MEX_FILE, SL_INTERNAL, or        FIPXT_SHARED_MODULE        |   ^ This error can occur when two models use the shared location and one model defines the preprocessor symbol RT ? and the other model defines the preprocessor symbol NRT .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink Coder generates incorrect code for reusable subsystem with array of nonvirtual Bus Element inputs
Description: Simulink Coder generates incorrect code for a reusable subsystem under the following circumstances: The input port of a reusable subsystem is connected to the output of a Vector Element Selector block, where an element is selected that is not the first element in the source vector The input port of the Vector Element Selector block has a array of nonvirtual buses as its input type The input port block of the reusable subsystem is connected to a Bus Element Selector block The Bus Element Selector block is selecting a non-scale element from this bus  In the generated code, the element offset from the array bus selection is incorrectly subtracted from the Bus Element selection. This may lead to negative indexing or compliable code if the selected Bus Element is connected to a Vector Element Selection block that selects an element that is not the first element.?
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Export script erroring out in PCB Antenna Designer due to extra tags in the names of copied shapes
Description: In PCB Antenna Designer, when a shape is copied multiple times,? an extra tag "_Copy(1)" is added to the name of the copied shape. If "Rectangle1" is copied and pasted the name of the copied shape will be "Rectangle1_Copy(1)"?  Ex:  Rectangle1 --> Rectangle1_Copy(1)?    If "Rectangle1_Copy(1)" is copied and pasted the name of the copied shape will be "Rectangle1_Copy(1) _Copy(1)"  Ex:  Rectangle1_Copy(1) --> Rectangle1_Copy(1)_Copy(1).    When the design is exported as script, the names of the shape objects are used as variable names.? The exported script for the shape "Rectangle1" will be:?  Ex:  Rectangle1 = antenna.Rectangle( <arguments> );    ?Similarly the exported script for the shape "Rectangle_Copy(1)_Copy(1)" will be.?  ?Ex:  Rectangle_Copy(1)_Copy(1)?= antenna.Rectangle( <arguments> );    Rectangle_Copy(1)_Copy(1)  is not a valid variable name and a syntax error is thrown in the editor.?

Summary: Incorrect visualization for some of the pcbStack arrays.
Description: Incorrect visualization of the pcBStack array ground plane. The ground plane dimensions are smaller than expected. Use case: patchAnt= patchMicrostripEnotch; pcbObj= pcbStack(patchAnt); % Array of pcb Stack? pcbLinArr= array(pcbObj, 'linear', 'NumElements', 5, 'ElementSpacing', 0.1); figure; %Ground plane dimensions will be incorrect when visualized. show(pcbLinArr);?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Embedded Coder might generate incorrect code with Optimize global data access set to Minimize global data access
Description: Embedded Coder might generate incorrect code for a model under the following circumstances: The Optimize global data access  option is set to Minimize global data access  The model contains nested reusable subsystems (more than 1 layer deep)  The optimization might replace global variables with local variables that do not contain the same value as the replaced global variable.
Workaround: Set the Optimize global data access model parameters option to none.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: FPGA prediction could stall due to MATLAB version mismatch
Description: The FPGA prediction could stall when:?  1. You upgrade to a newer version of MATLAB and do not upgrade the Deep Learning HDL Toolbox hardware support packages. For example when you upgrade MATLAB to R2021a Update 4 or newer, and didn't upgrade the corresponding Deep Learning HDL Toolbox support packages to latest version (version?21.1.3 or newer), the FPGA bitstreams shipped with the older version of the support packages are not compatible with newer MATLAB version.? 2. You upgrade the Deep Learning HDL Toolbox hardware support packages to the latest version, and did not upgrade MATLAB version R2021a to the latest version. The FPGA bitstreams shipped in the newer version of the hardware support packages are not compatible with the older MATLAB version.?  3. You build and generate an FPGA bitstream using an older version of MATLAB, and use a newer version of MATLAB to deploy the generated bitstream?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: When targeting cuDNN with INT8 precision, the code generated for deep learning network having grouped convolution layers might produce incorrect results
Description: Generating cuDNN INT8 code for a deep learning network with grouped convolution layer might produce incorrect result if?all  these conditions are true  Number of filter groups is equal to 2?? Total number of filters from all the groups is not a multiple of 4 Number of filter channels is not a multiple of 4

Summary: Running Back Annotation may fail in Pre_route mode
Description: Runningthe Back Annotation  in?Pre_route ?mode might fail under the following conditions:  Mappingof node points to the input ports fails when it tries to map more than oneport that have similar names ending with different suffix like "port_mult_1_1" and"port_mult_1_2".  Whenblocks or ports are optimized or renamed and hence cannot be mapped.   When this happens, you can see one of the following errors while running back annotation:  Error:Failed Undefined function 'SimulinkHandle' for input arguments of type'hdlcoder.signal'  Error:Failed Unable to match the starting point because it has been elaborated inAltera or it is inside a Stateflow or a MATLAB function block

Summary: Software interface model fails compilation for AXI4 Master and AXI4-Stream Video interfaces
Description: When using the AXI4 Master or AXI4-Stream Video interfaces in the IP Core generation workflow and generating a software interface model, the generated software interface model might fail compilation with the following error message:? ----------------------------------Coder:builtins:ClassMismatchAssign----------------------------------This assignment writes a 'embedded.fi {ufix1}' value into a 'logical' type. Code generation doesnot support changing types through assignment. Check preceding assignments or input type specifications for type mismatches.?
Workaround: In the generated software interface model, you can modify the data type(s) of the bus elements by looking under the mask of the generated subsystem. Under the mask, there are bus creator subsystems that recreate the control signals for each interface. These bus creator subsystems contain a constant block for each bus element. Each constant block with datatype "ufix1" should be changed to datatype "boolean" for the model to compile successfully.

Summary: MATLAB on macOS 11.4 and later crashes while running?blelist
Description: MATLAB installed on macOS 11.4 and later versions can crash when using the following steps to scan for nearby BLE peripheral devices: After installation, double-click the MATLAB icon to launch MATLAB. At the MATLAB command prompt, run blelist .  MATLAB displays an error message about inadequate Bluetooth permission and then crashes.
Workaround: To work around this issue, follow one of these methods:Launch MATLAB only from the Terminal app. While running blelist for the first time, a prompt appears to allow Bluetooth permission for the Terminal app; click Allow.Change the privacy preferences to allow MATLAB to use Bluetooth on your macOS. Go to System Preferences > Security & Privacy > Privacy, and then click the Bluetooth tab. Add MATLAB to the list of apps requesting use of Bluetooth.

Summary: Changing the rounding mode might produce incorrect results for some mathematical expressions
Description: The expression -(a*b)  might disregard a change in the rounding mode when it is evaluated in a loop that runs thousands of times. For example, when you run this function, MATLAB displays failure  in the Command Window. function testround  % a and b are floating-point numbers with several significand digits  a = 1 - 2^(-53);  b = 0.8712198487674925;  for i = 1:1e4  ? ? feature("setround",inf)? ? ?% Change MATLAB rounding mode  ? ? c1 = -(a*b);??  ? ? feature("setround",-inf)  ? ? c2 = -(a*b);  ? ? if c1 == c2? ? ?% c1 and c2 must not be equal  ? ? ? ? disp("failure")  ? ? ? ? return  ? ? end  end  disp("success")  end
Workaround: To take the change of rounding mode into account, evaluate the expression?-(a*b) in two steps.?function testrounda = 1 - 2^(-53);b = 0.8712198487674925;for i = 1:1e4? ? feature("setround",inf)? ? tmp1 = a*b;? ? c1 = -tmp1;? ? feature("setround",-inf)? ? tmp2 = a*b;? ? c2 = -tmp2;? ? if c1 == c2? ? ? ? disp("failure")? ? ? ? return? ? endenddisp("success")end

Summary: Rebuilding test harness slow when system being tested outputs bus objects and harness source is Chart or Test Sequence
Description: When a system under test has bus objects at its output interface, rebuilding its test harness whose source is a Chart or Test Sequence might be slower than test harness creation. If the number of bus objects at the output increases, the time taken to rebuild the harness also increases.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Stateflow charts that use C as the action language do not truncate stringtype(maximum_length) type values in generated C++ code
Description: If you use stringtype(maximum_length)  type in a Stateflow chart that uses C as the action language, and choose C++ for the language of your generated code, the generated C++ code doesn't truncate strings that are?longer than specified maximum_length .?

Summary: MATLAB crashes during simulations with 'SaveFinalState/SaveState'? is off, 'SaveFormat'?is 'Dataset' , and 'BlockReduction'? is on
Description: MATLAB may crash when the following conditions are met:? 1) The model contains a Bus Selector block which selects only a subset of input signals. 2) 'SaveFormat ' is 'Dataset ' format.? 3) Both 'SaveState ' and 'SaveFinalState ' configurations are turned off.? 4) 'BlockReduction ' is turned on. ?

Summary: Dependency Analyzer does not list Polyspace as a required product
Description: When using Polyspace functions in your code, the Dependency Analyzer does not list Polyspace as a required product?in the?Products  section of the?Properties ?pane. The Dependency Analyzer also reports Outside project root ?problems in the?Problems? section of the?Properties? pane.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 5, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: Rebuilding real-time application and then starting previously loaded version of application may halt MATLAB
Description: MATLAB may halt when this series of steps occur: Build a model, and load the generated real-time application. Re-build the model and run the previously generated version of the real-time application.  These steps may cause this error message: UUID is already set. It can not be overwritten .
Workaround: Press Ctrl+C,?then use the reset(tg) command.Load and run the newly generated version of the real-time application.

Summary: Summary of requirements in exported ReqIF file contains escaped HTML tags in plain text after import from PREEvision
Description:
Many third-party requirements management applications can export and import requirements using the ReqIF file format.?If you manage your requirements in a third-party tool, you can import the requirements to Simulink Requirements, edit or link the requirements, and export the requirements back to your third-party tool with ReqIF files. This procedure is referred to as a ReqIF round trip.When you perform a ReqIF round trip with PREEvision, the requirements in the ReqIF file exported by Simulink Requirements might contain escaped HTML tags in plain text. For example, a requirement in the ReqIF file might have a summary that says

Requirement 1

. When this requirement is exported back to ReqIF, the HTML tags are?escaped.?

Summary: Incorrect Unsatisfiable objectives for Saturation blocks with a vector of Inf bounds
Description: Simulink Design Verifier might report incorrect?Unsatisfiable (or Dead Logic) objectives ?for a Saturation  block if one of the following conditions is met:The Upper limit   block parameter has a vector of at least 2?Inf   values specified The?Lower limit  ?block parameter has a vector of at least 2 -Inf  ?values specified
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2021b Update 2, R2022a

Summary: Simulation 3D Camera, Simulation 3D Camera Get, and Simulation 3D Scene Configuration block error " (mask) does not have a parameter named 'ActorName'"?
Description: Reverting a deletion of the following blocks results in error " (mask) does not have a parameter named 'ActorName'". Simulation 3D Camera Simulation 3D Camera Get Simulation 3D Scene Configuration

Summary: Running a timer whose ExecutionMode property has value 'singleShot' does not update the TasksExecuted property
Description: Running a timer whose ExecutionMode  property is set to 'singleShot'  (which is the default value for that property) fails to update the timer's TasksExecuted  property. After running the example code below, the expected behavior is that tasksExecuted will be 1. Instead it is 0. t = timer('TimerFcn', @(src, evt) disp('1'), 'StartDelay', 0.001, 'ExecutionMode', 'singleShot');   start(t);  pause(1);  stop(t);  tasksExecuted = get(t, 'TasksExecuted')

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Tunable parameters in FMU Import block are discarded when you generate standalone FMU
Description: Tunable parameters in FMU Import block are discarded when you generate nested standalone FMU, but definitions are preserved in model description file. Tuned parameters in top-level FMU do not result in the updated values propagating to child FMU.

Summary: The Scalarize Ports option in MATLAB HDL workflow advisor does not have impact on generated HDL code
Description: When generating HDL code from MATLAB code using the HDL workflow advisor, with the?Scalarize Ports  option turned on, the generated HDL code may still contain vector types.
Workaround: Use the MATLAB commands shown below to avoid this issue and correctly generate scalar types in the generated HDL code.hdlc = coder.config('hdl');hdlc.ScalarizePorts = 'on';?codegen -config hdlc -args {A, B} designFcn
