Summary: Clean Outlier Data can produce histogram plot with incorrect axes labels
Description: In the Clean Outlier Data Live Editor task, when a table is selected as the input and a histogram is selected as the plot type, the table variable name appears on the plot as the y-axis label when it should appear as the x-axis label.
Workaround: In the live script, add code directly following the Live Editor task (in the same section) to remove the y-axis label and create the correct x-axis label:ylabel("")xlabel("Var1")

Summary: When generating GRT code, a data store with an alias type might produce an incorrect error
Description: Generating code for a model hierarchy might produce an incorrect error about inconsistent data types when all of the following conditions are true:? Code generation uses a GRT-based system target file. Multiple models in the model hierarchy use the same global data store. The global data store uses an alias type.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when simulating models that contain a Simulation 3D Actor Transform Get block and Simulation 3D vehicle block
Description: MATLAB might crash when you simulate a model containing a Simulation 3D Actor Transform Get block and any of these blocks: Simulation 3D Vehicle Simulation 3D Vehicle with Ground Following Simulation 3D Tractor Simulation 3D Trailer Vehicle Terrain Sensor

Summary: When debugging a live script in MATLAB Online, the Live Editor sometimes does not display embedded output
Description: In MATLAB Online, after stopping at a breakpoint in a live script, clicking the Run ?button to continue running causes the Live Editor to display output in the Command Window instead of in the live script.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Deep Learning processor stalls when in Continuous Streaming Mode
Description: The deep learning processor might stall when running in continuous streaming mode. In this mode the deep learning processor runs continuously consuming input data and providing output data. In certain situations the OutputValid handshaking signal does not go high and you will not receive any output data leading the deep learning processor to stall.

Summary: Simulink Test Code Importer does not generate stub for undefined functions called with function pointer
Description: When using the Code Importer, if your code uses a function pointer and the function registered to it is outside the file, a stub is not automatically created. ?For example, for this C code, a stub is not generated in the sandbox auto_stub.c  file: Source.c  #include "Header.h"  FP fp = externalFunc; // pointer to undefined functionvoid function1(void){??fp(); //?externalFunc?function call}?  Header.h  typedef void (*FP)(void);extern FP fp;extern void externalFunc(void); ?

Summary: Data might appear incorrect in the Simulation Data Inspector when you log a signal using frame-based input processing
Description: When you log a signal using signal logging, the Record block, or the File Log block and configure the signal to use frame-based input processing, the data might appear incorrect in the Simulation Data Inspector.?

Summary: Function Caller block does not call scoped Simulink functions in SIL/PIL simulations
Description: Consider a parent model that contains two referenced models.  Model_A  is a referenced model that follows export-function rules and contains a Simulink Function block with Function visibility  set to scoped .??Model_B ?is a referenced model with a Function Caller block that is configured to call the scoped function defined in Model_A . If the Simulation mode ?parameter for the Model block that references Model_B ?is set to Software-in-the-loop (SIL) ?or Processor-in-the-loop (PIL) ,? when the parent model is run, the Function Caller block in Model_B ?does not call the scoped function defined in Model_A . Output signals from the Function Caller block? in?Model_B ?have a value of zero.
Workaround: If you want the?Model_B?Function Caller block to call the?Simulink Function, set?Function visibility?for the?Model_A?Simulink Function block to?global and change the Function Caller block to call the function using its unqualified function name.

Summary: Default optimization setting results in high execution time for Sliding Mode Observer block
Description: Creating a new model containing the Sliding Mode Observer block in Motor Control Blockset results in a high execution time when deployed on hardware targets. Because the default value of optimization setting named Default parameter behavior  is set to Tunable  for new models to be deployed on targets, the block generates code for all expressions.
Workaround: In the Optimization pane of Configuration Parameters dialog box, set the Default parameter behavior value to Inlined.Alternatively, run this command to change the setting:set_param(<model_name>,'DefaultParameterBehavior','Inlined')

Summary: Sliding Mode Observer block displays an error if the block parameters use fixed-point or single datatype
Description: The Sliding Mode Observer block displays an error if one or more block parameter values are type casted to fixed-point or single datatype.

Summary: JMAAB check for jc_0009 does not flag input signals across subsystem hierarchy
Description: Model Advisor?check?Check for propagated signal labels(id :?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0009)  does  not flag input signal across subsystem hierarchy.

Summary: AUTOSAR-C++14 M5-0-7 false positive when the output of a member function call is cast to float explicitly
Description: Polyspace incorrectly views member function calls as cvalue  expressions. This can result in a false positive violation of AUTOSAR-C++14 M5-0-7.
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder, Polyspace Bug Finder Server

Summary: customAntennaGeometry and customArrayGeometry support feed edge orientation only along Y-axis.
Description: Custom geometry models support feed edge orientation only along the Y-axis. Mesh generation is updated to support? feed edge along X-axis or an arbitrary direction. ant = customAntennaGeometry;? ant.Operation = 'P1+P2'; % FeedWidth ant.FeedWidth = 0.5e-3; % Define a rectangle rect1 = antenna.Rectangle('Length', 37e-3, 'Width', 37e-3); p1 = getShapeVertices(rect1); % Feed shape feed1 = antenna.Rectangle('Length', 0.5e-3, 'Width', 0.5e-3,? ? ? ? ? ? ... ? ? 'Center', [-17.25e-3 0]); p2 = getShapeVertices(feed1); ant.Boundary = {p1,p2}; ant.FeedLocation = [-17.5e-3,0,0]; figure;? % Feeding edge after meshing will be along Y-axis mesh(ant,'MaxEdgeLength',0.004); % Feeding edge is still along X-axis even after the direction is? modified. p2=[p2(:,2),p2(:,1),p2(:,3)]; ant.Boundary = {p1,p2}; ant.FeedLocation = [0,-17.5e-3,0]; figure;? mesh(ant,'MaxEdgeLength',0.004);

Summary: Simulation 3D Lidar block returns point cloud data from areas that do not have objects
Description: The Simulation 3D Lidar block returns point cloud data for samples in areas that do not have objects.?The block incorrectly processes points that are on different objects, returning point cloud data from the space between the objects.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model?consisting of cascading Hit Crossing and Unit Delay blocks
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of these conditions:The model contains a Unit Delay block and a Hit Crossing block. The output of the Hit Crossing block feeds into the Unit Delay block. The model configuration parameter Reuse local block outputs  is selected.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model with muxed root-level function-call signals connected to a Function-Call Subsystem might generate incorrect code
Description: Embedded Coder might generate incorrect code for model with the following condition: The model is using more that one root-level Inport that outputs a function-call signal. The function-call signals are muxed and connected to the trigger port of a reusable Function-Call Subsystem. The reusable Function-Call Subsystem might have a Bus Selector block of matrix elements connected to its Outport block. The subsystem output is connected to a root-level Outport block.  Embedded Coder might not generate the selection operation from the output function of the reusable Function-Call Subsystem.?

Summary: DWARF parser fails to extract debugging information from ELF files compiled using Microchip's XC16 compiler
Description: Microchip's XC16 16-bit compiler generates binary ELF files that have a special structure in the .debug  sections. When a standard DWARF parser tries to extract debugging information from an ELF file, the structure causes the parser to fail. The ELF file appears as if it has been compiled without debugging information, which blocks XCP external mode workflows on Microchip 16-bit dsPIC target hardware. The workflows require the debugging information to infer memory addresses of global variables.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: The generated model may fail to compile for Bus Creator or Bus Selector blocks containing named signals
Description: During HDL Code Generation, the generated model may fail to compile due to the following conditions:? A subsystem has buses, and the Bus Creator blocks have named signals. The named signals are connected to blocks like Data-type conversion that uses the same data type for inputs and outputs.  The model fails to compile with the following error message: ?Selected signal 'signalOut3' in the Bus Selector block 'gm_bussel_rename/Bus Selector ' cannot be found in the input bus signal.?

Summary: Polyspace in-product documentation not displayed on Linux
Description: If you use the Polyspace desktop interface on Linux, the in-product documentation might not open when you try one of these: Going to Help  -> Documentation.  Clicking a link on the Start Page. Clicking a link to open the context help for an option or a result.
Workaround: To work around the issue:Open the file polyspaceroot/polyspace/bin/polyspace?in a text editorComment out lines 35 and 36 in the file by adding a '#' character at the start of each line, like so:# LD_LIBRARY_PATH="$MATLAB_ROOT/sys/os/$ARCH:$MATLAB_ROOT/bin/$ARCH:$LD_LIBRARY_PATH"# export LD_LIBRARY_PATHHere, polyspaceroot is your Polyspace installation folder, for instance /usr/local/Polyspace/R2021b.

Summary: Saving and re-simulating a model with Array Plot block results in a segmentation violation.
Description: A segmentation violation happens if you simulate a model containing an Array Plot block after saving it with a new name.

Summary: Generated VHDL can show out of bounds errors can occur during simulation at time step 0
Description: When simulating generated VHDL code in ModelSim or QuestaSim, you might see out of bounds error for generated index expressions of the form: out <=? mySignal[idx - 1];  At time 0, the indexing expression idx-1  may evaluate to?-1,  causing this error. An example error could be of the form, "# ** Fatal:? (vsim-3734) Index value -1 is out of range 0 to 100." ?The generated VHDL correctly captures the original MATLAB design, the error occurs because of propagation time needed for the index values during simulation. This problem does not occur for generated Verilog??code.?

Summary: Double-Acting Servo Valve Actuator (IL) block incorrectly offsets spool position by half of the spool stroke
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 5, R2022a

Summary: SIGSEGV (Invalid memory access) when running code generated targets from models with scalable compilation
Description: When a Simscape model has the?Reuse components during compilation ?configuration option selected, running code generated targets from such model might result in a SIGSEGV (Invalid memory access).

Summary: MATLAB may crash during code generation for a design file that creates copies of a network with states and calls predictAndUpdateState or classifyAndUpdateState
MATLAB might crash for models with Code Mappings
Description: MATLAB might crash while opening,?building?or?closing?a model that has Code Mappings configured for signals, Data Store Memory blocks, or blocks with states. The generated stack trace contains lines similar to:[ 14] 0x00007ffcfdb71eab C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2021b\bin\win64\codemapping_c.dll+01187499 coderdictionary::ModelMappingBase::~ModelMappingBase+00000251  [ 15] 0x00007ffcfdaea0d3 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2021b\bin\win64\codemapping_c.dll+00630995 coderdictionary::CoderDictionaryMapping::~CoderDictionaryMapping+00014483  [ 16] 0x00007ffd2de2f2ad C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2021b\bin\win64\codermapping_core.dll+00389805 sl::MappingManager::removeMappingsOfType+00000285  [ 17] 0x00007ffd2de29cac C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2021b\bin\win64\codermapping_core.dll+00367788 sl::MappingManager::destroyMappings+00000060  [ 18] 0x00007ffd2de262f8 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2021b\bin\win64\codermapping_core.dll+00353016 sl::MappingManager::~MappingManager+00000024

Summary: HDL code generation may fail when a referenced model has bus ports with multiple connections
Description: HDL code generation may fail when a referenced model has bus ports with multiple connections. The following error message is generated: Dot indexing is not supported for variables of this type.Error in slhdlcoder. SimulinkFrontEnd/ isArrayInsideBusError in slhdlcoder. SimulinkFrontEnd/ checkReferencedModelPorts>isaVectorPort

Summary: Asynchronous requests with large payloads fail when MATLAB Production Server deployment is behind a load balancer
Description: Asynchronous requests that clients send to MATLAB Production Server fail if all of the following conditions are true: The MATLAB Production Server deployment is behind any load balancer or web proxy server, such as Azure Application Gateway or AWS Application Load Balancer. The request payload is large, for example, larger than 1 MB.  The following workflow results in the error: Client sends an asynchronous request with large payload data, for example, over 1 MB:? POST <mps_endpoint>/<archive>/<function>?mode=async      Client retrieves the result of the asynchronous request: GET <mps_endpoint>/{request-uri-string}/result    The GET  request results in the following error: {    "error": {        "type": "httperror",        "code": 400,        "messageId": "InvalidJson",        "message": "Invalid JSON"    }}

Summary: Code replacement for MATLAB Fast Fourier transform functions might cause build error or produce incorrect code replacement report
Description: This issue occurs if both ?these conditions are true:? Your MATLAB code or Simulink model contains MATLAB Fast Fourier transform functions?(fft , ifft , fft2 , ifft2 , fftn , ifftn ). When generating code, you either select multiple code replacement libraries (CRLs) or set the CodeReplacementLibrary  configuration parameter to 'ANSI_C' .   In such situations, the generated code replacement report might contain incorrect results. You might also get errors when building the generated code.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code for computing minimum or maximum of two values might be nonequivalent to MATLAB code when one input is NaN
Description: MATLAB Coder might generate incorrect code when the following conditions are true: The MATLAB code contains logic that computes the minimum or maximum of two values of floating point types, and propagates NaN. That is, the logic is such that the result is NaN  if one of the input values is NaN . The input to that logic could be NaN .  For example, consider the following MATLAB code, function out = max_propagating_nan(x1,x2)if x1 > x2??? out = x1;else??? out = x2;endend  If you run this code in MATLAB with arguments 1  and NaN , you get NaN  as the result: >> max_propagating_nan(1, nan)ans =?? NaN  However, if you generate a MEX function and call it with the same arguments 1  and NaN , you get the non-NaN  value, 1 , as the result: >> codegen max_propagating_nan -args {double(0), double(0)}Code generation successful.>> max_propagating_nan_mex(1, NaN)ans =???? 1  The incorrect generated C code for max_propagating_nan  is:  real_T max_propagating_nan(real_T x1, real_T x2){? return muDoubleScalarMax(x1, x2);}   where muDoubleScalarMax  is a utility function that computes the maximum of two values of double precision floating point type, and returns the non-NaN  value if one of the input values is NaN . Examples of functions that do not propagate the NaN  input but might get incorrectly generated for MATLAB code that computes minimum or maximum of two values and does propagate the NaN  input include Utility functions muDoubleScalarMin,  muDoubleScalarMax, muSingleScalarMin, muSingleScalarMax, and    C and C++ standard library functions    fmin, fminf, fminl, fmax, fmaxf, fmaxl.     See also Bug Report ID 2593033 .

Summary: Generated model from the Subsystem that has Repeat block may fail to compile when Allow tasks to execute concurrently on target?is selected
Description: Generated model may fail to compile when the Subsystem contains Repeat block and the model has concurrent execution enabled by selecting the 'Allow tasks to execute concurrently on target'(Configration settings->Solver pane)? parameter in the Configuration Parameters. The following error message will be generated: The block 'gm_Testbench_fixpt/ Filter/ PCM/ Serializer_Subnetwork/ ratechange' of type 'S-Function' is a multi-rate block. ?Currently Simulink supports only single-rate blocks of this type when the model is configured for concurrent execution.
Workaround: Consider replacing the Repeat block with a Rate Transition block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch in simulation results between normal and software-in-the-loop (SIL) modes in model that reads from and writes to data store
Description: The simulation results between normal and software-in-the-loop (SIL) modes might not match when all the following conditions are true: The model contains one or more Data Store Read and Data Store Write blocks. One of the Data Store Read or Data Store Write blocks is connected to an Outport block through a variant block. The connection of the variant block to the Outport block is inactive. The variant block has its Variant activation time  set to code-compile .  Under these circumstances, a mismatch between normal and SIL modes might result from inconsistency in the relative execution order of the Data Store Read and Data Store Write blocks.
Workaround: Set the relative execution orders of Data Store Read and Data Store Write blocks explicitly, for example, by using block priority.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model using S-function block and symbolic dimensions
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of the following conditions: The S-function block has two or more inputs, with first input to S-function being non symbolic and second or subsequent inputs contain symbolic dimensions. The S-function block uses?%roll ?TLC directive.  In the generated code, the symbolic dimension variables might appear incorrectly in the?for ? loop.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a

Summary: MATLAB crashes when?a classification experiment?uses?a large datastore for training or validation.
Description: When a classification experiment uses a large datastore (for handling more than around 2GB of data) for training or validation, a memory leak can cause MATLAB to crash. The memory leak happens when a trial is nearing completion.  Datastores are designed for working with collections of data that        are too large to fit in memory at one time. The problem here is caused by the readall  function incorrectly trying to load all the data in memory at once.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 2, R2022a

Summary: Error if data input to root-level Inports is a Simulink.Signal object
Description: Simulink Real-Time errors out if the data input that you specify in the model configuration parameter?Data Import > Export > Input  is a Simulink.Signal  object.

Summary: Variation point proxies in the ARXML are incorrect when using the (default) variant keyword
HDL Workflow Advisor may display incorrect target frequency when switching workflows
Code generation might produce uncompilable C++ code that contains incorrect headers if the configuration parameter?FilePartitionMethod?is set to 'SingleFile'
Description: Certain usage patterns of data types in MATLAB code might cause the generated C++ code to contain incorrect header include patterns if the FilePartitionMethod ?code configuration parameter is set to ' SingleFile ' (or,? in the MATLAB Coder app,?the equivalent parameter?Generated file partitioning method  is set to  Generate all functions into a single file ). In such situations, the generated C++ code fails to compile.  The example code below defines a class?MyClass , an enumeration class? MyEnum , and a function  foo ?in three separate files.? The input to the function  foo  is a structure with a field populated by an instance of? MyClass .  % File: MyClass.mclassdef MyClass    properties        f;    end    methods        function obj = MyClass(in)            obj.f = in;        end    endend% File: MyEnum.mclassdef MyEnum < int32    enumeration        VAL1 (1);    endend% File: foo.mfunction out2 = foo(in)out2 = MyEnum.VAL1;end  Generate code for by running these commands:? c = coder.config('lib');c.FilePartitionMethod = 'SingleFile';c.TargetLang = 'C++';s = struct('a',MyClass(1));codegen -config c -args {s} foo  The code generator produces an incorrect set of include dependencies in the generated C++ code. This causes a system compiler error that references a missing type definition for the C++ type?MyClass .  This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Workaround: In the code generation configuration object, set the?FilePartitionMethod?parameter?to 'MapMFileToCFile'. Alternatively, in the MATLAB Coder app, set the equivalent parameter?Generated file partitioning method?to?Generate one file for each MATLAB file.

Summary: timescope axes time limits will be out of sync if zoom is performed in an area that does not have any data
Description: If the input signals provided to the timescope object have different sample rates and plotted on multiple displays, zooming Into an area that does not have any data, might cause the axes time limits to out of sync.

Summary: Dot indexing error while running Hardware Setup Wizard for HDL Coder Support Package for Xilinx RFSoC Devices
Description: After the installation of HDL Coder Support Package for Xilinx RFSoC devices, the following error may occur while running the Hardware Setup Wizard:? "Dot indexing is not supported for variables of this type. "?  This behavior is mainly related to the Network Interface Card (NIC) and it is observed when multiple Virtual Private Networks (VPN's) are installed in the system.?

Summary: Code generation report missing search box
Description: When you generate a code generation report using HDL Coder, the search box is missing from the report.

Summary: Compiler error in Simulink when using custom code from different directories
Description: Simulink might report a compiler error when you simulate a model that uses multiple custom code files from different directories.?
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b, R2024a

Summary: Unconnected geometry ports on Spatial Contact Force blocks can cause MATLAB to crash
Description: When a geometry port on a Spatial Contact Force block is unconnected, MATLAB can crash during compilation at the beginning of simulation. The crash only occurs when one of the geometry ports is unconnected, not both.

Summary: FMU simulation in Simulink errors out due to small step size and long simulation time
Description: When an FMU import block runs at a small communication sample time (for example, 0.0001s) in a long simulation (for example, 1000s), Simulink throws the following error: An error occurred while running the simulation and the simulation was terminated?   Caused by:?  Error in supplied FMU: An error occurred in function 'fmi2DoStep' for block '<block name>' during simulation.?    At the same time, the FMU logger prints out an error related to communication time inconsistency, for example: Log from FMU: [category:error, status:fmi2Error] Stepsize must be non-negative and divisible by 0.0001?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect std::copy and std::memset generated in C++ code when loop induction variable is unsigned int
Description: For sequential copy or initialization of an array inside for  loop, the code generator emits incorrect std::copy  and std::memset  which leads to incorrect result if all of the following conditions are true:Model configuration parameter Language  is set to C++.  Loop induction variable is unsigned int . Loop upper bound is not known at compile time. Subscript of source/destination array contains loop induction variable minus some offset, for example, A (k1 - 5) .  In the below function ebr_2527097_memcpy , there is a copy from array A to array B: function [B] = ebr_2527097_memcpy(A, B, in)    for k2 = uint8(6):uint8(1):uint8(in)        B(k2 - 5,1) = A((k2) - (5),1);    end end Following is the expected generated std::copy : if (u >= 6) {    std::copy(&A[0], &A[u - 5] , &B[0]);} Instead, the code generator produces the following which causes overflow for the subscript 'u + 251 ':if (u >= 6) {    std::copy(&A[0], &A[u + 251] , &B[0]);} Here is an example of std::memset  which initializes array B with int8(127) :function [B] = ebr_2527097_memset(B, in)        for k2 = uint8(6):uint8(1):uint8(in)        B(k2 - 5,1) = int8(127);    endend Following is the expected generated std::memset :if (u >= 6) {    std::memset(&B[0], 127, static_cast(u - 5)  * sizeof(signed char));}  Instead, the code generator produces the following:if (u >= 6) {    std::memset(&B[0], 127, static_cast(u + 251)  * sizeof(signed char));}
Workaround: Disable std::copy in coder.CodeConfig object.cfg = coder.config; cfg.EnableMemcpy = 0; There is no workaround for std::memset as?Use memcpy for vector assignment (EnableMemcpy) only disables std::copy.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Design file that creates copies of a network with states and calls predictAndUpdateState or classifyAndUpdateState may generate incorrect code
Description: For stateful networks such as networks with LSTM, BiLSTM, or GRU layers, incorrect code is generated when the design file meets all of the following conditions: A stateful DAG or series network is copied (whether by explicit copy, passing the network to another function, or other copy techniques). Either of the networks isupdated with predictAndUpdateState  or classifyAndUpdateState .  Inference is run on the network that was not updated.?  Example 1: Explicit Copy function out= net_predict(in)    %#codegen?    persistentnet;    if isempty(net)? ?   ? ? net =coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork('net.mat');   end   netCopy =net;   netCopy = predictAndUpdateState(netCopy,in);    out = predict(net, in);?    end?    In this case, only the copied network, netCopy , is updated. However, the output produced by the generated code is as if the original network, net , was?also?updated by the?predictAndUpdateState ? call. Example 2: Copy via Subfunction ? ? ? function out = net_predict(in)   ? ? ? %#codegen?   ? ? ? persistent net;   ? ? ? if isempty(net)? ?   ? ? ? ? ? ?net = coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork('net.mat');   ? ? ? end   ? ? ? updateNetwork(net,in);   ? ? ? out = predict(net, in);?   ? ? ? ? ? ? end??  ? ? ? ? ? ? function updateNetwork(netCopy, in)?  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?netCopy = predictAndUpdateState(netCopy, in);?  ? ? ? ? ? ? end?  In this case, only the subfunction's local copied network, netCopy , is updated. However, the output produced by the generated code is as if the original network, net , was also updated by the?predictAndUpdateState ?call.??
Workaround: To maintain a copy of the network thatis not updated by?predictAndUpdateState?or?classifyAndUpdateState?calls, create a separate network object?by using?coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork. For instance, in Example 1, replace the?netCopy = net?line with:if isempty(netCopy)? ? ?? ? netCopy =coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork('net.mat');end?

Summary: Maximum number of elements decreased for deep learning operations on GPU
Description: For several deep learning functions, including layers, the maximum number of elements allowed for inputs and outputs on the GPU has decreased. Running affected functions using inputs and outputs with more than 2*10^9 elements results in the following error: Maximum variable size allowed on the device is exceeded.  This is a decrease from the maximum number of elements of? 2147483648 (2^31) in previous releases. Affected functions include, but are not limited to, the following:? convolution - convolution2dLayer , convolution3dLayer , dlconv?  batch normalization - batchNormalizationLayer , batchnorm?  activations -  reluLayer ,  relu

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for?isprop does not return true for Constant properties of a class
MATLAB issues warnings about missing fonts on startup on Mac OS 12?
Description: On macOS 12 open MATLAB and you can see the warnings. ? Warning: the font "Times" is not available,  so "Lucida Bright" has been substituted,  but may have unexpected appearance or behavor.  Re-enable the "Times" font to remove this warning. ? Warning: the font "Times" is not available,  so "Lucida Bright" has been substituted,  but may have unexpected appearance or behavor.  Re-enable the "Times" font to remove this warning.

Summary: DAGNetworks using imported flatten layers error out during compilation
Description: Imported flatten layers (such as 'nnet.keras.layer.FlattenCStyleLayer' or 'nnet.onnx.layer.FlattenLayer') are only supported within SeriesNetworks. When you use DAG Networks with these flatten layers it generates this error message "Layer nnet.keras.layer.FlattenCStyleLayer is not supported. See the documentation for list of supported layers."   This error is not very descriptive and does not tell the user that flatten layers are not supported for DAG networks. This error occurs during compilation of a DAG Network containing a flatten layer, so either while estimating performance or compiling a Workflow object.

Summary: MATLAB might freeze sporadically when running large live scripts that produce many figure outputs
Description: When running Live Editor code that produces many figure outputs, MATLAB might freeze and execution cannot be stopped. The freeze occurs most commonly when the many figure outputs contain image plots.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: A conditionally executed subsystem containing a C Function block can give different results in simulation and generated code
Description: Simulation results might not match generated code results when all of the following conditions are true: A model contains a C Function block placed inside a conditionally executed subsystem. The C Function block initializes the value of a block Output   symbol in the Start Code  of the block. The C Function block output corresponding to the Output   symbol is connected to an Outport block. The Outport block is configured to inherit its initial output value from the input signal.  In such a case, the initial value set by the C Function block is not propagated to the downstream Outport block during simulation.
Workaround: Explicitly specify the initial value in the Outport block parameters.

Summary: Typing in the desktop Search Documentation box on the desktop can cause MATLAB to become unresponsive
Description: The typeahead feature of the MATLAB desktop Search Documentation box can use a significant amount of memory. This can cause MATLAB to run out of memory and become unresponsive, which might result in loss of work.
Workaround: When searching the documentation, use the Search Documentation box in the Help browser instead of the Search Documentation box on the desktop.

Summary: SIL might fail when model contains Direct Lookup Table (n-D) block with input table data as a Constant block input having parameter
Description: Software-in-the-loop (SIL) simulation might fail to perform verification for a model?that meets the following conditions:? The model contains a Direct Lookup Table (n-D)? block. Block parameter Make table an input  (TableIsInput ) is selected on Direct Lookup Table (n-D)? block?to provide table data as a block input. The input port, T providing table as input to Direct Lookup Table (n-D) block is connected to a Constant block that has a value as Simulink.Parameter  object.? The model configuration parameter?Default parameter behavior? (DefaultParameterBehavior ) is set to Tunable .?

Summary: Initialization of custom storage class data object by a non-owner model
Description: When you set the Owner  property of a Simulink.Signal  or Simulink.Parameter  custom storage class and select the Use owner from data object for data definition placement  configuration parameter, a model other than the one you specify in the Owner ?property might generate a?model_initialize ?method that initializes the signal.

Summary: Invalid HDL code can be generated from General CRC Syndrome Detector HDL Optimized
Copying an Inport or Outport? block in the rtwdemo_cppclass_expfcn?example model causes MATLAB to crash
Description: Copying an Inport or Outport? block in the?rtwdemo_cppclass_expfcn ?example model causes MATLAB to crash.
Workaround: Delete each Inport and Outport block from the model canvas of the?rtwdemo_cppclass_expfcn?example model. Add new Inport and Outport blocks to the model.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Side effects associated with a function handle access might be lost in the generated code if the function handle is called immediately as it is accessed.
Description: This issue occurs if all  these conditions are true: Your MATLAB code defines a function handle Accessing the function handle involves side effects The function handle is accessed and called in the same statement  In such situations, the side effects associated with the function handle call might be lost in the generated code. For example, consider the MATLAB function?main ?defined below. This function contains a call to the function?local ?which has the side effect of displaying the string "local ". The code generated for main  might not have this side effect because the function handle x(local(arg)).f ?is both accessed and called in the same statement in the body of the  main  function.  function result = main(arg)x = struct('f', {@sin @sin});result = x(local(arg)).f(arg);function result = local(arg)disp('local');result = arg;? This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Workaround: If a function handle call involves side effects, store the function handle in a variable and call it in a separate line of code.?For example, rewrite the previous code snippet as shown here:function result = main(arg)x = struct('f', {@sin @sin});f = x(local(arg)).f;  % Create a variable to store call to function handleresult = f(arg);function result = local(arg)disp('local');result = arg;

Summary: Incorrect text in Header file field might cause MATLAB to crash and model corruption
Description: Incorrect text or typos in the?Header file ?field of the Simulation Target pane of the Model Configuration Parameters might cause MATLAB to crash, and might cause your model to become corrupted. The crash log might point to "strcxxtrim. mexw64". For example, specifying #include "foo.h  without the closing quotation mark.?
Workaround: Look for and correct any errors or typos when specifying text in the Header file field, before compiling or simulating your model.

Summary: pspectrum uses very low minimum threshold when plotting
Description: The pspectrum  function generates a plot with improper z -axis scaling when called without output arguments and without specifying the MinThreshold ?argument. The function chooses an unnecessarily small value for the minimum power, and as a result the power values do not occupy the entire range of the colormap. This code illustrates the bug: load batsignalpspectrum(batsignal,1/DT,'spectrogram')  The minimum spectral power displayed in the graph is lower than ¨C140 dB, as shown by the color bar.
Workaround: Use the MinThreshold?name-value argument to produce a spectrogram with a specified minimum value that does not display unwanted background content.This example uses pspectrum to plot a spectrogram with an adequate minimum threshold:https://www.mathworks.com/help/signal/ug/practical-introduction-to-time-frequency-analysis.html#TimeFrequencyAnalysisExample-2

Summary: Code generation might fail when SubsystemReuse parameter is set to 'Atomic and Virtual'
Unexpected error for Stateflow charts with variant transitions that call Simulink Functions with an enabled variant condition
Description: An unexpected error is produced when a Stateflow chart calls a Simulink Function and both of the following conditions are true: The Simulink Function has 'Enable Variant Condition' checkbox checked, and Variant Control is set to an expression that is not always true The Stateflow chart calls the Simulink Function from a state or transition which is only active in variant configurations where the Simulink Function is active as well (i.e, its variant control evaluates to true in all those configurations).  The error is similar to:  Propagated condition on block 'sfVariantLampExample/ Lamp' will lead in a build error during code generation if 'sfVariantLampExample/ Lamp' is calling an inactive Simulink Function block 'sfVariantLampExample/ SimulinkFunction'.  Variant condition on blocks:  ? 'sfVariantLampExample/ Lamp': unconditional  ? 'sfVariantLampExample/ SimulinkFunction': HAS_DIMMER1 == 1
Workaround: Create an atomic subchart that is guarded with the same (or stricter) variant condition and move the Simulink Function call inside the atomic subchart. For example, if the function was called from a state A, convert A to an atomic subchart and use atomic subchart mappings to pass all the necessary data to it.?

Summary: Resource utilization report does not report the data size of RAM accurately?for complex inputs
Description: Blocks with complex inputs that use RAM (e.g. a HDL FIFO block) show incorrect values for data size of RAMs in the Resource utilization report. The data size is half of what it should be.

Summary: In Design Error Detection workflow, upcasting the datatype for Abs block shows an overflow objective being falsified, but the simulation of the counterexample does not result in an overflow
Description: In the Design Error Detection workflow, when a numeric datatype (example, int8) is upcasted to a higher data type (example, int16) in an Abs  block, Simulink Design Verifier analyses the overflow objective to be falsified. However, simulating the counterexample does not result in an overflow.

Summary: Real-time application crashes when stopped if UDP Send block and UDP Recieve block use the same IP address or port combination
Description: If the UDP Send block and the UDP Recieve block are configured to use the same IP address or port combination and send/receive data simultaneosly, the real-time application crashes when stopped.

Summary: Incorrect HDL code might be generated for a model that contains Blackbox subsystem followed by Complex to Real-Imag block
HDL code generation may fail for a referenced model when the port name ends with a space
HDL code generation may fails when your Simulink model contains floating-point bus elements other than native floating point (NFP).?
Description: For a Simulink model that has non-NFP double-precision or single-precision bus elements, HDL code generation fails and generates an assertion.??

Summary: Rerunning sections in the Live Editor which contain subplot or tiledlayout can result in overlapping text and distortion in the figure outputs
Description: When using subplots or a tiled chart layout in the Live Editor, axes titles or labels created in one section can affect figure outputs in other sections after rerunning one of the sections. The result can be overlapping text and other distortions. This result can even occur if there is only one modified code section and that section is run individually (that is, even when not using the Run button on the toolstrip).
Workaround: In cases where you intend to run all the sections, resolve any overlapping text or other distortions by using the Run button on the Live Editor toolstrip rather than executing each section separately.In cases where you are able to clear all of the figures (including those in the Live Editor), avoid the issue by running the following code in the MATLAB Command Window before running sections in the Live Editor:delete(findobjinternal(groot,'type','figure'))

Summary: Simulink customization might fail when starting Simulink
Description: During Simulink startup, you might receive this warning: Warning: The following error occurred while evaluating simulinkrc: An error occurred during Simulink customization.  If you receive this warning, Simulink UI customization might fail and the Simulink Toolstrip might sporadically appear blank.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when a root level Inport block outputs a function call with minimum or maximum values set
Description: When a root level Inport block emits a function call with minimum or maximum values set, MATLAB might crash.
Workaround: Follow these steps:Clear the Output function call signal attributeEnter empty brackets for the Minimum and Maximum valuesReselect the Output function call signal attribute

Summary: Incorrect Dead Logic or Unsatisfiable objectives for Relational Operator?blocks that have muxed vector signal inputs
Description: Relational Operator? blocks with the following configuration may incorrectly report?Dead Logic ?or?Unsatisfiable? objectives:At least one of the inputs to the Relational Operator  block has a muxed vector signal This muxed vector signal is a concatenation of more than one signal At least one of the individual concatenated signals has a dimension size that is as large as RollThreshold  Model Configuration Parameter

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Unexpected Stateflow chart behavior when a local message LIFO or priority queue is full, and the queue overflow diagnostic is not set to error
Description: A Stateflow chart incorrectly handles incoming messages for local messages when all of the following conditions are true:? Queue type  is set to LIFO  or Priority  Queue overflow diagnostic  is set to Warning  or None  The queue is full  Instead of rejecting the incoming message, the Stateflow chart accepts the incoming message and drops the oldest message from the LIFO queue and the lowest priority message from the priority queue. This bug does not affect generated code in R2018b and later, but still affects simulation results.

Summary: Simulink blocks with fixed-point input might report incorrect relational boundary coverage results
Description: If your model contains a block with fixed-point inputs, you might get incorrect coverage results. For example, consider a Switch block whose control input signal is fixdt(1,3,0) , with threshold u > 3 ,  and data input signals 1  and 4 , respectively. For this case, the expected result is trigger - threshold = -4 , but the coverage report shows that +LSB  (which is +1 ) is satisfied instead:  trigger - threshold? 33%  ? ? -LSB? 0/  2  ? ? ? 0 ? 0/  2  ? ? +LSB? 2/  2
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 2, R2022a

Summary: Using From/Goto blocks in HDL Coder may result in a crash
Description: MATLAB may crashes when using From/Goto blocks in HDL Coder with the following signature: pir::FromGotoSignalManager::propagateFromGotoGlobalSignalsUp at b:\ matlab\ src\ cgir_hdl\ dom_pir_core\ globalsignalmanager. cpp??
Workaround: Consider not using From/Goto blocks, and manually wiring components together.

Summary: Generated HDL code may not preserve user-provided index guarding logic
Description: For the MATLAB code shown below, the generated HDL may have the indexing expression,?u(i), ?not protected by the condition i > 0 . if i > 0  ? ? ? ??y = uint16(u(i));  else  ????????y = uint16(2);  end
Workaround: For a workaround, rewrite the MATLAB code:?if i > 0? ? ? ??y = uint16(u(i));else????????y = uint16(u(1));end?to:if i < 10 % u is of size 10x1? tempIdx = i;?else?? ?tempIdx = 1;?endy = uint16(u(tempIdx));?

Summary: Co-simulation with Unreal Engine fails when certain vehicle names are specified in the Simulation 3D Vehicle with Ground Following block
Description: The Simulation 3D Vehicle with Ground Following block instantiates a corresponding vehicle actor in Unreal Engine and? tags it with the name specified in the block. The Unreal Actor tag does not preserve the case of the tag string. This leads to the mismatch of the vehicle name in Simulink and the Unreal Vehicle Actor for certain?short names (e.g. c1 or c2).
Workaround: Avoid short names (e.g. c1 or c2) and use longer ones instead (e.g. SimulinkVehicle1)

Summary: Code generation may take long time with For Each subsystems connected to terminator blocks
Description: HDL code generation may take long time with For Each subsystems and the output ports of the subsystems are connected to terminator blocks.
Workaround: Move the terminators inside the For Each subsystem

Summary: uavLidarPointCloudGenerator object properties change when loaded from MAT file
Description: When you load a saved uavLidarPointCloudGenerator  object from a MAT file, the loaded object has all its properties reset to their default values.

Summary: HDL code generation may fail for Simple Dual Port RAM block when Address port width is set with variable under certain conditions
Generated code might not produce an error if a potentially undefined function handle is called
Description: Suppose that your MATLAB code satisfies all  these conditions: Your code includes a function handle The function handle is conditionally-defined? There exists an execution path in which the function handle is not defined but is called  In MATLAB, if the execution of your code invokes the undefined function handle, you get an error.? However, code generation or execution of the generated code might not produce an error. For example, consider this MATLAB code that defines a function handle f  conditionally. The handle is defined only if the input argument arg  is true . function result = main(arg)if arg    f = @local;endresult = f(arg);endfunction result = local(arg)result = ~arg;end If you run this code in MATLAB with false  as the argument, you get an error: >> main(false)Unrecognized function or variable 'f'.Error in main (line 5)result = f(arg); If you generate a MEX function for main , no error is emitted during code generation. Also, if you call the generated MEX function main_mex ?with? false ?as the argument, you do not get an error. >> codegen main -args trueCode generation successful.>> main_mex(false)ans =  logical   1 Even if the argument is false , the generated code invokes the the function named local . This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.?

Summary: MATLAB might crash for models with an AUTOSAR mapping
Description: For models with an AUTOSAR mapping, MATLAB might crash during model load, while updating the diagram, or during code generation due to corrupt code mappings.

Summary: Compilation error when simulating uavDubinsConnection object as a referenced model using MATLAB function block in Accelerator mode
Error in rapid accelerator simulations run using?SimulationInput?objects?when variable Workspace property is a string
Description: Rapid accelerator simulations run using?SimulationInput ? objects throw the following error if the SimulationInput ? object contains a variable with the Workspace ? property specified as a string instead of a character vector: Cell array input must be a cell array of character vectors.   For example, a simulation run in rapid accelerator mode using this SimulationInput object would throw the error: simIn = simIn.setVariable('A', 3, 'Workspace', "myModel");
Workaround: Define the Workspace property of the variable using a character vector instead of a stringsimIn = simIn.setVariable('A', 3, 'Workspace', 'myModel');

Summary: Error when comparing requirement set file revisions?
Description: When you compare different versions of a requirement set file with highlighting enabled, you might see an error message that says?Unable to locate Requirement Set . This occurs when a version of the requirement set that you are comparing is already opened in the Requirements Editor .
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 1, R2023a

Summary: Deleting a single connector that shares a source port with other connectors using the destroy?function removes all the connectors connected to that source port
Description: When using the destroy  function? on a?systemcomposer.arch.Connector  object to remove a connector that shares a source port with other connectors, all connectors sharing that source port are deleted. To share a source port with other connectors, use branching for System Composer connectors.

Summary: Writing a command to an instrument with VISA-USB generates a "Query UNTERMINATED" error
Description: Writing a command to a VISA-USB controlled instrument with the visadev  interface, might result in the instrument displaying a "Query UNTERMINATED"  error. For example:  vd = visadev("MY_DMM");writeline(vd,"CONF:VOLT:DC 10");s = writeread(vd,":SYSTEM:ERROR?")s =    "-420,"Query UNTERMINATED""
Workaround: You can avoid this issue with either of the following options:Use the visa interface instead of the visadev interface.If you prefer to continue using the visadev interface, contact technical support at https://www.mathworks.com/support/contact_us.html.
Products: Instrument Control Toolbox, Industrial Communication Toolbox

Summary: PLC Code generation for MultiProg and PCWorx targets may fail for some models
Architecture Views Gallery is unresponsive after opening
Description: The Architecture Views Gallery ?might be unresponsive and show a spinning progress bar whilst none of the interface can be interacted with.

Summary: Duplicate HDL files are generated for identical atomic subsystems inside two or more different hierarchical paths of conditional networks
Unexpected error when compiling a SimBiology model that has a parameter with Units set to 'kelvin'
Description: An unexpected error can occur when compiling a SimBiology model that has a parameter with?Units ?set to?'kelvin' . The error message could be any of the following:  vector  invalid vector subscript  vector::_M_range_check: __n (which is 0) >= this->size() (which is 0)  Caught "std::exception" Exception message is: invalid vector subscript   Tasks that compile a SimBiology model include: Simulating Verifying Getting model equations  The error occurs only if the model has one or more parameters with Units  set to 'kelvin' ?and the model has unit conversion enabled (that is, UnitConversion  set to true ).
Workaround: To avoid this issue, add a custom temperature unit to the SimBiology custom unit library. One way to do this is by executing the following at the MATLAB command prompt:sbioaddtolibrary(sbiounit('mytemperature', 'kelvin'))You might need to execute this once for each user on each computer where they use SimBiology. This custom unit will persist across MATLAB sessions, because it is stored in the user's preference directory.

Summary: False positive violation of UINT_CONSTANT_OVFL on unary operator ~
Description: Polyspace shows a false positive violation of the defect UINT_CONSTANT_OVFL  when the chosen compiler is GCC or Tasking. For instance,?in this code, Polyspace raises a false positive violation on ~tmp  when tmp  is a const ?object. typedef unsigned char UINT8;UINT8 SSC_NLimOfRcptProtn_Stop_Flag;void SSC_NLimOfRcptProtn_PreInit(void) {           const UINT8 tmp = (UINT8)0;           tmp = (UINT8)0;            SSC_NLimOfRcptProtn_Stop_Flag = (UINT8)(~tmp);  // False positive }

Summary: Saving model with test harnesses?and immediately closing it might crash MATLAB?
Description: If you save a model that has one or more test harnesses, and then immediately close the model, MATLAB might crash.

Summary: Demux block with partially dead output ports might produce disconnected cosimulation model and incorrect test bench
MATLAB becomes unresponsive when running tests on a model that contains a Record block
Description: When running tests programmatically or using the Test Manager, MATLAB stops responding at the end of simulation if the model contains a Record block. In the Test Manager, MATLAB becomes unresponsive when the Test Manager displays?Simulation Progress 100% .

Summary: Generated model after HDL code generation may give incorrect results when Clock-rate pipelining and Sharing optimizations turned on.
Description: Generated model after HDL code generation may give incorrect results when following conditions are met: Model having Clock-rate pipelining   optimization turned on Model having Oversampling    factor value greater than 1 Subsystem is set with SharingFactor ?  value greater than 0 in HDL Block Properties dialog.  The mismatches can be observed in the validation model  by enabling the following option: Configuration parameters => Global Settings => Model Generation => Enable the Validation model   check box  The above issue will be observed for 4-D/5-D Lookup Table block when following configuration is set: Subsystem having the 4-D/5-D Lookup Table   block Interpolation method parameter? set as  Linear point-slope   HDL Block Property  AreaOptimization   set as Serial   under Native Floating Point  tab Model having Oversampling   factor greater than 1?
Workaround: The following command can be run in command window as Workaround:? >>hdlset_param(<modelname>, 'ReduceMatchingDelays', 'off')?

Summary: Specifying an input type for a function as gpuArray?during MEX generation and then calling the generated MEX with non-gpuArray input might cause? a memory leak
Web apps running on MATLAB Web App Server do not record or play sound
Description: Web apps running on MATLAB Web App Server that access a client computer microphone or speakers do not record or play sound. Failure to record or play sound is captured in the app session logs.

Summary: MATLAB Function containing?bitorreduce,?bitandreduce?or?bitxorreduce?might produce HDL code that causes compilation error
Incorrect Code Generation: Embedded Coder might not initialize an exported or imported signal with a constant value
Description: Embedded Coder might not initialize an exported or imported variable that is mapped to a constant (infinite sample time) signal if the model is using bus element ports at the root-level.
Workaround: Avoid using bus element ports at the root-level and replace those with regular Inports, Outports, and Bus Selector blocks.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results for referenced models that simulate in accelerator mode when nontunable mask parameter values change during simulation
Description: Changing the value of a masked parameter of a referenced model during simulation does not affect the simulation results when both of these conditions apply:The referenced model simulates in accelerator mode. The masked parameters of the referenced model are marked as nontunable.

Summary: add_line might not connect a branched line that has negative coordinates.
Description: When adding a branched line with the add_line  function, specifying negative coordinates as points of the line might result in the line not connecting to another line.

Summary: Error occurs while writing a vector of data as foreground signal generation
Description: When using certain NI devices such as the USB-6356, foreground signal generation errors out while writing a vector of data.? For example, the following code generates an error when writing of a vector of ones: d = daq("ni");ch = addoutput(d, "Dev1", "ao0", "Voltage");write(d, ones(100,1))"Unexpected exception in plug-in: 'Input data can only contain ASCII characters.'"

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Selecting a "Device Vendor" other than "ASIC/FPGA" can lead to generation of mismatching HDL code for models containing Stateflow Charts.
Description: Models containing Stateflow charts with "Device Vendor" under "Hardware Implementation" section in configuration parameter selected to any value other than "ASIC/FPGA" can lead to generation of HDL code that may not match Simulink simulation.  HDL Coder matches the behavior of Stateflow charts when the "Device Vendor" is selected as "ASIC/FPGA".
Workaround: Change the "Device Vendor" to "ASIC/FPGA".

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code results might be incorrect when solver setting Automatically handle rate transition for data transfer is enabled
Description: The generated HDL code for a Simulink model might be incorrect when the following settings are applied: Configuration Parameters > Solver >? Solver details > Tasking and? sample time options > Automatically handle rate transition for data transfer  checkbox is enabled? Model has multiple sample times??  The incorrect HDL code can result in simulation failures in ModelSim when verifying the generated code.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulation in Accelerator mode using an enabled subsystem and signals of non-matching data type sizes provides a wrong answer
Description: When a signal inside an enabled subsystem uses a signal size that is inconsistent with all other signals in the model, wrong values might be caused outside of the subsystem. This problem only is present in the JIT accelerator mode. You can observe this issue by comparing simulation values in normal and JIT accelerator mode. The normal mode and the classic accelerator mode simulations provide correct values
Workaround: When encountering this issue, switch to classic accelerator mode using the following command:?>>?set_param(0, 'GlobalUseClassicAccelMode', 'on')After issuing this command, the simulation in accelerator mode will produce correct results for models that use enabled subsystem and signals of non-matching data types sizes.

Summary: Conversion to fixed point using float2fixed may fail when same struct variable is used as input and output
Description: When converting a design which uses the same struct variable as input and output to fixed point using float2fixed , conversion may fail with an error similar to the following: Undefined function or variable 'copyTo_<NameOfStruct>'.
Workaround: Rename either the input or output struct and update the rest of the function as needed to reflect the change.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Hidden block inserted to terminate signal logging might cause different results for normal mode simulation and code generation
Description: Normal mode simulation and code generation might have different results when all these conditions apply: Output signals from one or more Terminate Function blocks in a referenced model connect to a Merge block. The output signal from the Merge block connects to a block that represents an output port of the referenced model. The output signal from the Merge block is marked for logging.  The execution order of the hidden block differs for normal mode simulation and code generation, which includes code generation of the model reference simulation target that accelerator mode simulation uses. The different execution orders cause the different results.
Workaround: Use normal mode simulation.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect error message for multi-word datatype during PLC code generation
Incorrect Code Generation: Stateflow charts or MATLAB Function blocks containing persistent variables might produce incorrect HDL code because of pipeline latencies
Description: HDL code generated from Stateflow charts or MATLAB Function blocks containing persistent variables might produce different numerical answers than the original Simulink model if any  of these conditions are true: Input pipelines are requested on the block itself Upstream optimizations introduce pipeline registers to the input of these blocks

Summary: Code inspection fails if a model contains multiple instances of a subsystem from the same library with a referenced model inside it
Description: Simulink Code Inspector fails to verify the code generated from a model that meets all of these conditions: The model contains multiple instances of a subsystem from the same library. The subsystem contains a referenced model.  Simulink Code Inspector is unable to uniquely map buffers in the model for the different subsystem instances to the corresponding buffers in the generated code. Code inspection fails with a "Failed to verify" ?status.

Summary: Objects not deleted after generating PDF reports
Description: When generating PDF documents, MATLAB Report Generator fails to delete some objects that are no longer needed .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect HDL code may be generated for a trigger subsystem with non-boolean trigger value
Incorrect Code Generation: Triggered Subsystem that has no HDL optimizations configured, generates an incorrect HDL code
Description: For a triggered subsystem inyour design that has no HDL optimization configured on it, and when applied with the following settings, ˇ®Minimize Global ResetˇŻ isturned ˇ®OnˇŻ. ˇ®No-reset registers initializationˇŻis set either to ˇ®Generate initialization inside moduleˇŻ or ˇ®Generate anexternal scriptˇŻ.  HDL Coder generates an incorrect HDL code which results in a simulation mismatchwith testbench. No warning message is generated for this problem.?
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 2, R2022a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Referenced model not rebuilt?with change in active variant controls
Description: Consider the case where a top model referencing a model with inline variants. In this case, the referenced model is in accelerator mode, and the top model configuration parameter? UpdateModelReferenceTargets  is set to IfOutOfDate . The incorrect behavior is not  observed when the top model is simulated and the referenced model picks the active variant based on the variant controls specified in base workspace or data dictionary. The incorrect behavior is observed if variant control is changed to select a different active variant, and the top model is simulated, new active variant is not picked by the reference model giving a wrong answer. Please note that if UpdateModelReferenceTargets  is? set as IfOutOfDateOrStructuralChange , this issue is not seen.

Summary: gpuArray/pagefun with mldivide errors if there are more than 682 pages
Description: Using the gpuArray/pagefun  function with @mldivide  as first argument will fail if there are more than 682 pages to process. Calling pagefun  with these conditions will result in the following error message: Error using gpuArray/pagefun  Call to zgetrsBatched in CUBLAS failed with error status: CUBLAS_STATUS_INTERNAL_ERROR.
Workaround: Call pagefun multiple times, only processing 682 pages or less each time.For example, the following code will perform pagefun mldivide in batches of size 682.A = rand(250,250,60,50,'single','gpuArray');B = rand(250,1,60,50,'single','gpuArray');C =?pagefunMldivideWorkaround(A,B);?function C = pagefunMldivideWorkaround(A, B)% Preallocate CC = zeros(size(B),'like',B);% Get number of pagesnumPages = prod(size(A,3:ndims(A)));% Use a max of 682 pages for the callnumPagesToProcess = min(numPages,682);% Index in the array per batch of numPagesToProcess pagesstartIdx = 1:numPagesToProcess:numPages;endIdx = [ startIdx(2:end)-1 numPages];for i = 1:numel(startIdx)? ? % Get indices for this call? ? idx = startIdx(i):endIdx(i);? ? % Call pagefun mldivide with fewer pages? ? C(:,:,idx) = pagefun(@mldivide,A(:,:,idx),B(:,:,idx));endend

Summary: Incorrect output for export-function model in accelerator and rapid accelerator modes when logged signal or root Out Bus Element block uses union sample time
Description: When simulating an export-function model in accelerator or rapid accelerator mode, a root-level Out Bus Element block or a logged signal might output incorrect data for bus elements with union sample time in either of the following situations: The Out Bus Element block uses a union sample time, and the model contains at least one other root-level Out Bus Element block corresponding to the same bus. An element of the bus being logged using signal logging at the root-level uses a union sample time.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Output of Phased Array System Toolbox (PST) based Behavioral model does not match with HDL model after HDL Code Generation.?
Description: HDL Code generation adds unwanted balancing delays to the model. The HDL Output is delayed from the PST behavioral model by the amount of balance delays.  The delay is caused by the enabling of HDL Coder optimizations for Natural floating point blocks. This results in PST based Behavioral model output not matching the HDL model results.?
Workaround: Disable all HDL Coder optimizations in Model Settings,?in addition Model Settings > HDL Code Generation > Floating Point > set 'Latency Strategy' to 'Zero'.

Summary: JMAAB check for?jc_0736?displays an incorrect warning for conditional and loop statements in state actions
Description: Model Advisor?check?jc_0736 :?Uniform indentations in Stateflow blocks ?displays the following warning for conditional and loop statements in state actions.Warning :? The following Stateflow states do not have uniform indentation.  This warning message is incorrect as Stateflow performs auto-indentation for conditional and loop statements.

Summary: Simulation mismatches for the selector block
Description: Generated code may have simulation mismatches for the selector block when Input is 1-D vector? Number of dimensions parameter is 2? Index options table rows are as follows Index vector (dialog) Index vector (port)

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Accumulation operations modeled in Simulink using feedback loops may cause latency related HDL mismatch
Description: This potential mismatch is similar to what is described in this?external bug report . The only difference is in the cases that Clock Rate Pipelining is requested, the mismatch may not be fixed from the solution in the prior external bug report. HDL code generation for a Simulink model might result in a numeric mismatch if these conditions are met: Operations in a Simulink model involve performing arithmetic using a prior value from a feedback loop? Latency is introduced from the upstream logic during code generation due to optimizations such as pipeline insertion and resource sharing/streaming Clock Rate Pipelining is requested for the feedback loop logic  HDL Coder does not properly schedule or reset this accumulation due to the latency introduced upstream. In the generated model, the feedback loop executes on the introduced cycles of latency, which can result in a numeric mismatch seen in the validation model.?
Workaround: The mismatch can be resolved by introducing an enable logic on the Delay block. You can either add an Enable port on the Delay block, or use an Enabled Subsystem for other blocks with state that do not support Enable ports.If the?boolean?signal driving the Enable is routed to the DUT, the delay balancing optimization inserts the appropriate matching latency to align with the latency introduced by the other optimization settings. This signal can be treated as a valid input signal, and allows the appropriate timing for the accumulation operation.

Summary: Unexpected error during HDL code generation for Stateflow Blocks containing enumerated data with specified initial values
MATLAB Function block with input names same as enumeration member names has name conflict in generated VHDL
Description: When the MATLAB Function block has inputs with same names as enumeration member names used in the MATLAB Code, generated VHDL does not prefix the enumeration names to differentiate it from the input names. This causes synthesis errors. Example, MATLAB code that can produce this error is shown below. In this code the input 'in1' has same name as the enumeration field myEnum.in1. function out = fcn(in1)  if (in1)??  ??out = myEnum. in1;  else  ????out = myEnum. in2;?  end

Summary: Several Simulink Check tests cases fail to execute when Fixed-Point Designer is not available
Description: The IEC Certification Kit test procedure for Simulink Check requires Fixed-Point Designer to execute tests?SLCHK_MISRA_Q007 ?and?SLCHK_MISRA_Q008. However, when the license isn't available, the test procedure does not exclude the tests as expected. Instead, the tests fail to execute and the results for these tests state "Error occurred during model compile " in the qualification report.? Test procedures in the IEC Certification Kit are expected to exclude tests when the license that is required to execute a test is not available. When this occurs, the results for the test states?"EXCLUDED: no <XYZ> license" ?in the qualification report. ?
Workaround: Remove the rows from the trace matrix artifact (certkitiec_slchk_tests.xls) corresponding to the test cases SLCHK_MISRA_Q007, SLCHK_MISRA_Q008?and rerun the Simulink Check tests in the IEC Certification Kit.?

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier analysis may provide incorrect results when observing a constant signal
Description: When any Observer Port  block is mapped to a constant signal in the design model, Simulink Design Verifier analysis of the design model may provide incorrect results. For example, in Property Proving mode, some Valid  objectives might be reported as Falsified .

Summary: Axes in grid layout managers within apps have inconsistent appearance
Description: Creating multiple axes, and placing them into separate panels, and then into an app's grid layout manager might produce axes that differ in appearance. For example, the tick label fonts might be different, and the box outlines might differ in thickness.
Workaround: Place the axes directly into the grid layout manager without placing them into panels.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Using an assignment block with 2-D matrix and vector assignment with for-iterator indexing could result in incorrect code generation
Incorrect Code Generation: Latency upstream from a feedback loop might cause a numeric mismatch when the feedback loop traverses through the hierarchy
Description: Before R2021b  The original issue is reported in the following Bug Report:?https://www.mathworks.com/support/bugreports/2226678 The issue is described below.? HDL code generation for a Simulink model might result in a numeric mismatch if the following conditions are met: Operations in a Simulink model involve performing arithmetic using a prior value from a feedback loop. Latency is introduced from the upstream logic during code generation due to optimizations such as pipeline insertion, and resource sharing/streaming.  HDL Coder does not properly schedule or reset this accumulation due to the latency introduced upstream. In the generated model, the feedback loop executes on the introduced cycles of latency, which can result in a numeric mismatch.?? In R2021b?  Feedback loop protection is implemented in R2021b to resolve the original issue. However, HDL code generation for a Simulink model might still result in a numeric mismatch if the following condition is met: The feedback loop traverses multiple levels of hierarchy.  Feedback loop protection does not trigger correctly when the feedback loop traverses multiple levels of hierarchy, which can result in a numeric mismatch.
Workaround: Before R2021bPlease refer to the following Bug Report for workarounds:?https://www.mathworks.com/support/bugreports/2226678?In R2021b?Locate and flatten any subsystem in the feedback loop by moving the contents of the subsystem to the same level as the feedback loop.

Summary: intlinprog returns a non-optimal solution
Description: Rarely, the intlinprog  solver can return a feasible but non-optimal solution.??This might occur when intlinprog  finds an integer feasible solution with the rounding heuristic during cut generation or IP preprocessing. Because of a bug, intlinprog  might return this feasible solution as optimal, even when it is not optimal. To see if you have hit this bug, look for?intlinprog ?to show the following in the iterative display: A single pass of the rounding heuristic, followed by Cut generation showing a large relative gap (greater than options.RelativeGapTolerance ), followed by Immediate termination claiming the optimal solution is found.  In this case, the solver might have hit the bug.
Workaround: Set the Heuristics option to 'none'?using optimoptions. For example,>> options = optimoptions('intlinprog','Heuristics','none');>> [x,fval,exitflag,output] = intlinprog(f,intcon,A,b,Aeq,beq,lb,ub,x0,options);
