Summary: Test generation shows objectives status as?Undecided with Testcases for certain models with Block Reduction off?
The CPE report in HDL Codegen shows one or more blocks with timing delay, but clicking on on highlightCriticalPathEstimationOffendingBlocks.m script returns "Block not characterized".
Description: In some cases, estimating the critical path of a model can report a finite, non-zero timing delay value for a block, for example 5 ns. However, the script?highlightCriticalPathEstimationOffendingBlocks.m reports  block not characterized  for the same block. These two results contradict each other, as an HDL Coder block should either have a finite, non-zero timing delay or be marked? block not characterized.

Summary: Accessibility Issue: No way to open the context menu for a focused element using the keyboard in MATLAB Online on macOS system
Description: On Windows, the?Shift+F10 ?keyboard shortcut opens the context menu for the currently focused element. On macOS, no such keyboard shortcut exists. Because of this, when using MATLAB Online on a macOS system, there is no way to open the context menu for a focused element using the keyboard. For our best accessibility support, use?MATLAB Online . MathWorks is committed to improving the accessibility of its software products, focusing first on the accessibility of MATLAB Online. See the?Accessibility Statement for MATLAB ?site for the latest information on accessible workflow support.?

Summary: Only the first model will use fast restart when executing different models using Simulink Test Manager
Description: In Simulink Test, if multiple test cases are executed at once which have different system under tests, then only the first one is executed in fast restart mode.

Summary: Deletion/corruption of the?boost_interprocess?shared subdirectory created by C++ Boost interprocess when opening a Simulink model?
Description: If your application requires access to shared memory between different processes and creates a root shared directory called boost_interprocess  in the path C:\ProgramData\boost_interprocess , then opening a Simulink model deletes/corrupts the subdirectory inside this directory.  The deleted subdirectory may look like:? C:\ProgramData\boost_interprocess\1590098449
Workaround: Rebuild your application after defining the Boost macro BOOST_INTERPROCESS_SHARED_DIR_PATH to point to a different path.Example :?BOOST_INTERPROCESS_SHARED_DIR_PATH="C:\\ProgramData\\myapp_boost_interprocess_path" ,where the?path C:\\ProgramData\\myapp_boost_interprocess_path must exist before.

Summary: Non-ASCII character input to add-on functions in MATLAB Client for MATLAB Production Server is not supported
Description: If the input argument to a MATLAB Client for MATLAB Production Server add-on function contains non-ASCII characters, you receive the error?Can't create a std::string from this data .

Summary: Accuracy regression using inv?on?ill-conditioned matrices
Description: Using inv(A) ?to compute the matrix inverse of an? ill-conditioned matrix A ?can produce a higher order?residual compared to previous versions of MATLAB ?.?The result still lies within the expected error bounds of the calculation, but previous versions of MATLAB were able to calculate a more accurate answer.
Workaround: In most cases, calculating the inverse of a matrix explicitly is neither recommended nor required.To solve a linear system A*x=b once or twice, use the backslash operator \:x = A\b;To solve a linear system with the same matrix A multiple times for different right-hand sides b1, b2, ..., use?decomposition instead:dA = decomposition(A);x1 = dA\b1;x2 = dA\b2;...If the explicit matrix inverse is needed and inv(A) does not give sufficiently accurate results, an alternative way to calculate it is with one of these commands:invA = A\eye(size(A))invA = eye(size(A))/ANote that the usage of either?mldivide (\) or?mrdivide?(/) depends on how?invA?will be subsequently used, since the error propagation is different for each case.

Summary: comm.FMBroadcastDemodulator and the FM Broadcast Demodulator Baseband block do not successfully demodulate FM signals when the Stereo setting is true
Description: When using?the comm.FMBroadcastDemodulator ?System object or the FM Broadcast Demodulator Baseband block with?Stereo ?set to?true, the FM signal?is not successfully demodulated into the left (L) and right (R) channels. The expected output is [L R] , but the returned signal is two different columns of data, each containing a combination of L+R. Before R2017a, significant noise is also present.

Summary: When starting a MATLAB process on Windows via certain external interfaces, the MATLAB process may stall or hang.
Description: When using MATLAB C++, Java, or Python engine APIs to start a MATLAB process, the process might stall or hang. The stall or hang might appear as a timeout while trying to find graphics devices during MATLAB graphics initialization.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 2, R2022a

Summary: Accessibility Issue: Opening list of keyboard shortcuts from the toolstrip can create a keyboard trap
Description: When the documentation page containing the list of keyboard shortcuts is opened from the toolstrip by going to the Home ?tab and selecting?Help > Keyboard Shortcuts , and then the Tab ?key is used to navigate through the content of the page, a keyboard trap is created and the Help Browser cannot be closed with the keyboard. Pressing the Tab ?key to navigate through the content of the page never brings the keyboard focus to the title bar of the Help browser (with the close button) and pressing the?Esc ?key does not close the Help browser.
Workaround: To work around this issue, do either of the following:Use Tab + Shift to navigate backwards through the content of the page until the close button on the Help browser title bar is in focus.?Then press the Space or Enter?key to close the window.Use the Tab?key to navigate through the entire contents of the page until focus moves out the Help browser.?Then press the Esc key to close the Help browser.For our best accessibility support, use?MATLAB Online. MathWorks is committed to improving the accessibility of its software products, focusing first on the accessibility of MATLAB Online. See the?Accessibility Statement for MATLAB?site for the latest information on accessible workflow support.

Summary: Accessibility Issue: Table of keyboard shortcuts in the Navigation Keyboard Shortcuts window read incorrectly by screen readers in MATLAB Online
Description: When you log into MATLAB Online, the first Tab ?or Alt ?key press brings up the Navigation Keyboard Shortcuts window that includes a table with the keyboard shortcuts for navigating MATLAB Online. This table does not fully support screen readers. As a result, when the table is in focus, the screen reader announces all of the content in the table at once instead of providing information about specific cells one at a time.
Workaround: The Navigation Keyboard Shortcuts window includes a link to the documentation page. Press the Tab key to navigate to this link and then press the?Enter key to open the documentation page that contains the same table of keyboard shortcuts in a separate window. This table has the desired screen reader support.For our best accessibility support, use?MATLAB Online. MathWorks is committed to improving the accessibility of its software products, focusing first on the accessibility of MATLAB Online. See the?Accessibility Statement for MATLAB?site for the latest information on accessible workflow support.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Streaming/Sharing with Hierarchical Clock-Rate Pipelining can cause validation model mismatch
Description: The clock-rate pipelining optimization has the ability to work across hierarchical boundaries. For more information, see?Clock-Rate Pipelining and Hierarchy Flattening .? When this optimization is combined with streaming or sharing, the result of the optimizations might be incorrect due to invalid latency information.
Workaround: Hierarchical clock-rate pipelining is not applied if no hierarchy exists. For the parts of the design that are affected by this issue, apply?FlattenHierarchy?on the subsystem to avoid the mismatch. To perform this action, run this command:hdlset_param(<path_to_subsystem>, 'FlattenHierarchy', 'on')?To disable hierarchical clock-rate pipelining for the entire model, run the following command. Doing so might cause clock-rate pipelining results to be less optimal.hdlset_param(<model_name>, 'CRPWithoutFlattening', 'off')

Summary: Accessibility Issue: Keyboard shortcut for Run?button?in MATLAB Online Editor (Meta+Alt+R) causes Safari to refresh the page
Description: In MATLAB Online on a macOS system, the keyboard shortcut for the?Run ?button?in the Editor (Meta+Alt+R ) causes Safari to refresh the page.
Workaround: To run a file in MATLAB Online on a macOS system,?use the F5 keyboard shortcut instead, or manually click the Run button.For our best accessibility support, use?MATLAB Online. MathWorks is committed to improving the accessibility of its software products, focusing first on the accessibility of MATLAB Online. See the?Accessibility Statement for MATLAB?site for the latest information on accessible workflow support.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: FIR Rate Conversion HDL Optimized block may generate incorrect HDL code if it uses the request port
Description: If a Simulink? model contains an FIR Rate Conversion HDL Optimized block that uses the request port, there may be differences between the output signals of the block and the corresponding signals in the generated HDL code. This difference causes the HDL test bench and the co-simulation model to return errors and assertions that report a mismatch.
Workaround: The attached files contain a model that uses a FIFO to provide request signal behavior. The?rateConverterWithFIFO.slx?model implements the workaround, and?verifyRateConverterWithFIFO.m verifies the model by comparing it to the dsp.FIRRateConverter System object. Use these files to verify that the model works for your use case and parameter values.You can copy the?FIR Rate Converter with FIFO masked subsystem into your model. Set the interpolation factor, decimation factor, and coefficients by using the?FIR Rate Converter with FIFO?mask parameters. The subsystem automatically calculates the optimal FIFO size. To set the data types, look under the mask and configure the data type parameters of the FIR Rate Conversion HDL Optimized block.

Summary: Accessibility Issue: Outputs in the Live Editor are not accessible using only the keyboard
Description: The Live Editor does not provide a keyboard accessible way to interact with outputs. For example, there is no way to access the scrollbars or open the context menu for an output using the keyboard.
Workaround: To access output content that is inaccessible due to scrollbars, consider these workarounds:?To increase the amount of space available for outputs in the Live Editor, consider displaying output inline. To display output inline, go to the?View?tab and select?Output Inline. In this view, output spans the entire width of the Live Editor.To see a textual representation of all output, run the live script from the Command Window.?Some output context menu items are available in the Live Editor's main context menu. To open the main context menu in the Live Editor, place the cursor on the line of code that is associated with the output and press Shift+F10 to open the context menu. This keyboard shortcut is not supported on macOS systems.For our best accessibility support, use?MATLAB Online.?MathWorks is committed to improving the accessibility of its software products, focusing first on the accessibility of MATLAB Online. See the?Accessibility Statement for MATLAB?site for the latest information on accessible workflow support.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when using a DBC-file that contains inconsistencies
Description: When trying to open a DBC-file that contains inconsistencies, MATLAB might crash without generating any crash log. The impacted workflows include:? Calling the function canDatabase  with such a DBC-file. Specifying such a DBC-file to use with the following blocks: CAN Pack, CAN Unpack, CAN FD Pack, CAN FD Unpack, J1939 Network Configuration.
Workaround: If accessing a DBC-file causes MATLAB to crash, you can use Vector CANdb++ to perform a consistency check for the file in question. Fixing the reported inconsistencies should prevent the crash.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b
